DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART SECURES $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW AGREEMENT WITH FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP

Upstart Holdings, an AI lending marketplace, has announced a forward-flow commitment from funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group, under which Fortress has agreed to purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through the Upstart platform over 15 months. The announcement was made on 5th May 2026.This forward-flow agreement marks a significant expansion of the existing partnership between Upstart and Fortress, which initially began with a transaction in 2025. The collaboration illustrates Upstart’s ongoing efforts to broaden its network of capital partners, which now includes over 100 banks and credit unions leveraging its AI-driven models and cloud applications. The platform offers a wide range of financial products, including personal loans, automotive retail and refinance loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product."We’re excited to expand our partnership with Fortress with this latest agreement. Our wide array of capital partners strengthens our resilient and stable foundation to continue driving down the cost and complexity of borrowing," said Sanjay Datta, President of Capital and Enterprise at Upstart. This sentiment underscores Upstart's commitment to enhancing its service offerings while fostering relationships with key financial institutions.Commenting on the expanded agreement, Matt Biczak, Managing Director at Fortress Investment Group, stated, "This expanded agreement with Upstart underscores our focus on sourcing differentiated, high-quality consumer credit opportunities for our investors. Upstart’s data-driven platform enables efficient access to scaled origination, and we believe this partnership positions us well to continue generating durable, compelling risk-adjusted returns across market environments."The partnership between Upstart and Fortress Investment Group reflects a growing trend in the financial sector, where technology-driven solutions are increasingly being embraced to enhance the efficiency and accessibility of consumer credit. As Upstart continues to innovate and expand its offerings, the strategic alignment with Fortress positions both companies to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the evolving lending landscape.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH $180 MILLION INVESTMENT

Pure Salmon Japan secures a significant preferred note investment of over USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. This investment marks a pivotal milestone for Pure Salmon Japan, as it continues to develop its state-of-the-art land-based salmon farming facility in Japan. The transaction was completed on 1st April 2026 and is expected to support the construction of the facility, which is designed to meet the growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The partnership with Fortress Investment Group highlights the institutional backing that Pure Salmon Japan has garnered for its ambitious project. With a target production of 10,000 tonnes, this facility aims to become the largest land-based salmon farm in Japan. By utilizing advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology, the facility is set to contribute to Japan's food security by reducing reliance on imports and ensuring a stable supply of locally produced salmon.Erol Emed, CEO of Pure Salmon Japan, expressed gratitude for the investment, emphasizing the strategic importance of the project amidst Japan's focus on enhancing domestic seafood production. He stated, 'We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan.'Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding history in Japan, is not only bringing capital but also valuable expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, noted the importance of the facility in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure. This investment aligns with Fortress’s broader strategy to support sustainable initiatives that address pressing global challenges such as food security and environmental sustainability.As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan aims to engage with additional long-term equity partners to further bolster its operational capabilities. The successful closing of this financing not only showcases the increasing institutional investor interest in sustainable aquaculture but also positions Pure Salmon Japan as a key player in the evolving seafood supply chain in Japan.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. This acquisition, made on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by affiliates of Fortress, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.The Tuscan Gardens community features 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus approximately one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, catering to an affluent and growing senior population along Florida’s northeast coast. Under AgeWell’s operational leadership, occupancy and performance metrics have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, expressed enthusiasm about the acquisition, stating, "We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families."Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast features thoughtfully designed residences, diverse dining options, wellness and fitness amenities, landscaped courtyards, and personalized care programs tailored to individual resident needs. The community benefits from strong demographic trends and limited new senior housing supply locally, as well as proximity to healthcare, retail, and coastal amenities.This acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. PARTNERS WITH XPV WATER TO OFFLOAD AXIUS WATER TO CRH

KKR & Co. Inc. has announced a strategic divestment in collaboration with XPV Water Partners, selling their portfolio company, Axius Water, to global building materials group CRH. This significant transaction is poised to reshape the water management landscape, as both investment firms focus on maximizing returns for their stakeholders. The deal was confirmed on 6th May 2026 at 18:26:48.The sale of Axius Water is expected to enhance CRH's capabilities in providing innovative water solutions, a growing necessity in today’s environmental landscape. KKR & Co. Inc., a leading global investment firm, and XPV Water Partners, renowned for their expertise in water-related investments, have strategically aligned their interests to ensure a smooth transition and continued growth for Axius Water under CRH's stewardship.Axius Water has been recognized for its cutting-edge technologies and solutions in the water sector, which aligns with CRH's commitment to sustainability and efficiency. The deal underscores KKR & Co. Inc.'s strategy to focus on high-potential sectors while enabling XPV Water Partners to realize the value of their investment in Axius Water. Financial details of the transaction have not been disclosed, but it is anticipated to be a significant milestone for all parties involved.KKR & Co. Inc. has been actively involved in the water sector, recognizing the increasing importance of water management solutions globally. By divesting Axius Water, KKR & Co. Inc. continues to refine its portfolio, concentrating on areas projected for substantial growth. This move is indicative of the broader trend among private equity firms to capitalize on emerging markets while ensuring their investments yield optimal results.The partnership between KKR & Co. Inc. and XPV Water Partners reflects a growing trend in the private equity space, where firms are increasingly looking to streamline their operations and focus on core competencies. As the water industry continues to evolve, this transaction positions CRH to leverage Axius Water's strengths in the market, potentially leading to enhanced service offerings and increased shareholder value.

+ KKR'S VERTIS INFRA TRUST PLANS IPO FILING FOR UP TO ₹3,000 CRORE

KKR's infrastructure investment trust, Vertis Infrastructure Trust, is poised to file draft papers for an initial public offering (IPO) by the end of May 2026. As per sources, the filing is expected to take place on or before the 31st of May, 2026-05-04 20:29:41.558299, and could enable the trust to raise up to ₹3,000 crore through a combination of primary and secondary share sales.The IPO plans come as Vertis operates a portfolio of toll and annuity-generating road projects across India. While the potential deal size is estimated at ₹3,000 crore, insiders note that this figure may be adjusted downward since the trust has limited requirements for new primary capital. The final amount remains under discussion, reflecting a cautious approach to fundraising amid a competitive InvIT landscape.Investor interest in infrastructure investment trusts (InvITs) has surged, particularly among institutional players and high-net-worth individuals (HNIs) seeking refuge from stock market volatility. The current geopolitical climate, notably influenced by the Iran war, has prompted a shift towards safer investment vehicles that offer stable, long-term yields. Reports suggest that InvIT fundraising could exceed ₹25,000 crore this calendar year, driven by a series of upcoming IPOs.Notably, recent months have seen significant activity in the InvIT sector, with the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) successfully raising over ₹9,000 crore through two separate InvIT offerings in March. This momentum continued into April, when EAAA India Alternatives-sponsored Citius Transnet Investment Trust raised ₹1,105 crore, showcasing strong demand despite challenging market conditions.Vertis Infrastructure Trust has established a robust presence in India's infrastructure landscape, having been primarily developed through strategic acquisitions. KKR's Asian infrastructure fund initiated this platform in 2021 with the acquisition of operational toll and annuity road assets, and further expanded its portfolio in 2024-25 with a significant acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The trust currently manages a portfolio of 28 road projects spanning nine states, valued at nearly ₹26,500 crore.

+ KKR & CO. INC. ALLOCATES $10B FOR AI INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENT

KKR & Co. Inc. has announced a significant investment of $10 billion dedicated to the development of AI-focused power plants and data centers. This strategic move reflects the increasing mobilization of capital in the United States aimed at bolstering AI infrastructure. On 3rd May 2026, KKR's commitment highlighted its role in a broader trend where major tech firms, including Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta, are collectively investing over $100 billion worldwide in similar infrastructure improvements.This hefty investment is designed to secure long-term power agreements, thereby providing a strategic advantage in power generation as the demand for electricity from AI data centers continues to rise. KKR's initiative not only underscores its commitment to enhancing the tech ecosystem but also aligns with a growing geopolitical strategy. Middle powers like Canada and Germany are increasingly consolidating their efforts to counterbalance US-China dominance in the AI sector, and KKR's investment could play a pivotal role in shaping that landscape.The implications of KKR & Co. Inc.'s $10 billion investment are significant, particularly for companies like Google. Market interpretations suggest a positive correlation between this investment and Google's prospects for developing the leading AI model by May 2026. The infrastructure enhancements are seen as supportive of optimistic predictions for Google’s stock price, which is anticipated to reach $310, reflecting a broader confidence in the growth potential stemming from such substantial infrastructure investments.As the technology sector evolves, observers are advised to monitor subsequent developments in AI infrastructure investments from major players like KKR & Co. Inc. Additionally, geopolitical responses from nations resisting US-China influence in AI will be crucial. Google’s strategies in relation to these investments and its ongoing advancements in AI capabilities will be significant factors to watch in the coming months, as they could substantially impact market dynamics and investor sentiment.In conclusion, KKR & Co. Inc.'s $10 billion commitment to AI power plants and data centers marks a pivotal moment for the US tech infrastructure landscape. The investment not only bolsters KKR's portfolio but also enhances the operational capabilities of major tech firms, setting the stage for potential breakthroughs in AI development and market performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP'S STRATEGIC MOVE INTO HEALTHCARE TECHNOLOGY SERVICES

The Carlyle Group L.P. has garnered attention following its recent strategic move to integrate two healthcare service providers into a unified platform focused on advanced digital capabilities. This development, announced on the 4th of May, 2026, highlights a broader shift towards automation and data-driven systems within the healthcare services landscape across the United States.Carlyle's expansion into healthcare services is a response to the ongoing structural changes faced by healthcare systems across the United States, which are influenced by administrative complexities, workforce challenges, and evolving care delivery models. The integration of Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM aims to address these challenges by creating a platform capable of supporting various healthcare segments, including physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers that require tailored operational assistance.The significance of the combined platform lies in its ability to enhance revenue cycle management through the integration of complementary strengths from both Knack and Equalize. Knack offers a globally distributed operational framework, while Equalize brings proprietary tools for predictive analytics and process optimization. This unified approach is designed to improve efficiency in billing, claims processing, and payer coordination, ultimately reshaping how healthcare organizations manage their financial workflows.Carlyle's move aligns with broader market trends as healthcare providers increasingly seek technology-driven solutions to navigate operational complexities. The demand for integrated platforms that can streamline billing processes while ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements has led to a growing interest in specialized service providers. By positioning itself within this evolving landscape, Carlyle is tapping into a sector that is experiencing significant transformation.As the healthcare services sector continues to embrace digital infrastructure advancements, Carlyle's focus on integrating advanced technology within its platform underscores the importance of innovation in addressing industry challenges. With automation and data-driven systems becoming critical components of healthcare administration, Carlyle is poised to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of healthcare services.

+ CARLYLE GROUP EXPANDS HEALTHCARE PORTFOLIO WITH KNACK AND EQUALIZERCM ACQUISITION

Carlyle Group Inc. has announced its acquisition of a majority stake in healthcare revenue cycle management (RCM) firms Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, marking a significant expansion in its healthcare portfolio. The announcement was made in a statement on May 4th, 2026, although specific financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.The acquisition allows Carlyle Group to integrate the expertise of Knack's founder Rajiv Sharma and EqualizeRCM's Nagi Rao, both of whom will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the newly formed RCM platform. This collaboration aims to enhance Carlyle's advisory services by leveraging advanced analytics and global operations, as emphasized by Rao in the statement.Knack and EqualizeRCM serve a diverse clientele, including physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals. The partnership is expected to tackle complex aspects of the revenue cycle, such as rural cost reports and intricate anesthesia cases, according to Knack's Chief Executive Officer Gautam Barai. This strategic move aligns with Carlyle's vision to broaden its reach in the RCM industry.Carlyle Group has expressed intentions to pursue further opportunities within the RCM sector, indicating a continued focus on enhancing its capabilities in healthcare management. The acquisition of Knack and EqualizeRCM is seen as a crucial step in solidifying Carlyle's position in a rapidly evolving market, where efficient revenue management is essential for healthcare providers.As the healthcare landscape continues to change, the integration of advanced technologies and strategic partnerships will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of revenue cycle management. Carlyle Group’s acquisition of Knack and EqualizeRCM is poised to deliver innovative solutions that address the challenges faced by healthcare providers today.

+ CARLYLE GROUP STRENGTHENS LEADERSHIP WITH THREE NEW CO-PRESIDENTS

Carlyle Group L.P. has announced the elevation of three senior executives to the role of co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz. This strategic move, revealed on 22nd April 2026, aims to bolster decision-making and streamline operations at the private equity firm as it seeks to refine its business strategy in a challenging economic environment.The appointments of these co-presidents signal a significant shift in Carlyle Group's leadership structure, enhancing the company's focus on execution across its various business lines. With rising interest rates and a slowdown in deal flow, the firm is adapting to new market realities that require a more cohesive and agile leadership approach. By consolidating power within a closer circle around Schwartz, Carlyle Group is positioning itself to navigate these challenges effectively.Harvey Schwartz, who took the helm during a transitional leadership period, has been vocal about the need for clearer accountability and expedited decision-making processes. The introduction of three co-presidents reflects a strategy that has been successfully employed by other major firms, allowing for shared oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and operational management. This approach is expected to enhance the firm's responsiveness to market dynamics and improve overall performance.As Carlyle Group continues to adapt to the evolving landscape of private equity, the new leadership structure is expected to play a crucial role in guiding the firm through the complexities of the current economic climate. Investors will be keenly observing how these changes impact Carlyle Group's investment strategies and financial performance in the coming quarters.In conclusion, Carlyle Group's decision to elevate three executives to co-presidents underlines a commitment to a more unified leadership approach, aimed at fostering efficiency and effectiveness. As the firm navigates through an era marked by economic uncertainty, the focus will be on how well this new leadership structure translates into tangible results for investors and stakeholders alike.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS HOLDINGS SECURES $300 MILLION LOAN FROM OAKTREE CAPITAL

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility with Oaktree Capital Management, which is poised to significantly reshape the company's capital structure. This financing arrangement, which refinances existing debt through 2031 while reserving US$35 million for future growth initiatives, was established on 3rd May 2026.The loan features a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure complemented by flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets. This arrangement enhances Establishment Labs Holdings' funding capacity and provides greater balance sheet flexibility as it embarks on operational and expansion plans. The infusion of capital could also serve to bolster its investment narrative in a competitive market.Central to the investment case for Establishment Labs is the belief that its Motiva implant platform and minimally invasive procedures will translate into significant global market adoption. Recent management guidance projects 2026 revenues between US$264 million and US$266 million, suggesting a growth rate of approximately 25% to 26% compared to the previous year. The new loan not only alleviates refinancing pressures but also offers liquidity that assists in tackling ongoing losses and cash burn without necessitating an immediate equity raise.While the improved balance sheet flexibility presents a positive outlook, investors are advised to remain cautious about potential risks associated with continued cash burn and negative free cash flow. The new facility comes at a time when some analysts have projected an optimistic revenue growth trajectory of about 30% annually, with earnings reaching US$73.5 million by 2029. This could either reinforce bullish sentiments surrounding the company's future or prompt a more tempered view of its path to profitability.In summary, Establishment Labs Holdings' recent agreement with Oaktree Capital Management marks a pivotal moment in its financial strategy. Investors are encouraged to evaluate the potential impact of this loan on the company's long-term growth objectives, while remaining aware of the inherent risks in its operational execution and market demand.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK WITH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a significant Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks an important milestone in the company's efforts to manage its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026, and it includes both the amendment of existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders.The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, introducing new provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, allowing lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, highlighting the company's strategic approach to financing.In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel has committed to filing registration statements with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This move is essential for maintaining investor confidence, as it reinforces the company's intention to provide liquidity and transparency to its stakeholders. The lenders have indicated their intent to hold the securities for investment purposes only, which could prevent immediate market fluctuations due to rapid selling.Investors should be aware of the potential implications of these developments. The issuance of a substantial number of new warrants could lead to dilution of existing shareholders' equity if exercised. Additionally, the company’s obligations to register these shares for resale could increase the total share count, which may affect the stock's market price. Such financing strategies can signal to the market the company's liquidity needs or strategic objectives.BioXcel Therapeutics has clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company, thereby adhering to full reporting and compliance requirements. The registration rights agreement, which includes various Oaktree entities among other institutional holders, reflects a commitment to ongoing compliance with securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor upcoming filings and press releases closely, as these changes may significantly influence BioXcel's financial landscape and market performance.

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT AND NORTH MILL COMPLETE $355.8 MILLION SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have successfully closed a $355.8 million securitization backed by equipment finance assets, indicating robust investor interest in the equipment finance sector. As announced on 10th April 2026, the transaction was oversubscribed by approximately 6.3 times, reflecting a strong demand for such financial products.The securitization includes equipment loans and leases acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in November 2025, with North Mill Equipment Finance (NMEF) taking on the servicing responsibilities. This strategic partnership allows NMEF to leverage its expertise in managing small- to mid-ticket equipment loans and leases, typically valued between $15,000 and $3 million.The notes issued in the transaction received ratings ranging from AAA to A, with an average yield of 4.7% and a spread of 99 basis points. Truist Securities led the transaction, with Deutsche Bank serving as co-manager, underscoring the confidence of major financial institutions in the equipment finance market.Headquartered in Norwalk, Connecticut, North Mill Equipment Finance will continue to manage the portfolio post-securitization, ensuring ongoing operational support and loan management. Meanwhile, Los Angeles-based Oaktree Capital Management, which oversees approximately $223 billion in assets, is expanding its presence in the asset-backed securities (ABS) finance space.As the equipment finance landscape evolves, industry professionals will gather at the fourth annual Equipment Finance Connect, scheduled for May 18-19 at the C. Baldwin Hotel in Houston. This event will serve as a platform for equipment lenders and dealers to discuss trends, challenges, and opportunities in the sector, further highlighting the significance of partnerships like the one between Oaktree and North Mill.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO EXPANDS FRESH FOOD FOOTPRINT WITH PROSOL GROUP ACQUISITION

Apollo Global Management LLC has acquired a majority stake in Prosol Group, a prominent player in the French fresh food retail market, marking a significant expansion of its presence in this sector. The transaction, finalized on the 7th of May 2026, was executed through Apollo-managed funds, which have successfully secured this investment from Ardian, a global private investment firm.Founded in 1992, Prosol Group has built a strong reputation in the fresh food sector by establishing a vertically integrated supply chain that emphasizes quality and freshness. The company operates nearly 450 stores across France, primarily under the brands Grand Frais and fresh, which have fostered a loyal customer base and robust growth. Jean-Paul Mochet will continue to lead Prosol as Chief Executive Officer, working towards the company’s long-term growth objectives in collaboration with Apollo.This acquisition represents an opportunity for Apollo to enhance its footprint in the fresh food retail sector, leveraging Prosol's established supply chain and customer loyalty. As Apollo Global Management LLC manages approximately $1.03 trillion in assets, the firm aims to utilize its expertise to support Prosol's expansion and maximize potential returns from this investment. Financial advisory services for this transaction were provided by UBS AG, with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard also involved in the advisory process.While the acquisition presents numerous growth opportunities, it also carries inherent risks, particularly in the volatile fresh food market. Challenges such as supply chain disruptions and shifting consumer preferences could impact the investment. Furthermore, the financial terms of the acquisition have not been disclosed, leading to questions regarding the valuation of Prosol and the expected financial implications for Apollo.As the integration of Prosol into Apollo's portfolio unfolds, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the alignment between Prosol's operational strategy and Apollo's broader investment objectives. With a strong management team in place and a commitment to maintaining high-quality standards, Apollo Global Management is poised to capitalize on the growth potential within the French fresh food retail sector.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT SET TO OFFER DAILY PRICING FOR PRIVATE ASSETS

Apollo Global Management is making strides in the private asset management sector by launching a pilot program to introduce daily pricing for its private asset portfolios. The announcement comes as the firm reported that its total assets under management surpassed the $1 trillion milestone for the first time, a significant achievement for the alternatives manager. On the 6th of May 2026, during the first quarter results call, Marc Rowan, chairman and chief executive of Apollo, emphasized the company's strong performance, stating that record fee-related earnings contributed to this growth.Apollo's total inflows reached a remarkable $115 billion in the first quarter alone, with a cumulative $300 billion over the past twelve months. This surge in assets under management represented a 31% year-over-year increase, totaling $1.03 trillion as of March 31, 2026. The asset management business saw inflows of $95 billion, largely driven by Athora's acquisition of the Pension Insurance Corporation in the UK, alongside significant institutional and global wealth inflows into credit-focused strategies. The firm's retirement services business also contributed $20 billion in inflows, reflecting robust activity across various segments.In a broader context, the Financial Stability Board released a report on vulnerabilities in private credit on the same day Apollo announced its new pricing initiative. While acknowledging the rapid growth of private credit, the report highlighted various risks, including valuation opacity and significant sector exposures. In response, Rowan defended Apollo's approach, asserting that the media's focus on a narrow segment of the private credit market misrepresents the overall landscape. He emphasized that prudent underwriting is crucial for performance in private credit markets.To bolster investor confidence and transparency, Apollo initiated a pilot last year to produce estimated daily values for its investment-grade fixed income products. Under this new framework, investors can expect daily pricing for corporate investment-grade fixed income assets by 6:30 PM ET and for direct lending and asset-backed finance by 9:30 PM ET. Rowan articulated the importance of rigorous processes in maintaining transparency, underscoring that when public markets adjust, private markets should follow suit. This initiative aims to enhance liquidity and transparency in private markets, which Rowan believes will drive significant growth in the asset class.Looking ahead, Apollo's performance indicators suggest a strong outlook for the remainder of 2026. The company reaffirmed its growth targets, anticipating 20% fee-related earnings growth and 10% SRE growth, despite ongoing geopolitical pressures. With a robust pipeline for origination, Rowan expressed confidence in achieving or nearing record origination levels. As institutional interest in private credit remains high, Apollo appears well-positioned to navigate market cycles while maintaining a d

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT JOINS FORCES FOR $1.5B AI SERVICES VENTURE

Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) is positioning itself at the forefront of the rapidly evolving artificial intelligence landscape by collaborating with prominent firms such as Anthropic, Blackstone, and Goldman Sachs to launch a $1.5 billion enterprise AI services venture. This initiative aims to deliver cutting-edge AI tools specifically tailored for mid-market businesses and private equity portfolio companies. On the 6th of May 2026, the announcement marks a significant pivot for Apollo as it seeks to integrate advanced technology into its investment strategy.The newly formed company is expected to enhance operational efficiencies for mid-sized businesses, many of which may lack the resources to develop AI technologies independently. Apollo’s partnership with Anthropic and others underscores a strategic shift that leverages collaboration over traditional balance sheet capital, as the firm aims to capture productivity gains and create new fee-based revenue streams within its portfolio. In addition to this venture, Apollo is actively raising capital for its Hybrid Value Fund III and Accord Fund VII, which focus on hybrid and dislocation-oriented investment strategies.Apollo Global Management is known for its diverse portfolio that spans private equity, credit, and real assets. By adding a dedicated enterprise AI initiative, the firm is not only diversifying its investment offerings but also reinforcing its commitment to operational improvements across its portfolio. The fresh funds for Hybrid Value Fund III, which has closed at approximately $6.5 billion, and Accord Fund VII at $1.9 billion, ensure that Apollo remains well-capitalized to navigate various financing situations, whether in stable markets or during times of dislocation.However, investors should remain aware of potential risks associated with this new direction. Apollo’s profit margins have slipped to 11%, down from 17.9% reported a year earlier, indicating pressure on profitability even as the firm explores new avenues for growth. Furthermore, the company's dividend track record has been described as unstable, which could be a concern for income-focused investors. Conversely, Apollo is currently trading at 25.2% below one estimate of its fair value, suggesting possible upside if the AI initiative and new funds perform as anticipated.Looking ahead, stakeholders should monitor Apollo Global Management's ability to convert its enterprise AI venture into tangible results across its portfolio companies. The success of this model, which is designed to target mid-market clients alongside industry giants like Blackstone and Goldman Sachs, will be crucial in determining Apollo’s future trajectory. Additionally, tracking the deployment of capital from Hybrid Value Fund III and Accord Fund VII in dislocated situations will provide insight into how Apollo navigates the current market landscape and adapts its strategies to maintain profitability.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT SECURES $1.6 BILLION FOR JAPAN LOGISTICS FUND AMID STRONG INVESTOR DEMAND

Ares Management L.P has successfully raised $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, reflecting a sustained investor appetite for industrial real estate in the country. This fundraising effort comes in the first three months of 2026, with the first close expected to occur in spring, according to chief executive Michael Arougheti during an earnings call.Arougheti emphasized the strong demand for the new fund, stating, "Our fifth Japan Logistics Development Fund is seeing very strong demand following the excellent performance of prior vintages." This fundraising initiative follows more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund, which amounted to 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares Management completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP International.The recent deal has also opened doors for Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle, backed by significant investors such as the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. The Canadian LP contributed $1.3 billion to this vehicle, which includes some seed assets. Blair Jacobson, co-president of Ares, mentioned that the firm is currently fundraising to address both the seed assets and the robust pipeline associated with them.In addition to its efforts in Japan, Ares Management raised an additional $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy in the first quarter of 2026. This capital influx is largely attributed to commitments made to its seventh special situations fund, SSG Fund VII. The latest fundraising comes on the heels of a $400 million close in 2025 and follows a successful $2.4 billion raise by the predecessor vehicle just two years prior.Notable returning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System and the New York State Common Retirement Fund, which committed $300 million and $200 million, respectively. DealStreetAsia previously reported that the International Finance Corporation (IFC) is considering a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account, which shares a mandate similar to Ares’ SSG series. This fund concentrates on acquiring non-performing loans and single-asset distress situations across several Southeast Asian markets.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT REPORTS STRONG Q1 2026 DIRECT LENDING ACTIVITY

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) has reported a significant uptick in its U.S. direct lending activities, closing approximately $9.5 billion across 70 transactions during the first quarter of 2026. This announcement, made on the 4th of May 2026, highlights Ares Management's strong position in the lending market, which has been bolstered by an increase in demand for flexible capital solutions in various sectors.Over the 12 months leading up to March 31, 2026, Ares Management closed approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions, underscoring its robust performance in the direct lending space. The firm has established itself as a key player by providing capital to businesses seeking growth and expansion, which is evident from the diverse range of transactions it has facilitated in recent months.Among the noteworthy deals closed by Ares Management in the first quarter are senior secured credit facilities supporting various growth initiatives. For instance, Ares acted as the administrative agent for a credit facility aiding Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, a company specializing in fire and life safety solutions. Additionally, Ares was instrumental in Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, a provider of critical electrical and mechanical services across multiple industries.Further emphasizing its pivotal role in facilitating business growth, Ares Management also served as the lead arranger for a credit facility supporting ARCHIMED's plans for the North American Science Association, a leading contract research organization dedicated to the development of medical devices. Such transactions reflect Ares Management's commitment to supporting vital sectors that contribute to economic development.As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management's global platform boasts over $644 billion in assets under management, with operations spanning North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East. This diverse portfolio positions Ares Management as a leading global alternative investment manager, dedicated to delivering consistent and attractive returns for its investors while contributing positively to the communities in which it operates.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH WHITESTONE REIT ACQUISITION

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has announced an agreement to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at approximately US$1.7 billion, effectively taking the retail-focused real estate investment trust private. This strategic move is aimed at expanding Ares' portfolio in necessity-based retail centers located in rapidly growing metropolitan areas across the United States.The announcement, made on 14th April 2026, also included significant leadership changes within Ares Management's Asia operations. E.G. Morse has been appointed as Partner and Head of Asia Credit, while Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong will lead the Asia Special Situations team. These appointments reflect Ares' commitment to enhancing its credit and special situations strategies in the region, aligning with the company's broader growth objectives.The acquisition of Whitestone REIT is particularly noteworthy as it allows Ares Management to bolster its position in the retail real estate sector, catering to essential consumer needs. Investors monitoring NYSE:ARES should consider how this acquisition fits into Ares' overall strategy, which increasingly emphasizes private credit, real assets, and special situations. The move underscores Ares' intent to diversify its capital allocation across various geographies and asset types.Key considerations for investors include the integration of Whitestone into Ares’ existing operational framework, as well as the execution of fundraising initiatives in Asia. The new leadership team is expected to play a critical role in determining how capital is deployed and managed across regions. Nonetheless, investors should be mindful of potential risks, including profit margin compression, high levels of debt, and the current dividend yield of 5.38%, which is not fully supported by earnings.As Ares Management embarks on this significant acquisition and leadership restructuring, the implications for its future growth trajectory will be closely watched. The market's response to these developments could provide insights into how effectively Ares can leverage its new assets and leadership to enhance shareholder value over time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES CARGO SERVICES TO IRAQ WITH ENHANCED CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, according to its latest published schedule, marking the return of cargo connectivity between Doha and Baghdad. This development follows the airline's phased restoration of services, with cargo sales now open for shipments on the Doha to Baghdad route.Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will introduce a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad. This will be complemented by the launch of twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route starting from 10th May, thereby adding belly-hold cargo capacity alongside dedicated freighter operations. With both freighter and passenger services in place, Qatar Airways will offer a combined cargo capacity of over 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad beginning 10th May.The new addition is expected to significantly enhance cargo flows between the two markets, leveraging a mix of scheduled freighter uplift and passenger aircraft capacity. Qatar Airways Cargo reported that its freighter network has expanded to more than 60 destinations as of 1st May. The passenger network is also set to grow further, reaching over 150 destinations from 16th June as the airline continues to scale up operations.All cargo products are now available for booking through standard sales channels, including the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams. The airline confirmed that capacity is open for customer bookings, and those with shipment-specific inquiries are encouraged to reach out to cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or their local sales representatives.The resumption of services to Baghdad forms part of Qatar Airways Cargo ’s broader network strategy, which combines freighter operations with passenger aircraft capacity to support global cargo demand. The airline operates a fleet of 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, supported by a road feeder network, while continuing to invest in digitalization, products, and infrastructure as part of its operational strategy.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS MARKS RETURN TO ERBIL WITH RESUMPTION OF FLIGHTS

Qatar Airways will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the return of direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a wider reinstatement of its Iraq operations, the airline announced Saturday.The Doha-based carrier stated that it will restart services to three Iraqi cities — Erbil, Baghdad (BGW), and Basra (BSR) — in a phased return aimed at rebuilding its regional network and restoring connectivity across key Middle Eastern destinations.In parallel with passenger services, Qatar Airways will also resume freighter operations to Baghdad starting 7th May, reinforcing cargo and trade links with Iraq. The airline noted that these resumptions follow recent reinstatements of flights to several regional destinations, including Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus, and Kozhikode, as part of a wider strategy to restore its global network.By mid-June 2026, Qatar Airways expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide. Passengers traveling through Erbil International Airport (EBL) will gain renewed access to Qatar Airways’ global network via its Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, which the airline describes as a key connector linking six continents.The carrier, which has been repeatedly recognized as the 'World’s Best Airline' by Skytrax, emphasized that its return to Iraq reflects ongoing efforts to restore stability to its route network while maintaining flexibility in response to regional conditions. The resumption of passenger flights to Erbil follows the suspension of services in late February 2026 due to the outbreak of the US–Israel–Iran war, which triggered widespread airspace closures across Iraq and the wider Middle East.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS FLIGHT NETWORK, BOOSTING AUSTRALIA'S TOURISM POTENTIAL

Australia’s tourism industry is set to receive a major boost as Qatar Airways announces significant increases in its flights between Doha and key Australian cities. As of 5th May 2026, the expanded services come at a crucial time for the Australian travel market, helping to reconnect Australia with major international tourism sources in Europe, Asia, and the Middle East. With the airline resuming daily flights and adding more options for travelers, this move is expected to foster a strong recovery for Australia’s tourism sector after a period of disruption.Beginning in May 2026, Qatar Airways will reinstate daily flights from Doha to Sydney, Melbourne, and Perth. These routes, which were previously limited to around five flights a week, will now operate daily, ensuring better accessibility for international travelers looking to explore Australia’s top destinations. Additionally, the airline will launch four weekly flights to Brisbane starting 16th May, which will increase to daily flights by 16th June 2026, giving more tourists the opportunity to visit Queensland’s attractions.This flight expansion reflects Qatar Airways’ ongoing commitment to strengthening global connectivity, and the airline is capitalizing on its strategic hub in Doha. By offering these additional flights, Qatar Airways makes it easier for travelers from Europe, the Middle East, and Asia to plan direct routes to Australia, supporting the country’s tourism recovery post-pandemic. The increased connectivity will not only enhance tourism but also improve trade and business opportunities between the two nations.Australia’s travel industry stands to benefit significantly from this expansion. The extra flights are expected to provide an influx of international visitors, boosting demand for accommodations, tours, and local attractions. Major cities like Sydney, Melbourne, and Perth, which already attract millions of tourists annually, are likely to see a further rise in visitors, especially as the summer months approach. For Australia’s tourism bodies, the expanded flights mean more opportunities to market the country as an accessible, diverse, and attractive destination.However, it’s essential to acknowledge that travel advisories continue to affect certain routes. According to Australia’s Smart Traveller service, there remains an active “Do Not Travel” advisory for Qatar due to ongoing regional security concerns. This caution applies to all travelers, even those transiting through Doha’s Hamad International Airport. Despite these advisories, the increased flight frequencies demonstrate Qatar Airways’ confidence in the safety and operational efficiency of its services. Looking ahead, the partnership between Qatar Airways and Australia is expected to flourish, positioning the tourism sector for long-term growth.

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ACE US SPACE TECH ACTIVE ETF TOPS 100 BILLION WON IN NET ASSETS AS EARLY INTEREST GROWS

As the space sector evolves, the ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capture emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth space technologies.

+ KIC ANNOUNCES 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY TO STRENGTHEN FINANCE TALENT

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, set to begin on 20th April 2026, with a mission to raise finance professionals' expertise in both public and private sectors.The program comprises eight lectures over four days, starting with a 2026 outlook on the U.S. stock market and covering alternative investments such as infrastructure and private debt, plus post-investment portfolio management from both management and operational perspectives.It will also tackle topics including the U.S. interest-rate environment and the institutionalization of stablecoins, with applications already received from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, said the organization will continue to share its overseas investment experience and global network to foster domestic growth, noting that the International Finance Academy has been expanding since its 2022 inception to strengthen local investment professionals.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION EXPANDS FOCUS ON MID-MARKET INFRA AT NEW YORK FORUM

The 28th New York Finance Forum, hosted by KIC at the Korea Society, took place on April 16, 2026, with over 40 participants focusing on North American mid-market infrastructure investments.Northleaf Capital Partners outlined that mid-market deals are typically USD 1 billion or less, with eligibility expanding to up to USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics; 86% of North American infra transactions occur in the mid-market, while funds remain biased toward larger vehicles.Jessica Kennedy highlighted the appeal of mid-market investments for value creation and multiple exit options, predicting solid risk-adjusted returns; Yuryoung Kim noted optimism that the forum would help domestic investors sharpen overseas investment strategies and emphasized KIC's global network to support information sharing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ARDIAN AND GOLDMAN EXPAND SECONDARY FOOTPRINT WITH CIC PURCHASE

On 15th April 2026, Goldman Sachs and Ardian acquired a $1 billion CIC portfolio at notable discounts, with Ardian obtaining a double-digit markdown and Goldman a single-digit discount.CIC is reshaping its holdings to reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. By 2024, nearly half of its assets were allocated to alternatives, and about 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally.The sale follows CIC's earlier pause on divesting stakes in Carlyle, KKR and TPG and underscores the buyers' continued expansion of the secondary market. Ardian has just raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions totaling over $80 billion. The move comes as institutions such as Harvard and various Asian managers increasingly turn to secondaries for liquidity and rebalancing in a market where concentration and illiquidity concerns persist.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES GP POSITION IN CICC–HBIS FUND, AIMING TO ACCELERATE CHINA’S ADVANCED MATERIALS, NEW ENERGY AND IT INVESTMENTS

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The announcement was made at a ceremony at HBIS Group’s R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds plans to raise RMB32 billion over a 15-year term and will deploy capital through a blend of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS’s industrial priorities, including traditional sectors and emerging fields such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.Structured around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the vehicle is designed to connect local governments with international capital and drive industrial upgrading. BlueFive will work with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to support the fund’s long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, said the team is bullish on China’s long-term growth potential and sees opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group ranks among the world’s largest steel producers and has appeared on the Fortune Global 500 list for 17 consecutive years, while CICC provides a comprehensive financial services platform in Beijing. The partnership is expected to bolster investment opportunities and regional growth.

+ INVEST ALBERTA NOTES RENEWED CHINESE INTEREST AS CANADA ATTRACTS $60 BILLION IN FDI

China and Hong Kong’s foreign direct investment into Canada surpassed $60 billion by the end of 2023, making them the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta attracted roughly half of the total, with China at $24.9 billion and Hong Kong at $32.8 billion, according to Statistics Canada, placing them behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.The Globe and Mail, in a December 30, 2025 report, highlighted sustained Alberta–China/Hong Kong engagement despite federal tensions with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse noted rising talk with federal authorities and described the period as 'harvest time' for investors who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta says the investments are centered on oil and gas, carbon capture, green-energy projects and tourism in Alberta, with China serving as a key customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline. The relationship has shifted since the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians from 2018 to 2021, with policy steps such as a 2024 visit to Beijing by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly and a potential 2026 visit linked to a broader diplomatic reset. While ties evolve, Canada remains far more exposed to the United States for FDI (about 45.7% in 2023) than to Asia-Oceania (11.8%), where Japan, Hong Kong and China play lead roles.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJ7-P0T3-S31K-S1WD-00000-00May 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. & PEER INSIGHTS2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:19ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. & PEER INSIGHTS

Consigli Construction Co. is making significant strides with its recent involvement in the Williams College Museum of Art project, marking the museum's first purpose-built facility on campus. Set to begin on February 25, 2026, the project presents a unique design challenge that aims to enhance student access to art collections. Abigail Berkebile, a prominent figure at Consigli Construction Co., highlighted the project's complexity and the rewarding nature of collaborating with the museum staff. In conjunction with this development, Berkebile emphasized the vital role of women in the construction industry, noting that opportunities extend far beyond traditional field positions to include accounting, IT, legal, marketing, and design. By maintaining a structured approach to daily team collaboration, Consigli Construction Co. continues to focus on executing complex institutional builds with a diverse array of professional expertise.

In the broader sector, Suffolk Construction is advancing critical infrastructure and renewable energy initiatives. On May 5, 2026, the Virginia Department of Transportation is scheduled to lift a long-term lane closure on U.S. Route 58 westbound near downtown Suffolk, a change tied to the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by the firm. The project will ultimately provide a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to access a regional landfill. Concurrently, Virgin Media O2 announced a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement on May 1, 2026, to source energy from a 70MW solar farm in Suffolk, which is currently under construction and expected to commence power generation in 2027. This solar initiative is projected to supply approximately five percent of the telecommunications company's total energy demands.

Turner Construction is also marking major structural milestones across multiple large-scale developments. On April 28, 2026, the company celebrated the topping out of the 72-megawatt Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, a 450,000-square-foot facility featuring a hybrid cooling solution. Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction, credited the milestone to strong collaboration with Aligned and the dedication of approximately 345 skilled workers logging over 80,000 craft hours. On the same day, Turner Construction announced the commencement of vertical construction for the $900 million expansion of the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania. The 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower will increase the hospital's capacity to 500 beds and is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028, supporting up to 3,800 construction jobs and generating an estimated $1 billion in local economic impact.

Gilbane Development continues to expand its leadership and strategic partnerships to drive future growth. On April 28, 2026, the firm appointed Andreas Vlahakis as the new head of capital markets, based in New York City. Reporting to President and CEO James Patchett, Vlahakis will direct the firm's funding strategy and lead equity-raising efforts for ground-up multifamily and student housing investments. Meanwhile, Gilbane is moving forward with a major public-private partnership at Western Kentucky University. Following approval by the university's Board of Regents in December 2025 and authorization from the Kentucky Legislature on April 6, 2026, Gilbane is now cleared to design and develop new residence halls to replace outdated campus housing, signaling a continuous push toward modernizing student facilities.

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART SECURES $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW AGREEMENT WITH FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP

Upstart Holdings, an AI lending marketplace, has announced a forward-flow commitment from funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group, under which Fortress has agreed to purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through the Upstart platform over 15 months. The announcement was made on 5th May 2026.This forward-flow agreement marks a significant expansion of the existing partnership between Upstart and Fortress, which initially began with a transaction in 2025. The collaboration illustrates Upstart’s ongoing efforts to broaden its network of capital partners, which now includes over 100 banks and credit unions leveraging its AI-driven models and cloud applications. The platform offers a wide range of financial products, including personal loans, automotive retail and refinance loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product."We’re excited to expand our partnership with Fortress with this latest agreement. Our wide array of capital partners strengthens our resilient and stable foundation to continue driving down the cost and complexity of borrowing," said Sanjay Datta, President of Capital and Enterprise at Upstart. This sentiment underscores Upstart's commitment to enhancing its service offerings while fostering relationships with key financial institutions.Commenting on the expanded agreement, Matt Biczak, Managing Director at Fortress Investment Group, stated, "This expanded agreement with Upstart underscores our focus on sourcing differentiated, high-quality consumer credit opportunities for our investors. Upstart’s data-driven platform enables efficient access to scaled origination, and we believe this partnership positions us well to continue generating durable, compelling risk-adjusted returns across market environments."The partnership between Upstart and Fortress Investment Group reflects a growing trend in the financial sector, where technology-driven solutions are increasingly being embraced to enhance the efficiency and accessibility of consumer credit. As Upstart continues to innovate and expand its offerings, the strategic alignment with Fortress positions both companies to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the evolving lending landscape.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH $180 MILLION INVESTMENT

Pure Salmon Japan secures a significant preferred note investment of over USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. This investment marks a pivotal milestone for Pure Salmon Japan, as it continues to develop its state-of-the-art land-based salmon farming facility in Japan. The transaction was completed on 1st April 2026 and is expected to support the construction of the facility, which is designed to meet the growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The partnership with Fortress Investment Group highlights the institutional backing that Pure Salmon Japan has garnered for its ambitious project. With a target production of 10,000 tonnes, this facility aims to become the largest land-based salmon farm in Japan. By utilizing advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology, the facility is set to contribute to Japan's food security by reducing reliance on imports and ensuring a stable supply of locally produced salmon.Erol Emed, CEO of Pure Salmon Japan, expressed gratitude for the investment, emphasizing the strategic importance of the project amidst Japan's focus on enhancing domestic seafood production. He stated, 'We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan.'Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding history in Japan, is not only bringing capital but also valuable expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, noted the importance of the facility in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure. This investment aligns with Fortress’s broader strategy to support sustainable initiatives that address pressing global challenges such as food security and environmental sustainability.As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan aims to engage with additional long-term equity partners to further bolster its operational capabilities. The successful closing of this financing not only showcases the increasing institutional investor interest in sustainable aquaculture but also positions Pure Salmon Japan as a key player in the evolving seafood supply chain in Japan.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. This acquisition, made on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by affiliates of Fortress, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.The Tuscan Gardens community features 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus approximately one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, catering to an affluent and growing senior population along Florida’s northeast coast. Under AgeWell’s operational leadership, occupancy and performance metrics have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, expressed enthusiasm about the acquisition, stating, "We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families."Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast features thoughtfully designed residences, diverse dining options, wellness and fitness amenities, landscaped courtyards, and personalized care programs tailored to individual resident needs. The community benefits from strong demographic trends and limited new senior housing supply locally, as well as proximity to healthcare, retail, and coastal amenities.This acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. PARTNERS WITH XPV WATER TO OFFLOAD AXIUS WATER TO CRH

KKR & Co. Inc. has announced a strategic divestment in collaboration with XPV Water Partners, selling their portfolio company, Axius Water, to global building materials group CRH. This significant transaction is poised to reshape the water management landscape, as both investment firms focus on maximizing returns for their stakeholders. The deal was confirmed on 6th May 2026 at 18:26:48.The sale of Axius Water is expected to enhance CRH's capabilities in providing innovative water solutions, a growing necessity in today’s environmental landscape. KKR & Co. Inc., a leading global investment firm, and XPV Water Partners, renowned for their expertise in water-related investments, have strategically aligned their interests to ensure a smooth transition and continued growth for Axius Water under CRH's stewardship.Axius Water has been recognized for its cutting-edge technologies and solutions in the water sector, which aligns with CRH's commitment to sustainability and efficiency. The deal underscores KKR & Co. Inc.'s strategy to focus on high-potential sectors while enabling XPV Water Partners to realize the value of their investment in Axius Water. Financial details of the transaction have not been disclosed, but it is anticipated to be a significant milestone for all parties involved.KKR & Co. Inc. has been actively involved in the water sector, recognizing the increasing importance of water management solutions globally. By divesting Axius Water, KKR & Co. Inc. continues to refine its portfolio, concentrating on areas projected for substantial growth. This move is indicative of the broader trend among private equity firms to capitalize on emerging markets while ensuring their investments yield optimal results.The partnership between KKR & Co. Inc. and XPV Water Partners reflects a growing trend in the private equity space, where firms are increasingly looking to streamline their operations and focus on core competencies. As the water industry continues to evolve, this transaction positions CRH to leverage Axius Water's strengths in the market, potentially leading to enhanced service offerings and increased shareholder value.

+ KKR'S VERTIS INFRA TRUST PLANS IPO FILING FOR UP TO ₹3,000 CRORE

KKR's infrastructure investment trust, Vertis Infrastructure Trust, is poised to file draft papers for an initial public offering (IPO) by the end of May 2026. As per sources, the filing is expected to take place on or before the 31st of May, 2026-05-04 20:29:41.558299, and could enable the trust to raise up to ₹3,000 crore through a combination of primary and secondary share sales.The IPO plans come as Vertis operates a portfolio of toll and annuity-generating road projects across India. While the potential deal size is estimated at ₹3,000 crore, insiders note that this figure may be adjusted downward since the trust has limited requirements for new primary capital. The final amount remains under discussion, reflecting a cautious approach to fundraising amid a competitive InvIT landscape.Investor interest in infrastructure investment trusts (InvITs) has surged, particularly among institutional players and high-net-worth individuals (HNIs) seeking refuge from stock market volatility. The current geopolitical climate, notably influenced by the Iran war, has prompted a shift towards safer investment vehicles that offer stable, long-term yields. Reports suggest that InvIT fundraising could exceed ₹25,000 crore this calendar year, driven by a series of upcoming IPOs.Notably, recent months have seen significant activity in the InvIT sector, with the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) successfully raising over ₹9,000 crore through two separate InvIT offerings in March. This momentum continued into April, when EAAA India Alternatives-sponsored Citius Transnet Investment Trust raised ₹1,105 crore, showcasing strong demand despite challenging market conditions.Vertis Infrastructure Trust has established a robust presence in India's infrastructure landscape, having been primarily developed through strategic acquisitions. KKR's Asian infrastructure fund initiated this platform in 2021 with the acquisition of operational toll and annuity road assets, and further expanded its portfolio in 2024-25 with a significant acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The trust currently manages a portfolio of 28 road projects spanning nine states, valued at nearly ₹26,500 crore.

+ KKR & CO. INC. ALLOCATES $10B FOR AI INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENT

KKR & Co. Inc. has announced a significant investment of $10 billion dedicated to the development of AI-focused power plants and data centers. This strategic move reflects the increasing mobilization of capital in the United States aimed at bolstering AI infrastructure. On 3rd May 2026, KKR's commitment highlighted its role in a broader trend where major tech firms, including Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta, are collectively investing over $100 billion worldwide in similar infrastructure improvements.This hefty investment is designed to secure long-term power agreements, thereby providing a strategic advantage in power generation as the demand for electricity from AI data centers continues to rise. KKR's initiative not only underscores its commitment to enhancing the tech ecosystem but also aligns with a growing geopolitical strategy. Middle powers like Canada and Germany are increasingly consolidating their efforts to counterbalance US-China dominance in the AI sector, and KKR's investment could play a pivotal role in shaping that landscape.The implications of KKR & Co. Inc.'s $10 billion investment are significant, particularly for companies like Google. Market interpretations suggest a positive correlation between this investment and Google's prospects for developing the leading AI model by May 2026. The infrastructure enhancements are seen as supportive of optimistic predictions for Google’s stock price, which is anticipated to reach $310, reflecting a broader confidence in the growth potential stemming from such substantial infrastructure investments.As the technology sector evolves, observers are advised to monitor subsequent developments in AI infrastructure investments from major players like KKR & Co. Inc. Additionally, geopolitical responses from nations resisting US-China influence in AI will be crucial. Google’s strategies in relation to these investments and its ongoing advancements in AI capabilities will be significant factors to watch in the coming months, as they could substantially impact market dynamics and investor sentiment.In conclusion, KKR & Co. Inc.'s $10 billion commitment to AI power plants and data centers marks a pivotal moment for the US tech infrastructure landscape. The investment not only bolsters KKR's portfolio but also enhances the operational capabilities of major tech firms, setting the stage for potential breakthroughs in AI development and market performance.
