+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT SECURES $1.6 BILLION FOR JAPAN LOGISTICS FUND AMID STRONG INVESTOR DEMAND

Ares Management L.P has successfully raised $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, reflecting a sustained investor appetite for industrial real estate in the country. This fundraising effort comes in the first three months of 2026, with the first close expected to occur in spring, according to chief executive Michael Arougheti during an earnings call.Arougheti emphasized the strong demand for the new fund, stating, "Our fifth Japan Logistics Development Fund is seeing very strong demand following the excellent performance of prior vintages." This fundraising initiative follows more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund, which amounted to 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares Management completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP International.The recent deal has also opened doors for Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle, backed by significant investors such as the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. The Canadian LP contributed $1.3 billion to this vehicle, which includes some seed assets. Blair Jacobson, co-president of Ares, mentioned that the firm is currently fundraising to address both the seed assets and the robust pipeline associated with them.In addition to its efforts in Japan, Ares Management raised an additional $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy in the first quarter of 2026. This capital influx is largely attributed to commitments made to its seventh special situations fund, SSG Fund VII. The latest fundraising comes on the heels of a $400 million close in 2025 and follows a successful $2.4 billion raise by the predecessor vehicle just two years prior.Notable returning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System and the New York State Common Retirement Fund, which committed $300 million and $200 million, respectively. DealStreetAsia previously reported that the International Finance Corporation (IFC) is considering a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account, which shares a mandate similar to Ares’ SSG series. This fund concentrates on acquiring non-performing loans and single-asset distress situations across several Southeast Asian markets.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT REPORTS STRONG Q1 2026 DIRECT LENDING ACTIVITY

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) has reported a significant uptick in its U.S. direct lending activities, closing approximately $9.5 billion across 70 transactions during the first quarter of 2026. This announcement, made on the 4th of May 2026, highlights Ares Management's strong position in the lending market, which has been bolstered by an increase in demand for flexible capital solutions in various sectors.Over the 12 months leading up to March 31, 2026, Ares Management closed approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions, underscoring its robust performance in the direct lending space. The firm has established itself as a key player by providing capital to businesses seeking growth and expansion, which is evident from the diverse range of transactions it has facilitated in recent months.Among the noteworthy deals closed by Ares Management in the first quarter are senior secured credit facilities supporting various growth initiatives. For instance, Ares acted as the administrative agent for a credit facility aiding Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, a company specializing in fire and life safety solutions. Additionally, Ares was instrumental in Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, a provider of critical electrical and mechanical services across multiple industries.Further emphasizing its pivotal role in facilitating business growth, Ares Management also served as the lead arranger for a credit facility supporting ARCHIMED's plans for the North American Science Association, a leading contract research organization dedicated to the development of medical devices. Such transactions reflect Ares Management's commitment to supporting vital sectors that contribute to economic development.As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management's global platform boasts over $644 billion in assets under management, with operations spanning North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East. This diverse portfolio positions Ares Management as a leading global alternative investment manager, dedicated to delivering consistent and attractive returns for its investors while contributing positively to the communities in which it operates.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH WHITESTONE REIT ACQUISITION

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has announced an agreement to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at approximately US$1.7 billion, effectively taking the retail-focused real estate investment trust private. This strategic move is aimed at expanding Ares' portfolio in necessity-based retail centers located in rapidly growing metropolitan areas across the United States.The announcement, made on 14th April 2026, also included significant leadership changes within Ares Management's Asia operations. E.G. Morse has been appointed as Partner and Head of Asia Credit, while Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong will lead the Asia Special Situations team. These appointments reflect Ares' commitment to enhancing its credit and special situations strategies in the region, aligning with the company's broader growth objectives.The acquisition of Whitestone REIT is particularly noteworthy as it allows Ares Management to bolster its position in the retail real estate sector, catering to essential consumer needs. Investors monitoring NYSE:ARES should consider how this acquisition fits into Ares' overall strategy, which increasingly emphasizes private credit, real assets, and special situations. The move underscores Ares' intent to diversify its capital allocation across various geographies and asset types.Key considerations for investors include the integration of Whitestone into Ares’ existing operational framework, as well as the execution of fundraising initiatives in Asia. The new leadership team is expected to play a critical role in determining how capital is deployed and managed across regions. Nonetheless, investors should be mindful of potential risks, including profit margin compression, high levels of debt, and the current dividend yield of 5.38%, which is not fully supported by earnings.As Ares Management embarks on this significant acquisition and leadership restructuring, the implications for its future growth trajectory will be closely watched. The market's response to these developments could provide insights into how effectively Ares can leverage its new assets and leadership to enhance shareholder value over time.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES CARGO SERVICES TO IRAQ WITH ENHANCED CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, according to its latest published schedule, marking the return of cargo connectivity between Doha and Baghdad. This development follows the airline's phased restoration of services, with cargo sales now open for shipments on the Doha to Baghdad route.Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will introduce a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad. This will be complemented by the launch of twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route starting from 10th May, thereby adding belly-hold cargo capacity alongside dedicated freighter operations. With both freighter and passenger services in place, Qatar Airways will offer a combined cargo capacity of over 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad beginning 10th May.The new addition is expected to significantly enhance cargo flows between the two markets, leveraging a mix of scheduled freighter uplift and passenger aircraft capacity. Qatar Airways Cargo reported that its freighter network has expanded to more than 60 destinations as of 1st May. The passenger network is also set to grow further, reaching over 150 destinations from 16th June as the airline continues to scale up operations.All cargo products are now available for booking through standard sales channels, including the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams. The airline confirmed that capacity is open for customer bookings, and those with shipment-specific inquiries are encouraged to reach out to cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or their local sales representatives.The resumption of services to Baghdad forms part of Qatar Airways Cargo ’s broader network strategy, which combines freighter operations with passenger aircraft capacity to support global cargo demand. The airline operates a fleet of 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, supported by a road feeder network, while continuing to invest in digitalization, products, and infrastructure as part of its operational strategy.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS MARKS RETURN TO ERBIL WITH RESUMPTION OF FLIGHTS

Qatar Airways will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the return of direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a wider reinstatement of its Iraq operations, the airline announced Saturday.The Doha-based carrier stated that it will restart services to three Iraqi cities — Erbil, Baghdad (BGW), and Basra (BSR) — in a phased return aimed at rebuilding its regional network and restoring connectivity across key Middle Eastern destinations.In parallel with passenger services, Qatar Airways will also resume freighter operations to Baghdad starting 7th May, reinforcing cargo and trade links with Iraq. The airline noted that these resumptions follow recent reinstatements of flights to several regional destinations, including Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus, and Kozhikode, as part of a wider strategy to restore its global network.By mid-June 2026, Qatar Airways expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide. Passengers traveling through Erbil International Airport (EBL) will gain renewed access to Qatar Airways’ global network via its Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, which the airline describes as a key connector linking six continents.The carrier, which has been repeatedly recognized as the 'World’s Best Airline' by Skytrax, emphasized that its return to Iraq reflects ongoing efforts to restore stability to its route network while maintaining flexibility in response to regional conditions. The resumption of passenger flights to Erbil follows the suspension of services in late February 2026 due to the outbreak of the US–Israel–Iran war, which triggered widespread airspace closures across Iraq and the wider Middle East.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS FLIGHT NETWORK, BOOSTING AUSTRALIA'S TOURISM POTENTIAL

Australia’s tourism industry is set to receive a major boost as Qatar Airways announces significant increases in its flights between Doha and key Australian cities. As of 5th May 2026, the expanded services come at a crucial time for the Australian travel market, helping to reconnect Australia with major international tourism sources in Europe, Asia, and the Middle East. With the airline resuming daily flights and adding more options for travelers, this move is expected to foster a strong recovery for Australia’s tourism sector after a period of disruption.Beginning in May 2026, Qatar Airways will reinstate daily flights from Doha to Sydney, Melbourne, and Perth. These routes, which were previously limited to around five flights a week, will now operate daily, ensuring better accessibility for international travelers looking to explore Australia’s top destinations. Additionally, the airline will launch four weekly flights to Brisbane starting 16th May, which will increase to daily flights by 16th June 2026, giving more tourists the opportunity to visit Queensland’s attractions.This flight expansion reflects Qatar Airways’ ongoing commitment to strengthening global connectivity, and the airline is capitalizing on its strategic hub in Doha. By offering these additional flights, Qatar Airways makes it easier for travelers from Europe, the Middle East, and Asia to plan direct routes to Australia, supporting the country’s tourism recovery post-pandemic. The increased connectivity will not only enhance tourism but also improve trade and business opportunities between the two nations.Australia’s travel industry stands to benefit significantly from this expansion. The extra flights are expected to provide an influx of international visitors, boosting demand for accommodations, tours, and local attractions. Major cities like Sydney, Melbourne, and Perth, which already attract millions of tourists annually, are likely to see a further rise in visitors, especially as the summer months approach. For Australia’s tourism bodies, the expanded flights mean more opportunities to market the country as an accessible, diverse, and attractive destination.However, it’s essential to acknowledge that travel advisories continue to affect certain routes. According to Australia’s Smart Traveller service, there remains an active “Do Not Travel” advisory for Qatar due to ongoing regional security concerns. This caution applies to all travelers, even those transiting through Doha’s Hamad International Airport. Despite these advisories, the increased flight frequencies demonstrate Qatar Airways’ confidence in the safety and operational efficiency of its services. Looking ahead, the partnership between Qatar Airways and Australia is expected to flourish, positioning the tourism sector for long-term growth.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-R103-SD9Y-N29S-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - PEER SECTOR NEWS UPDATES2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - PEER SECTOR NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART SECURES $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW PACT WITH FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP

Upstart Holdings, an AI lending marketplace, announced a forward-flow commitment from funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. Fortress will purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through the Upstart platform over 15 months. The revelation came on 5th May 2026.The arrangement deepens a partnership that began in 2025. It underscores Upstart’s push to diversify its capital base, now drawing on more than 100 banks and credit unions that use its AI-driven models and cloud applications. Upstart’s platform supports a range of products, including personal loans, automotive loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit option.

+ FORTRESS-LED FINANCING INJECTS OVER USD 180 MILLION INTO PURE SALMON JAPAN’S LAND-BASED SALMON PROJECT

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is earmarked to support the construction of Pure Salmon Japan’s land-based farming facility and to help meet rising domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores institutional backing for the project, which envisions a facility capable of producing up to 10,000 tonnes and potentially becoming Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. It relies on advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to bolster food security by reducing import dependency.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding presence in Japan, brings capital and expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, described the initiative as a meaningful step for strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS SENIOR HOUSING PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. The deal, completed on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by Fortress affiliates, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018 on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, the property serves a growing, affluent senior population along Florida's northeast coast. Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE: KKR) DIVESTS AXIUS WATER TO CRH WITH XPV WATER PARTNERS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and XPV Water Partners divest Axius Water to CRH, the global building materials group. The deal was confirmed on 6th May 2026 at 18:26:48. The transaction aims to maximize value for stakeholders while positioning CRH to expand its water management capabilitiesAxius Water owns advanced water technologies and solutions that align with CRH's sustainability goals. KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and XPV Water Partners supported a smooth transition to ensure continued growth for Axius Water under CRH's stewardship

+ VERTIS INFRASTRUCTURE TRUST (NYSE:VIT) PLANS IPO FILING FOR UP TO ₹3,000 CRORE

Vertis Infrastructure Trust (NYSE:VIT) plans to file draft papers for an initial public offering by end May 2026, aiming to raise up to ₹3,000 crore through a mix of primary and secondary share sales. The InvIT operates a portfolio of toll and annuity road projects across India, developed primarily through acquisitions by KKR's Asian infrastructure fund which launched the platform in 2021 and expanded in 2024-25 with the acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The trust currently manages 28 road projects across nine states, valued at about ₹26,500 crore.Market observers note that the target size could be adjusted downward given limited needs for fresh primary capital, with the final amount under discussion amid a cautious InvIT fundraising environment. The broader backdrop shows appetite for infrastructure InvITs as investors seek stable, long-term yields amid volatility in other asset classes. The InvIT sector has seen ongoing momentum, underscored by large issues and continued demand in the space.Industry momentum remains evident as the National Highways Authority of India has raised over ₹9,000 crore through two InvIT offerings in March, and Citius Transnet Investment Trust raised ₹1,105 crore in April, signaling strong demand despite challenging market conditions. Vertis Infrastructure Trust aims to capitalize on this momentum while maintaining a measured approach to capital raising

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) ALLOCATES $10 BILLION FOR AI INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) allocates $10 billion for the development of AI-focused power plants and data centers, signaling a rise in U.S. capital aimed at strengthening AI infrastructure.On 3rd May 2026, the investment sits within a broader trend in which Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta are collectively investing over $100 billion worldwide in similar AI infrastructure improvements.The program aims to secure long-term power agreements, strengthening KKR's position in power generation as demand from AI data centers grows. Observers note that Canada and Germany are consolidating their efforts to counterbalance U.S.–China AI influence, and KKR's investment could shape the landscape. Market interpretations link the move to Google's AI plans, with some analysts predicting the stock could reach $310

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) ACQUIRES MAJORITY STAKE IN KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO EXPAND HEALTHCARE PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, expanding its healthcare portfolio. The terms of the deal are not disclosed in a release dated May 4, 2026. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the new RCM platform, reinforcing Carlyle's strategy to blend Knack's analytics with Rao's global operations to enhance advisory servicesKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM serve physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals, and the partnership targets challenging elements of the revenue cycle such as rural cost reports and intricate anesthesia cases, according to Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle views the acquisition as a step to broaden its footprint in the RCM sector and to pursue additional opportunities within healthcare management

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) INTEGRATES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO BUILD UNIFIED HEALTHCARE TECHNOLOGY PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is integrating two healthcare service providers, Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, into a single platform focused on advanced digital capabilities in healthcare services. The move reflects a broader shift toward automation and data-driven systems within the U.S. healthcare landscape. The combined platform is expected to support physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers with tailored operational assistance.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ELEVATES THREE EXECUTIVES TO CO-PRESIDENTS TO STRENGTHEN LEADERSHIP

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective from 22nd April 2026. The move aims to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as Carlyle refines its strategy in a challenging economic environment.The three co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and operational management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s franchises, positioning the firm to respond more quickly to market dynamics. By concentrating authority around Schwartz, Carlyle Group seeks greater execution discipline and improved performance in the coming quarters.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS SECURES $300 MILLION SENIOR LOAN FROM OAKTREE CAPITAL TO RESTRUCTURE FINANCING

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility with Oaktree Capital Management, a move that is set to reshape the company's capital structure. The financing refinances existing debt through 2031 and reserves US$35 million for growth initiatives, with the deal completed on 3rd May 2026.The loan features a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure, flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets, strengthening the company's funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as it pursues expansion.Management projects 2026 revenue of US$264-266 million, implying growth of roughly 25-26% year over year. The loan alleviates refinancing pressure and provides liquidity to address losses and cash burn without requiring an immediate equity raise, though investors should weigh ongoing cash burn and negative free cash flow. Some analysts project about 30% annual revenue growth with earnings around US$73.5 million by 2029, a path that could support or temper profitability expectations.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK THROUGH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks a milestone in the company's approach to managing its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026 and includes both amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and it introduced provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, granting lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, underscoring the company's financing approach. In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel committed to filing registration statements with the SEC to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This step aims to support liquidity and transparency for investors, with lenders indicating they will hold the securities for investment purposes only. The arrangements could lead to dilution if warrants are exercised and may increase the share count once registered for resale. BioXcel notes it is not an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting requirements; the registration rights agreement, involving several Oaktree entities among other holders, signals continued adherence to securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor forthcoming filings and announcements, as these developments could influence BioXcel's financial position and market performance.

+ OAKTREE AND NORTH MILL CLOSE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization with North Mill Equipment Finance, known as OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026. The deal underscores investor appetite for high-quality equipment finance assets.The securitization pools assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal finalized in November 2025. The offering was rated from AAA to single A and drew peak subscriptions of about 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack with a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%. Truist Securities acted as lead structuring agent and Deutsche Bank served as co-manager.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC SECURES $1.25 BILLION INVESTMENT FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT (NYSE:APO)

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC has secured a $1.25 billion investment from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc. The deal grants Apollo a 13% stake and was formalized on April 20, 2026 through the issuance of convertible preferred shares, subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The funding values Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC at a post-money valuation of approximately $9.62 billion and aims to strengthen operations and expand market presence. Apollo Global Management, Inc., via Apollo Funds, brings strategic backing to the company as demand for surgical solutions grows, with analysts watching how the partnership could influence growth and shareholder value as the transaction proceeds through regulatory review

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) INVESTS $1.25 BILLION IN MCKESSON CORPORATION (NYSE:MCK) FOR 13% STAKE IN MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS DIVISION

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The investment supports McKesson's plan to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division and is expected to bolster operational efficiencies across the group. McKesson intends to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division, and the capital from Apollo is expected to bolster the strategic plan and fund initiatives across the business. The investment signals a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the company pursues the separation and optimization of its core operations. Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating investors anticipate growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will monitor the deal as it unfolds and its impact on McKesson's separation plans.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT EXPANDS UK PRESENCE WITH GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP ACQUISITION

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a deal completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed. The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans. GLG is a national leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired over 5,000 homes. Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios, enabling GLG to leverage both Apollo capital and third-party partners to accelerate development of high-quality rental housing. Gatehouse Bank's divestiture will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) POSTS $9.5 BILLION IN U.S. DIRECT LENDING ACROSS 70 TRANSACTIONS IN Q1 2026

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update dated 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors and highlights the breadth of the firm's lending platform.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, the firm Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, led Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's plans for the North American Science Association. As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management L.P.'s global platform Totals more than $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) POSTS $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS DEVELOPMENT FUND AMID INVESTOR DEMAND

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund in early 2026 as investor demand remains robust for Japanese logistics assets. The first close is expected to occur in spring, according to chief executive Michael Arougheti on an earnings call

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN US$1.7 BILLION TRANSACTION

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at approximately US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding the firm’s footprint in necessity-based retail centers across rapidly growing metropolitan areas in the United States. The deal supports Ares' strategy to diversify across geographies and asset types and align with its emphasis on private credit, real assets, and special situations. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares Management named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, while Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong will lead the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring the firm’s focus on strengthening credit and special situations capabilities in Asia. The Whitestone purchase broadens Ares’ exposure to essential retail real estate and supports its strategy to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types. Investors should heed risks including profit margin compression, high debt levels, and a dividend yield of 5.38% that is not fully supported by earnings

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESUMES BAGHDAD SERVICE WITH EXPANDED CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, reestablishing the Doha to Baghdad cargo link. Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will introduce a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad, complemented by twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route from 10th May, thereby adding belly-hold capacity alongside dedicated freighter operations. The combined cargo capacity from Doha to Baghdad will exceed 115 tonnes from 10th May.The resumption reflects a broader network strategy that blends freighter uplift with passenger capacity to meet growing global demand. The airline's freighter network has expanded to more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while its passenger network is set to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June as operations scale up. All cargo products are now bookable through standard channels, including the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, with shipment inquiries directed to cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or their local representatives. Qatar Airways notes its fleet comprises 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, underpinned by a road feeder network and ongoing investments in digitalization, products, and infrastructure.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS REINSTATES ERBIL FLIGHTS AS IRAQ NETWORK REOPENS

Qatar Airways said it will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the carrier 's return to direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a broader reinstatement of its Iraq operations. The plan calls for restarting passenger services to three Iraqi cities - Erbil, Baghdad (BGW) and Basra (BSR) - in a phased rollout designed to rebuild the regional network and reconnect key Middle Eastern destinations.In parallel, the airline will restart freighter operations to Baghdad starting 7th May, reinforcing cargo links with Iraq as part of the wider network restoration. These moves follow earlier resumptions to Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus and Kozhikode as the carrier expands its global reach.By mid-June 2026, Qatar Airways expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide. Passengers traveling through Erbil International Airport (EBL) will again access the airline's global network via Hamad International Airport in Doha, described as a key connector linking six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES KEY ROUTES AS REGIONAL RECOVERY GAINS MOMENTUM

Qatar Airways is resuming its key flight routes across the Middle East, Asia, and Europe as it rebuilds operations after the West Asia crisis. As of 5th May 2026, the carrier has announced the reinstatement of services to Dubai and Sharjah, signaling progress toward normalizing regional air travel.The airline also confirms the return of daily services to Bahrain (BAH) and Kozhikode (CCJ) starting 1st May 2026, with a broader plan to relaunch more than 150 destinations beginning 16th June 2026. The updated schedule runs through 15th September 2026 as part of the carrier's strategy to adapt to evolving travel demand.Qatar Airways says passengers should monitor its website or app for updates and ensure contact information is current. Confirmed travelers will receive direct notifications if any flights change, underscoring the airline's focus on customer service. The move aligns with a wider aviation sector recovery as demand climbs in the wake of the crisis.

urn:contentItem:6JHY-X4Y3-RS7B-H3N5-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXTENDS FORWARD-FLOW COMMITMENT WITH UPSTART AT $1.25 BILLION

Upstart Holdings said Fortress Investment Group will purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through its platform over 15 months, a forward-flow commitment disclosed on 5th May 2026.The deal expands a partnership that began in 2025 and comes as Upstart continues to broaden its network of capital partners, now counting more than 100 banks and credit unions using its AI-driven models and cloud applications. The Upstart platform offers personal loans, auto financing, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product.Executives framed the arrangement as a scalable opportunity for both firms: a stronger funding base for Upstart and access to differentiated consumer credit opportunities for Fortress, with Upstart’s data-driven platform enabling efficient, scalable origination and the potential for durable returns across market conditions. The collaboration signals a wider trend of technology-enhanced consumer credit solutions.

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE: KKR) REPORTS SALE OF AXIUS WATER TO CRH WITH XPV WATER PARTNERS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and XPV Water Partners have agreed to divest Axius Water to CRH, the global building materials group. The deal was confirmed on 6th May 2026 at 18:26:48. The transaction highlights a move to reallocate focus toward higher growth areasAxius Water brings advanced water technologies and solutions that support CRH's sustainability agenda and expand its water solutions portfolioNo financial terms have been disclosed, but the transaction marks a milestone for all parties involved as CRH gains access to Axius Water's capabilities under new ownership

+ VERTIS INFRASTRUCTURE TRUST (NYSE:VIT) PURSUES DRAFT PAPERS FOR ₹3,000 CRORE INVIT IPO BY END MAY

Vertis Infrastructure Trust (NYSE:VIT) plans to file draft papers for an initial public offering by end May 2026, targeting a raise of up to ₹3,000 crore through a mix of primary and secondary share sales. The InvIT operates a portfolio of toll and annuity road projects across India, largely assembled by KKR's Asian infrastructure fund since 2021, with growth in 2024-25 through the acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The portfolio comprises 28 road projects across nine states, valued at ₹26,500 crore.The final size of the offer remains under discussion, as management weighs limited requirements for fresh primary capital against a competitive fundraising environment for InvITs. Industry observers note that the market for infrastructure InvITs continues to attract institutional investors seeking stable, long-term yields, even as macro headwinds persist.Momentum in the sector is evident, with NHAI raising over ₹9,000 crore through two InvIT offerings in March and Citius Transnet Investment Trust raising ₹1,105 crore in April. Vertis Infrastructure Trust seeks to participate in this ongoing cycle with a disciplined approach to capital formation

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) COMMITS $10 BILLION TO AI POWER AND DATA CENTER EXPANSION

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) allocates $10 billion to AI power plants and data centers, expanding the firm’s footprint in AI infrastructure.On 3rd May 2026, this move aligns with a broader trend where Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta are investing over $100 billion worldwide in AI infrastructure.The investment seeks long-term power agreements to secure capacity as demand from AI data centers grows.Geopolitical dynamics are noted, with Canada and Germany coordinating to counterbalance U.S.–China AI influence, and Google's prospects are cited by market analysts as potentially supporting a stock price near $310.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) ACQUIRES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO BUILD INTEGRATED RCM PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, broadening its healthcare portfolio. The May 4, 2026 release notes the transaction includes a majority stake in both firms, with terms undisclosed. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the platform to support ongoing developmentKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM serve physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals, and the partnership aims to address rural cost reports and intricate anesthesia cases, according to Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle views this as a step to deepen its presence in the RCM sector and to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementThe deal underscores Carlyle's focus on technology-enabled revenue cycle management and the potential to deliver enhanced, analytics-driven solutions to providers

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ADVANCES REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT THROUGH INTEGRATED HEALTHCARE PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is combining Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a single platform focused on advanced digital capabilities in healthcare services. The integrated platform targets physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers, aiming to address administrative complexity and evolving care models. Knack supplies a globally distributed operational framework while Equalize adds predictive analytics and process optimization tools to improve revenue cycle management, billing, and payer coordination. The move reflects a broader trend toward technology-driven healthcare services as automation and data-driven systems become central to clinical administration.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) APPOINTS TRIO AS CO-PRESIDENTS TO DRIVE UNIFIED DECISION-MAKING

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) appoints three senior executives as co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective after 22nd April 2026, to accelerate decision-making and align operations with its evolving strategy.The co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and day-to-day management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s businesses, with the goal of improving execution as market conditions shift.Schwartz, who assumed leadership during a transitional period, seeks clearer accountability and faster decisions, a model that aligns with practices at other major firms to maintain momentum.The firm will assess the impact on investment activity and financial performance in forthcoming quarters.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS INKS $300 MILLION TERM LOAN WITH OAKTREE TO BACK MOTIVA PLATFORM EXPANSION

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility from Oaktree Capital Management, completed on 3rd May 2026, with refinancing through 2031 and US$35 million reserved for growth.The loan offers fixed-rate, no-amortization terms and a first-priority lien on assets, designed to boost funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues expansion.Analysts view Motiva as a driver of global adoption for its implants and minimally invasive procedures. Management projects 2026 revenue of US$264-266 million, up about 25-26% year over year. The facility provides liquidity to address losses and cash burn without forcing an immediate equity raise, though ongoing cash burn remains a risk.Some watchers project roughly 30% revenue growth with earnings around US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could underpin optimism about growth while highlighting the path to profitability.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

+ OAKTREE, NORTH MILL EXECUTE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization, OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026, reflecting healthy investor demand for quality equipment finance assets.The securitized assets originated from Midland Equipment Finance in a November 2025 deal. The notes were rated AAA through A and drew strong demand, about 6.3 times subscriptions, while delivering a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a 4.7% yield.Truist Securities led structuring with Deutsche Bank as co-manager. Brendan Beer of Oaktree noted the market reception as evidence of investor confidence in the strategy and in North Mill's role.North Mill's David Lee emphasized asset quality and the servicing platform, reiterating that NMEF will continue as the primary servicer for the assets, ensuring ongoing management.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC AT $9.62 BILLION POST-MONEY VALUATION WITH $1.25 BILLION FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC secures $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., delivering a 13% stake via convertible preferred shares. The investment was formalized on April 20, 2026 and is contingent on regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The post-money valuation stands at about $9.62 billion, and the capital will be used to bolster operations and expand the company’s market footprint as Apollo provides strategic backing through its funds.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY INVESTMENT FOR 13% MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS STAKE

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The capital supports McKesson's planned separation of the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and funds strategic initiatives within the business. The transaction reflects a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the latter moves toward separating its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and concentrating on core operations. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating market expectations for continued growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will watch the arrangement for potential impacts on McKesson's separation plan.

+ APOLLO BROADENS UK HOUSING FOOTPRINT VIA GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP PURCHASE

Apollo Global Management Inc. has acquired Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, completed on 1st April 2026, bringing Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties into its UK housing platform.Financial terms were not disclosed.GLG is a Build-to-Rent single-family housing leader with more than 10,000 properties and 5,000 homes acquired, and will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell with Ascend Properties handling external portfolio management.The deal strengthens Apollo's UK strategy and follows its existing interests in Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans, while Gatehouse Bank focuses on Shariah-compliant financing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) FACILITATES GROWTH THROUGH DIRECT LENDING WITH ENCORE FIRE PROTECTION LOENBRO AND ARCHIMED

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update dated 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors. The firm also highlights notable Q1 activity.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, Ares Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions, underscoring the breadth of its direct lending platform. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, led Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's credit facility backing the North American Science Association.As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management L.P.'s global platform Totals over $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) SECURES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS DEVELOPMENT FUND WITH BROAD INVESTMENT MOMENTUM

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, signaling continued investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The first close is expected in spring, as noted during an earnings call by chief executive Michael AroughetiThe fund follows more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalThe firm’s broader capital activity includes Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets including a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, Ares' co-president, said the fundraising aims to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares also raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, supported by commitments to SSG Fund VIINotable returning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is considering a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account with a mandate aligned to the SSG series, targeting non performing loans and single-asset distress across Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) APPOINTS ASIA LEADERS AND ACQUIRES WHITESTONE REIT TO DIVERSIFY REAL ASSETS

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a US$1.7 billion transaction, taking private a retail-focused REIT and expanding essential consumer-focused centers across U.S. metros. On Apr. 14, 2026, E.G. Morse was named Partner and Head of Asia Credit, with Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong leading the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring Ares' intent to bolster Asia-focused credit strategies. The Whitestone acquisition broadens Ares' real estate, private credit, and real assets exposure and supports diversification across geographies. Investors should monitor integration progress and Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape, while considering risks such as margin pressure and elevated debt; the 5.38% yield is not fully covered by earnings

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO EXPANDS IRAQ CORRIDOR WITH FREIGHTER AND BELLY-HOLD CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freight services to Iraq, restoring the Doha to Baghdad link with both freighter and belly-hold capacity. Effective 7th May 2026, a weekly Boeing 777 freighter will serve Baghdad, complemented by twice-weekly passenger flights on the route from 10th May to provide additional belly-hold capacity.Together, these services will deliver a combined cargo capacity of more than 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad from 10th May.Qatar Airways Cargo notes the broader expansion of its network: the freighter network has grown to more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while the passenger network is scheduled to reach over 150 destinations from 16th June as operations scale up. Bookings are available through the Digital Lounge and local sales teams; inquiries can be directed to cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa

+ QATAR AIRWAYS BRINGS BACK ERBIL SERVICE TO BOOST REGIONAL CONNECTIVITY

Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of passenger flights to Erbil on 10th May 2026, as part of its broader return to Iraq. The carrier will restart flights to Erbil, Baghdad and Basra in a phased rollout to rebuild the regional network.Freighter operations to Baghdad will resume on 7th May, complementing passenger services and boosting cargo ties. By continuing to restore routes, the airline is expanding its footprint across the Middle East as conditions allow.By mid-June 2026, the airline aims to serve more than 150 destinations, with Erbil travellers able to access the Doha hub via Hamad International Airport and reach six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS NETWORK AMID POST-CRISIS REBOUND, TARGETS OVER 150 DESTINATIONS

Qatar Airways is advancing a targeted restart of its network, resuming routes across the Middle East, Asia, and Europe as it rebuilds after the West Asia crisis. The carrier confirmed the reintroduction of services to Dubai and Sharjah as of 5th May 2026, a step toward restoring regional air traffic.The company will also bring back daily services to Bahrain (BAH) and Kozhikode (CCJ) from 1st May 2026, while a broader rollout to more than 150 destinations is planned to begin on 16th June 2026 and run through 15th September 2026. This staged approach aligns with shifting travel demand.Travelers are advised to check Qatar Airways ' website or app for updates and to ensure their contact details are current, with affected passengers receiving direct notifications about any changes. The resumption signals a broader recovery trend in air travel and a renewed emphasis on connectivity.

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-YGX3-RV2R-V03M-00000-00WEEKLY REVIEW: OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:36ZLexisNexisWEEKLY REVIEW: OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP WIND-WIN: OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION MAKES NEARLY EUR600 MILLION EUROPEAN WIND PUSH

London, 6th May 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's largest specialist renewables investors, is making a major Europe-wide push into the wind energy market, acquiring 321 MW onshore wind farms across 17 sites.

Its fund management team has invested EUR584 million in wind farms in France, Germany and Poland, on behalf of the Sky fund (ORI SCSp) it manages, accelerating homegrown power across key European markets. These wind farms will generate enough clean power for over 250,000 homes.

Octopus now manages over 400 large-scale renewables projects across the globe, as it continues to scale cheap, green power at lightning speed. With these latest deals, it manages 67 onshore wind farms in Europe, including the UK, France, Germany, Poland, Ireland, Sweden and Finland.

Source: Company Website

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHN-37K3-SCCD-F1F3-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION LAUNCHES GRANTS-DRIVEN INITIATIVE TO MITIGATE AUTOMATION'S JOB DISPLACEMENT

On 28th April 2026, the Rockefeller Foundation outlined a bold push to counter AI-driven workforce disruption with a grant-based national initiative designed to prevent displacement and shift workers into AI-complementary roles. The Foundation emphasizes interventions for workers rather than product development, centering on program design, metrics and evaluation to track displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions caused by automation. Funding will flow through grants, programmatic pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments; decisions during implementation will matter for whether the effort yields scalable reskilling pipelines or fragmented pilots without lasting impact. As a strategic philanthropic signal, the move is not regulatory or corporate and aims to catalyze innovative retraining models and strengthened public-private collaboration, aligning with broader policy debates on universal basic income, sectoral training and apprenticeships. Stakeholders expect a growing emphasis on measurable, data-driven evaluations within skills and labor analytics, with close attention to funding priorities and partnership dynamics, including durable credentialing and employer engagement, as well as placement metrics.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES MULTI-YEAR INITIATIVE TO CREATE 1.6 MILLION JOBS ACROSS DISTRESSED COMMUNITIES

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, drawing more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business in Baltimore to discuss strategies to revitalize the economy and expand opportunity across communities.In a centerpiece announcement, the Foundation unveiled a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, targeting roughly 1.6 million additional jobs across growth sectors such as healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, the Foundation's President, underscored the promise of restoring a stable life through hard work and resilience for American workers.The Foundation also allocated $12 million to Invest in Our Future to mobilize clean energy opportunities and drive growth in underserved areas. Maryland Governor Wes Moore participated and announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards to advance education and childcare in neighborhoods with high childhood poverty. The gathering also introduced the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and showcased cross-sector partnerships with the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next Big Bets for America gathering is scheduled to take place in Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ DATA SILOS HAMPER AID, ROCKEFELLER OFFICIAL SAYS, URGING CROSS-SECTOR INTEGRATION

On April 21st, 2026, at the One Health Summit, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, addressed a panel titled "Philanthropies for One Health, One Health for Children" and pressed for a unified data platform. He argued that, despite unprecedented data, information remains trapped in silos across sectors, hindering decision-making and resource allocation, and he warned, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." Winter noted that the broader push for data integration mirrors earlier calls by Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025, who urged consolidating health data across nations into a single platform amidst about 3,000 fragmented databases. To demonstrate potential benefits, he pointed to Somalia’s drought crisis, where Rockefeller support is funding a local tech incubator to build a dashboard that unites health, nutrition, climate and food data to guide local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes around $2 billion annually. He closed by saying empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, merged with local information, could yield more effective and cost-efficient humanitarian responses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION AWARDED BY NHRC FOR COMMITMENT TO NIGERIA'S HUMAN RIGHTS AGENDA

On Apr. 28, 2026, in Abuja, the National Human Rights Commission honored the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its longstanding support of human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony drew senior NHRC officials and directors, and the honor was accepted by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.NHRC Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu praised the Foundation's steady backing, especially for initiatives that broaden access to justice and strengthen the Commission's capacity to protect rights. He stressed that Nigeria's human rights landscape is broad and complex, and that durable progress depends on ongoing partnerships with development partners rather than isolated action.Aniagolu-Okoye described the recognition as meaningful and encouraging, and reiterated the Foundation's commitment to equipping citizens with knowledge of their rights and support to pursue justice when those rights are violated. She warned that awareness alone is not enough without effective enforcement mechanisms and highlighted the Foundation's role in fostering stronger enforcement through collaboration and capacity building.The event also underscored the value of continued cooperation as the country confronts evolving human rights challenges. Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, expressed gratitude for the Ford Foundation's enduring partnership, citing the MOVE project and the Commission's dashboard system as notable advances that benefited from the Foundation's early support.

+ WEST AFRICA FIRST LADIES CALLED TO LEAD ON GENDER-BASED VIOLENCE, FORD FOUNDATION SAYS

At the ECOWAS First Ladies Forum on Zero Tolerance for GBV in Banjul on 24th April 2026, the Ford Foundation urged First Ladies across West Africa and the region to leverage their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence, framing the First Ladies' soft power as a catalyst for social change and stronger accountability.Aniagolu noted that despite existing frameworks and commitments, GBV remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with more than 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18.The Foundation highlighted the economic cost of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, a drain that rivals investments in key sectors such as education.The Ford Foundation stressed that First Ladies are not formal policymakers but can shape public discourse and influence decision-making across the continent. It cited Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as examples, noting that instruments like the Better Life for Rural Women Programme and Hands Off Our Girls campaign elevated women’s issues and contributed to national actions, underscoring the potential of soft power to drive systemic change.The Foundation has continued to invest in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, partnering with First Ladies and governors’ spouses in Nigeria to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based response systems, and address harmful social norms. Aniagolu argued that laws and policies alone are not enough without public trust and accessible services, pointing to persistent silence among survivors fueled by stigma, fear, and weak institutional confidence.

+ FORD FOUNDATION ALLOCATES $60 MILLION TO PROTECT DEMOCRACY AND RULE OF LAW

The Ford Foundation on April 14, 2026 announced a new $60 million grants program aimed at strengthening democratic processes nationwide. The grants will go to nonpartisan, nonprofit organizations dedicated to protecting the rule of law and voting rights. Recipients span the ideological spectrum, including Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. This initiative builds on a decade-long Civic Engagement and Government program, which has delivered more than $1 billion in grants over its history. President Heather Gerken described the effort as a proactive step to bolster democracy, stating, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment." Campaign Legal Center founder Trevor Potter emphasized the importance of voting access, saying, "Ensuring every American has access to voting is paramount for the health of the democracy." Pillars of the Community is co-chaired by Ben Ginsberg and Bob Bauer, representing a bipartisan push to uphold electoral integrity. The Ford Foundation carries a $16 billion endowment and has operated for 90 years.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHN-94N3-S060-316S-00000-00INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisINVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIMNEW YORK, May 7, 2026

PR NewswireNEW YORK, May 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Pomerantz LLP is investigating claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation ("Primoris" or the "Company") (NYSE: PRIM). Such investors are advised to contact Danielle Peyton at or 646-581-9980, ext. 7980.

The investigation concerns whether Primoris and certain of its officers and/or directors have engaged in securities fraud or other unlawful business practices.[Click here for information about joining the class action]On May 5, 2026, Primoris issued a press release reporting its financial results for the first quarter of 2026. Primoris reported results below analyst expectations and slashed full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million. Primoris attributed the reduction to lower renewable energy activity, delayed project starts, and increased costs on renewable energy projects. On this news, Primoris's stock price fell $101.69 per share, or 50.11%, to close at $101.23 per share on May 6, 2026.Pomerantz LLP, with offices in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, London, Paris, and Tel Aviv, is acknowledged as one of the premier firms in the areas of corporate, securities, and antitrust class litigation. Founded by the late Abraham L. Pomerantz, known as the dean of the class action bar, Pomerantz pioneered the field of securities class actions. Today, more than 85 years later, Pomerantz continues in the tradition he established, fighting for the rights of the victims of securities fraud, breaches of fiduciary duty, and corporate misconduct. The Firm has recovered numerous multimillion-dollar damages awards on behalf of class members. Seewww.pomlaw.com.Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.CONTACT:Danielle Peyton
Pomerantz LLP
