Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHF-8HY3-S51B-P1J4-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:36ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION LAUNCHES GRANTS-DRIVEN INITIATIVE TO MITIGATE AUTOMATION'S JOB DISPLACEMENT

On 28th April 2026, the Rockefeller Foundation outlined a bold push to counter AI-driven workforce disruption with a grant-based national initiative designed to prevent displacement and shift workers into AI-complementary roles. The Foundation emphasizes interventions for workers rather than product development, centering on program design, metrics and evaluation to track displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions caused by automation. Funding will flow through grants, programmatic pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments; decisions during implementation will matter for whether the effort yields scalable reskilling pipelines or fragmented pilots without lasting impact. As a strategic philanthropic signal, the move is not regulatory or corporate and aims to catalyze innovative retraining models and strengthened public-private collaboration, aligning with broader policy debates on universal basic income, sectoral training and apprenticeships. Stakeholders expect a growing emphasis on measurable, data-driven evaluations within skills and labor analytics, with close attention to funding priorities and partnership dynamics, including durable credentialing and employer engagement, as well as placement metrics.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES MULTI-YEAR INITIATIVE TO CREATE 1.6 MILLION JOBS ACROSS DISTRESSED COMMUNITIES

The Rockefeller Foundation hosted the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event on 21st April 2026, drawing more than 250 leaders from policy, philanthropy and business in Baltimore to discuss strategies to revitalize the economy and expand opportunity across communities.In a centerpiece announcement, the Foundation unveiled a three-year, $100 million initiative to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America's most distressed communities, targeting roughly 1.6 million additional jobs across growth sectors such as healthcare, energy transition, food systems and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, the Foundation's President, underscored the promise of restoring a stable life through hard work and resilience for American workers.The Foundation also allocated $12 million to Invest in Our Future to mobilize clean energy opportunities and drive growth in underserved areas. Maryland Governor Wes Moore participated and announced $1.5 million in philanthropic awards to advance education and childcare in neighborhoods with high childhood poverty. The gathering also introduced the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, ten innovators expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas, and showcased cross-sector partnerships with the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator. The next Big Bets for America gathering is scheduled to take place in Cleveland on 9th June 2026.

+ DATA SILOS HAMPER AID, ROCKEFELLER OFFICIAL SAYS, URGING CROSS-SECTOR INTEGRATION

On April 21st, 2026, at the One Health Summit, Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, addressed a panel titled "Philanthropies for One Health, One Health for Children" and pressed for a unified data platform. He argued that, despite unprecedented data, information remains trapped in silos across sectors, hindering decision-making and resource allocation, and he warned, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." Winter noted that the broader push for data integration mirrors earlier calls by Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025, who urged consolidating health data across nations into a single platform amidst about 3,000 fragmented databases. To demonstrate potential benefits, he pointed to Somalia’s drought crisis, where Rockefeller support is funding a local tech incubator to build a dashboard that unites health, nutrition, climate and food data to guide local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes around $2 billion annually. He closed by saying empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, merged with local information, could yield more effective and cost-efficient humanitarian responses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION AWARDED BY NHRC FOR COMMITMENT TO NIGERIA'S HUMAN RIGHTS AGENDA

On Apr. 28, 2026, in Abuja, the National Human Rights Commission honored the Ford Foundation with an Award of Appreciation for its longstanding support of human rights work in Nigeria. The ceremony drew senior NHRC officials and directors, and the honor was accepted by ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, the Foundation's Regional Director for West Africa.NHRC Executive Secretary Dr. Tony Ojukwu praised the Foundation's steady backing, especially for initiatives that broaden access to justice and strengthen the Commission's capacity to protect rights. He stressed that Nigeria's human rights landscape is broad and complex, and that durable progress depends on ongoing partnerships with development partners rather than isolated action.Aniagolu-Okoye described the recognition as meaningful and encouraging, and reiterated the Foundation's commitment to equipping citizens with knowledge of their rights and support to pursue justice when those rights are violated. She warned that awareness alone is not enough without effective enforcement mechanisms and highlighted the Foundation's role in fostering stronger enforcement through collaboration and capacity building.The event also underscored the value of continued cooperation as the country confronts evolving human rights challenges. Hilary Ogbonna, the Commission's Senior Human Rights Adviser, expressed gratitude for the Ford Foundation's enduring partnership, citing the MOVE project and the Commission's dashboard system as notable advances that benefited from the Foundation's early support.

+ WEST AFRICA FIRST LADIES CALLED TO LEAD ON GENDER-BASED VIOLENCE, FORD FOUNDATION SAYS

At the ECOWAS First Ladies Forum on Zero Tolerance for GBV in Banjul on 24th April 2026, the Ford Foundation urged First Ladies across West Africa and the region to leverage their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence, framing the First Ladies' soft power as a catalyst for social change and stronger accountability.Aniagolu noted that despite existing frameworks and commitments, GBV remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with more than 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18.The Foundation highlighted the economic cost of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of GDP in affected countries, a drain that rivals investments in key sectors such as education.The Ford Foundation stressed that First Ladies are not formal policymakers but can shape public discourse and influence decision-making across the continent. It cited Maryam Babangida and Fatima Bio as examples, noting that instruments like the Better Life for Rural Women Programme and Hands Off Our Girls campaign elevated women’s issues and contributed to national actions, underscoring the potential of soft power to drive systemic change.The Foundation has continued to invest in GBV prevention and response in West Africa, partnering with First Ladies and governors’ spouses in Nigeria to strengthen advocacy, improve evidence-based response systems, and address harmful social norms. Aniagolu argued that laws and policies alone are not enough without public trust and accessible services, pointing to persistent silence among survivors fueled by stigma, fear, and weak institutional confidence.

+ FORD FOUNDATION ALLOCATES $60 MILLION TO PROTECT DEMOCRACY AND RULE OF LAW

The Ford Foundation on April 14, 2026 announced a new $60 million grants program aimed at strengthening democratic processes nationwide. The grants will go to nonpartisan, nonprofit organizations dedicated to protecting the rule of law and voting rights. Recipients span the ideological spectrum, including Pillars of the Community, Veterans for All Voters, All Voting is Local, Campaign Legal Center, and We the Veterans Military Foundation. This initiative builds on a decade-long Civic Engagement and Government program, which has delivered more than $1 billion in grants over its history. President Heather Gerken described the effort as a proactive step to bolster democracy, stating, "As we look ahead, it is an honor to deepen this commitment and support the remarkable work taking place around the United States to strengthen democracy and the rule of law at this urgent moment." Campaign Legal Center founder Trevor Potter emphasized the importance of voting access, saying, "Ensuring every American has access to voting is paramount for the health of the democracy." Pillars of the Community is co-chaired by Ben Ginsberg and Bob Bauer, representing a bipartisan push to uphold electoral integrity. The Ford Foundation carries a $16 billion endowment and has operated for 90 years.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: WEEKLY CORPORATE COVERAGE: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-0823-S5JV-R21H-00000-00WEEKLY CORPORATE COVERAGE: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisWEEKLY CORPORATE COVERAGE: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ MILBANK LLP [8 Press Releases]

+ MILBANK AGAIN RECOGNIZED AMONG TOP TEN MOST PRESTIGIOUS LAW FIRMS IN 2027 VAULT RANKINGS

Milbank LLP is ranked 9th for overall prestige in the 2027 Vault Law Rankings, which are based on an annual survey of more than 20,000 associates.

In addition to achieving the #9 overall ranking, Milbank is rated the #1 firm for Project Finance and is among the top 10 for Clean Tech/Renewable Energy (#2), Banking & Financial Services (#5), Bankruptcy/Restructuring (#6), Energy, Oil & Gas (#6), General Corporate Practice (#9), Securities/Capital Markets (#9), Mergers & Acquisitions (#10) and Private Equity (#10). The firm is also ranked in the Securities Litigation (#19), General Commercial Litigation (#21), Business Outlook (#30) and Formal Training (#29) categories.

Source: Company Website

+ EVENTS CALENDAR: MILBANK PARTNER COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK TO PARTICIPATE IN STANFORD LAW SCHOOL 2026 SUPREME COURT CONFERENCE

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak will participate in the Stanford Law School (SLS) annual Supreme Court at Midterm conference. The conference, which takes place from May 1 - 2, 2026, brings together elite Supreme Court practitioners to evaluate the judicial landscape, covering key Supreme Court cases and analyzing how they reflect broader legal trends.

Ms. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Supreme Court and has worked on major Supreme Court cases at Milbank, including VOS Selections v. Trump (the tariffs case) and Wolford v. Lopez (the defense of a Hawaii gun law against a Second Amendment challenge).

Source: Company Website

+ EVENTS CALENDAR: MILBANK PARTNER LAUREN DOYLE TO SPEAK ON THE CONFIRMATION ISSUE/RELEASE PANEL AT THE NEW YORK CITY BANKRUPTCY CONFERENCE

Milbank Financial Restructuring partner Lauren Doyle will participate as a panelist at the American Bankruptcy Institute's New York City Bankruptcy Conference on Thursday, May 21, 2026, at the Marriott Marquis, New York.

Ms. Doyle will speak on the panel titled "Confirmation Issues/Release" which will Examine recent developments shaping chapter 11 plan confirmation, including evolving standards for consensual releases, gatekeeper provisions, and lessons from the In re ConvergeOne Holdings Corp. decision. The panel will also address how market uncertainty is affecting business projections and analyze plan implications stemming from prepetition liability management exercises (LMEs).

The ABI New York City Bankruptcy Conference is a premier gathering of insolvency and restructuring professionals, bringing together leading practitioners, judges, financial advisors and academics to discuss the most significant developments shaping the restructuring landscape.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK PARTNER COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK DISCUSSES SUPREME COURT AT DRI'S 2026 LIFE, HEALTH, DISABILITY AND ERISA SEMINAR

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak spoke at the Defense Research Institute's (DRI) 2026 Life, Health, Disability and ERISA Seminar, held April 29 - May 1 in Atlanta.

Ms. Sinzdak provided an overview of recent Supreme Court decisions that are relevant to the field. A distinguished Supreme Court attorney, Ms. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Court and has briefed scores more, including matters related to ERISA and healthcare law.

The conference is a key event for lawyers in the health, insurance, and ERISA industry. You can learn more here.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANKS ANTITRUST TEAM PUBLISHES ARTICLE IN CPI ON DOJS RENEWED SCRUTINY OF ENERGY AND DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE MARKETS

Milbank LLP Antitrust partners Adam Di Vincenzo and Fiona Schaeffer and associate John Ceccio authored an article published by Competition Policy International (CPI) examining the recent signaling by the Department of Justice (DOJ) of heightened interest in electricity generation and data center transactions.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES LENDERS ON $1.25B TERM LOAN FOR OIL AND GAS COMPANY ECOPETROL

Milbank LLP advised the lenders in connection with the $1.25 billion senior unsecured term loan facility for Ecopetrol S.A. ("Ecopetrol") (NYSE: EC). The financing will be used to refinance existing indebtedness, including the credit facility used for the acquisition of Ecopetrol's interest in Interconexion Electrica S.A. E.S.P.

Source: Company Website

+ 53 MILBANK LAWYERS FEATURED IN 2026 PRO BONO RECOGNITION LIST OF ENGLAND & WALES

Congratulations to the 53 Milbank lawyers featured in the 2026 Pro Bono Recognition List of England & Wales. The list recognizes barristers and solicitors who have given 25 hours of their time to pro bono work in the previous calendar year.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES SOCIEDAD TRANSMISORA METROPOLITANA ON $630M GREEN RULE 144A PROJECT BOND OFFERING

Milbank LLP advised Sociedad Transmisora Metropolitana S.A. ("STM") in connection with the $630 million issuance of 6.385% green senior secured project bonds due 2055. STM is a leading Chilean power transmission company and a subsidiary of Grupo Saesa (a JV between Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan ("OTTP") and Alberta Investment Management Corporation ("AIMCo")). The proceeds of the bonds were used by STM, together with the proceeds of a bond issuance in the Chilean market, to repay a $1.1 billion syndicated credit facility used to finance Grupo Saesa's acquisition of Enel Transmision Chile, the principal power transmission company in Chile's metropolitan region.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-4M53-RXNT-64JJ-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ACE US SPACE TECH ACTIVE ETF TOPS 100 BILLION WON IN NET ASSETS AS EARLY INTEREST GROWS

As the space sector evolves, the ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capture emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth space technologies.

+ KIC ANNOUNCES 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY TO STRENGTHEN FINANCE TALENT

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, set to begin on 20th April 2026, with a mission to raise finance professionals' expertise in both public and private sectors.The program comprises eight lectures over four days, starting with a 2026 outlook on the U.S. stock market and covering alternative investments such as infrastructure and private debt, plus post-investment portfolio management from both management and operational perspectives.It will also tackle topics including the U.S. interest-rate environment and the institutionalization of stablecoins, with applications already received from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, said the organization will continue to share its overseas investment experience and global network to foster domestic growth, noting that the International Finance Academy has been expanding since its 2022 inception to strengthen local investment professionals.

+ JAEHYUN JOO APPOINTED CRO OF KIC TO STRENGTHEN RISK FRAMEWORK AND GLOBAL INVESTING

Korea Investment Corporation has appointed Jaehyun Joo as Chief Risk Officer and Executive Vice President of its Investment Management Division. The decision, announced on April 17, 2026 after an Executive Committee review, establishes a three-year term for Joo.Joo brings extensive experience from his work at Korea Ratings KR since 1999 and at the Bank of Korea from December 2005, where he held key roles including Head of the Investment Strategy Team, Head of the Investment Planning Team, and Head of the Reserve Management Group.In his new post, he plans to strengthen KIC’s investment-management framework and support long-term, stable asset management to navigate complex global markets. The appointment is viewed as a crucial step in advancing KIC’s risk-management capabilities and strategic ambitions in the international arena.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ARDIAN AND GOLDMAN EXPAND SECONDARY FOOTPRINT WITH CIC PURCHASE

On 15th April 2026, Goldman Sachs and Ardian acquired a $1 billion CIC portfolio at notable discounts, with Ardian obtaining a double-digit markdown and Goldman a single-digit discount.CIC is reshaping its holdings to reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. By 2024, nearly half of its assets were allocated to alternatives, and about 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally.The sale follows CIC's earlier pause on divesting stakes in Carlyle, KKR and TPG and underscores the buyers' continued expansion of the secondary market. Ardian has just raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions totaling over $80 billion. The move comes as institutions such as Harvard and various Asian managers increasingly turn to secondaries for liquidity and rebalancing in a market where concentration and illiquidity concerns persist.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES GP POSITION IN CICC–HBIS FUND, AIMING TO ACCELERATE CHINA’S ADVANCED MATERIALS, NEW ENERGY AND IT INVESTMENTS

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The announcement was made at a ceremony at HBIS Group’s R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds plans to raise RMB32 billion over a 15-year term and will deploy capital through a blend of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS’s industrial priorities, including traditional sectors and emerging fields such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.Structured around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the vehicle is designed to connect local governments with international capital and drive industrial upgrading. BlueFive will work with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to support the fund’s long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, said the team is bullish on China’s long-term growth potential and sees opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group ranks among the world’s largest steel producers and has appeared on the Fortune Global 500 list for 17 consecutive years, while CICC provides a comprehensive financial services platform in Beijing. The partnership is expected to bolster investment opportunities and regional growth.

+ INVEST ALBERTA NOTES RENEWED CHINESE INTEREST AS CANADA ATTRACTS $60 BILLION IN FDI

China and Hong Kong’s foreign direct investment into Canada surpassed $60 billion by the end of 2023, making them the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta attracted roughly half of the total, with China at $24.9 billion and Hong Kong at $32.8 billion, according to Statistics Canada, placing them behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.The Globe and Mail, in a December 30, 2025 report, highlighted sustained Alberta–China/Hong Kong engagement despite federal tensions with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse noted rising talk with federal authorities and described the period as 'harvest time' for investors who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta says the investments are centered on oil and gas, carbon capture, green-energy projects and tourism in Alberta, with China serving as a key customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline. The relationship has shifted since the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians from 2018 to 2021, with policy steps such as a 2024 visit to Beijing by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly and a potential 2026 visit linked to a broader diplomatic reset. While ties evolve, Canada remains far more exposed to the United States for FDI (about 45.7% in 2023) than to Asia-Oceania (11.8%), where Japan, Hong Kong and China play lead roles.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MILBANK LLP [9 Press Releases]

+ MILBANK AGAIN RECOGNIZED AMONG TOP TEN MOST PRESTIGIOUS LAW FIRMS IN 2027 VAULT RANKINGS

Milbank LLP is ranked 9th for overall prestige in the 2027 Vault Law Rankings, which are based on an annual survey of more than 20,000 associates.

In addition to achieving the #9 overall ranking, Milbank is rated the #1 firm for Project Finance and is among the top 10 for Clean Tech/Renewable Energy (#2), Banking & Financial Services (#5), Bankruptcy/Restructuring (#6), Energy, Oil & Gas (#6), General Corporate Practice (#9), Securities/Capital Markets (#9), Mergers & Acquisitions (#10) and Private Equity (#10). The firm is also ranked in the Securities Litigation (#19), General Commercial Litigation (#21), Business Outlook (#30) and Formal Training (#29) categories.

Source: Company Website

+ EVENTS CALENDAR: MILBANK PARTNER COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK TO PARTICIPATE IN STANFORD LAW SCHOOL 2026 SUPREME COURT CONFERENCE

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak will participate in the Stanford Law School (SLS) annual Supreme Court at Midterm conference. The conference, which takes place from May 1 - 2, 2026, brings together elite Supreme Court practitioners to evaluate the judicial landscape, covering key Supreme Court cases and analyzing how they reflect broader legal trends.

Ms. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Supreme Court and has worked on major Supreme Court cases at Milbank, including VOS Selections v. Trump (the tariffs case) and Wolford v. Lopez (the defense of a Hawaii gun law against a Second Amendment challenge).

Source: Company Website

+ EVENTS CALENDAR: MILBANK PARTNER LAUREN DOYLE TO SPEAK ON THE CONFIRMATION ISSUE/RELEASE PANEL AT THE NEW YORK CITY BANKRUPTCY CONFERENCE

Milbank Financial Restructuring partner Lauren Doyle will participate as a panelist at the American Bankruptcy Institute's New York City Bankruptcy Conference on Thursday, May 21, 2026, at the Marriott Marquis, New York.

Ms. Doyle will speak on the panel titled "Confirmation Issues/Release" which will Examine recent developments shaping chapter 11 plan confirmation, including evolving standards for consensual releases, gatekeeper provisions, and lessons from the In re ConvergeOne Holdings Corp. decision. The panel will also address how market uncertainty is affecting business projections and analyze plan implications stemming from prepetition liability management exercises (LMEs).

The ABI New York City Bankruptcy Conference is a premier gathering of insolvency and restructuring professionals, bringing together leading practitioners, judges, financial advisors and academics to discuss the most significant developments shaping the restructuring landscape.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK PARTNER COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK DISCUSSES SUPREME COURT AT DRI'S 2026 LIFE, HEALTH, DISABILITY AND ERISA SEMINAR

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak spoke at the Defense Research Institute's (DRI) 2026 Life, Health, Disability and ERISA Seminar, held April 29 - May 1 in Atlanta.

Ms. Sinzdak provided an overview of recent Supreme Court decisions that are relevant to the field. A distinguished Supreme Court attorney, Ms. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Court and has briefed scores more, including matters related to ERISA and healthcare law.

The conference is a key event for lawyers in the health, insurance, and ERISA industry. You can learn more here.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANKS ANTITRUST TEAM PUBLISHES ARTICLE IN CPI ON DOJS RENEWED SCRUTINY OF ENERGY AND DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE MARKETS

Milbank LLP Antitrust partners Adam Di Vincenzo and Fiona Schaeffer and associate John Ceccio authored an article published by Competition Policy International (CPI) examining the recent signaling by the Department of Justice (DOJ) of heightened interest in electricity generation and data center transactions.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES LENDERS ON $1.25B TERM LOAN FOR OIL AND GAS COMPANY ECOPETROL

Milbank LLP advised the lenders in connection with the $1.25 billion senior unsecured term loan facility for Ecopetrol S.A. ("Ecopetrol") (NYSE: EC). The financing will be used to refinance existing indebtedness, including the credit facility used for the acquisition of Ecopetrol's interest in Interconexion Electrica S.A. E.S.P.

Source: Company Website

+ 53 MILBANK LAWYERS FEATURED IN 2026 PRO BONO RECOGNITION LIST OF ENGLAND & WALES

Congratulations to the 53 Milbank lawyers featured in the 2026 Pro Bono Recognition List of England & Wales. The list recognizes barristers and solicitors who have given 25 hours of their time to pro bono work in the previous calendar year.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES SOCIEDAD TRANSMISORA METROPOLITANA ON $630M GREEN RULE 144A PROJECT BOND OFFERING

Milbank LLP advised Sociedad Transmisora Metropolitana S.A. ("STM") in connection with the $630 million issuance of 6.385% green senior secured project bonds due 2055. STM is a leading Chilean power transmission company and a subsidiary of Grupo Saesa (a JV between Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan ("OTTP") and Alberta Investment Management Corporation ("AIMCo")). The proceeds of the bonds were used by STM, together with the proceeds of a bond issuance in the Chilean market, to repay a $1.1 billion syndicated credit facility used to finance Grupo Saesa's acquisition of Enel Transmision Chile, the principal power transmission company in Chile's metropolitan region.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK TRUSTS & ESTATES GROUP RANKED BAND 1 IN 2026 LEGAL 500 US PRIVATE CLIENT GUIDE

Milbank LLP's Trusts, Estates and Exempt Organizations Group is top-ranked for Private Wealth in New York in the 2026 Legal 500 US Private Client Guide. In addition to the firm recognition, co-practice group leader Austin Bramwell is featured as a "Leading Partner," and co-practice group leader Jessica Soojian is "Recommended."

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JH9-GXP3-S940-Y34D-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - RECENT NEWS FROM BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - RECENT NEWS FROM BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON APPOINTS VETERAN LEADER KRISTEN BEJARANO TO LEAD MISSION CRITICAL GROWTH

Apr. 8, 2026 — Swinerton Incorporated named Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical, reflecting the company's commitment to expanding its footprint in mission critical and data center infrastructure. Bejarano brings nearly a quarter century of experience delivering complex mission critical work for leading technology companies. She has launched and scaled a national mission critical and data center division and directed strategy and procurement for a major Americas data center program. In the newly created role, Bejarano will drive Swinerton's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate requirements into practical plans that emphasize speed to market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. She will also build on her track record of developing megaprojects for technology clients. Chany, Swinerton's Chief Revenue Officer, said Bejarano is well positioned to accelerate growth in the mission critical sector while enhancing the client experience. Her focus on cooling technologies and renewable energy integration is expected to yield innovative solutions that align with Swinerton's emphasis on sustainability and operational efficiency. Swinerton, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, includes Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, SAK Builders, and Lindgren Development, serving projects nationwide.

+ SWINERTON NAMES NEW LEADERS TO ACCELERATE NORTHEAST GROWTH

Swinerton Builders announced leadership changes in its Northeast Division, naming Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager, effective 16th March 2026. Nicholas Schifferle was appointed director of client relations and Robert Murphy was named project executive, as part of a broader plan to strengthen the firm's Northeast footprint.Quackenbush, a longtime New Yorker with nearly 30 years overseeing complex construction, brings a deep understanding of New York City's building conditions and strong client relationships that position him to lead initiatives from facility management to large-scale ground-up work.Ray Haj, Swinerton's chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, said Quackenbush’s leadership will expand the division’s capacity to meet client needs, from day-to-day facility services to large-scale projects.Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, brings more than 20 years of international business development experience, including managing high-profile design and construction projects across Asia and Australia, and founding a San Francisco design-build firm. His ongoing involvement with CoreNet Global underscores a commitment to the commercial real estate sector.Murphy, with his track record overseeing more than $650 million in commercial interiors work, will lead multiple project teams to ensure quality and client satisfaction. He will work alongside division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana as the Northeast continues to grow its interiors footprint.Since establishing a Northeast presence in 2021, Swinerton’s division has delivered a range of projects across office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up construction, with continued momentum into education, residential and public sectors.

+ DON ADAIR TO RETIRE; SWINERTON NAMES CHRIS CHANY TO LEAD REVENUE GROWTH

Swinerton announced that Executive Vice President and Chief Revenue Officer Don Adair will retire on January 9, 2026, marking the end of a nearly four-decade career in the construction industry. The company cited ongoing growth and strategic development under new leadership, and the retirement was communicated to Swinerton’s employee-owners on June 13, 2025.Adair joined Swinerton Builders in 1988 as a Project Engineer in Los Angeles and later led the San Diego office expansion before being named EVP and CRO in 2019. During his tenure, he helped shape Swinerton’s revenue-generation and client-experience strategies, contributing to overall growth.In his retirement remarks, Adair emphasized pride in Swinerton’s culture and the importance of building strong teams and lasting relationships that underpin long-term success for the company and its partners.Chris Chany, who has worked closely with Adair for nearly a decade, will succeed him. Chany joined Swinerton Builders in 2013 and is recognized for fostering partnerships and driving strategic growth. He said the company will continue to uphold core values while pursuing new growth opportunities.Chany added that the company would focus on strengthening relationships and delivering superior client experiences and reliable project execution, signaling Swinerton’s intent to sustain momentum in the construction sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ TRENT JOHNSON NAMED VICE PRESIDENT OF AVIATION OPERATIONS FOR BALFOUR BEATTY IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026. In the newly expanded role, he will lead and grow the company's aviation practice across North and South Carolina, leveraging nearly two decades in the construction industry. Johnson brings 12-and-a-half years with Balfour Beatty and a total of 19 years in the field. He has delivered 22 aviation projects, including targeted renovations and multi-phase programmes, with notable work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport, helping to solidify the company's aviation footprint in the region. In the new role, he will oversee the aviation segment across the Carolinas, continuing to support existing clients while pursuing opportunities at additional regional and international airports. His focus will include guiding project teams through active airport environments, prioritising safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination. Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, lauded Johnson for building a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner, and said his leadership will drive the next phase of growth for the company's aviation portfolio. Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and has been active in the community, notably with the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK SECURES ROLE ON BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK TO SUPPORT NETWORK RAIL

Mainmark UK has been awarded a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the geotechnical contractor to support Network Rail’s CP7 investment programme running from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an overall envelope of around £43-45 billion across Great Britain.CP7 is designed to maintain, renew, and modernise the railway, delivering a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable network. Investment priorities include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives to improve both freight and passenger performance.Within the CP7 Framework, contractors will undertake a mix of activities such as low complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will take on geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, commented, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans."Mainmark is renowned for its Teretek resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground and fills subsurface voids with minimal disruption. As heavier freight loads and severe weather stress railway assets, Teretek helps preserve structural integrity and asset resilience. The CP7 appointment positions Mainmark to contribute to a more resilient UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY POSTS 9% EPS RISE AS UK-US PROJECTS SUPPORT REVENUE GROWTH

Balfour Beatty PLC posted strong growth, powered by its construction arm, notably power-transmission work. The group’s market value has climbed about 66% over the past year as it rides a record order book, while underlying earnings per share rose 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the group said top-line growth was supported by UK demand for power transmission and a surge in US building activity. Operating profits in the support services segment jumped a third to £122m, and the underlying margin rose 80 basis points to 8.5%. The UK construction business also exceeded its margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, rising building volumes helped the group grow, though it faced cost overruns on a Civils project. The company maintains a dual-market strategy designed to sustain profitability amid these challenges. Analysts remain upbeat about the outlook, noting the large and expanding order book amid ongoing infrastructure investment. Balfour Beatty aims to improve margins and deliver projects on time, reinforcing its position in both the UK and US markets. Looking ahead, the company sees opportunity in expanding power grids and broader construction demand, while remaining mindful of rising costs and project-management challenges that could affect near-term performance.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: AES Corporation Secures Siting Permits for 343 MW Renewable Project in New York

The AES Corporation has successfully obtained siting permits for a significant renewable energy project in New York, encompassing 343 megawatts (MW) of wind and solar capacity. In a move that reinforces its commitment to sustainable energy solutions, the company has positioned itself to play a pivotal role in New York's clean energy landscape. The announcement comes on the heels of the permits being granted on the 27th of April, 2026.

The approved project aligns with New York's ambitious renewable energy goals, which aim to achieve 70% of the state's electricity from renewable sources by 2030. By integrating both wind and solar energy into its portfolio, the AES Corporation not only enhances its operational capabilities but also contributes to the state's effort to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and combat climate change.

The 343 MW project will involve a combination of onshore wind turbines and solar photovoltaic installations. AES Corporation's strategy reflects a broader trend in the energy sector, where companies are increasingly diversifying their renewable energy sources to meet rising demand. This dual approach is expected to create more resilient energy systems that can adapt to varying environmental conditions and energy needs.

In addition to contributing to New York's energy mix, the project is anticipated to create numerous jobs during the construction phase, further supporting local economies. AES Corporation has been vocal about its commitment to community engagement and the economic benefits that renewable energy projects can generate, particularly in areas where these developments are located.

The siting permits mark a significant milestone for AES Corporation as it continues to expand its footprint in the renewable energy market. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching the project's progression, as it could serve as a model for future initiatives in other regions. The company's proactive approach in securing these permits underscores its ambition to lead in the transition towards cleaner energy solutions.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 369 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: AESIndiana and Google Forge $770 Million Savings Pact for Central Indiana

AESIndiana, a subsidiary of The AES Corporation (NYSE: AES), has announced a significant partnership with Google that aims to deliver over $770 million in customer savings while powering growth in Central Indiana. The agreement, filed under HEA 1007, was officially disclosed on 24th April 2026. This initiative is poised to ensure that Google will pay for 100 percent of its energy consumption and the infrastructure required to support its proposed data center in Monrovia, thereby safeguarding residential and small business customers from any associated costs.

During a recent public meeting with the Indiana Utility Regulatory Commission (IURC), AES Indiana emphasized the importance of responsible growth that prioritizes customer protection. Brandi Davis-Handy, President of AES Indiana, stressed that affordability is one of Indiana's core energy pillars, guiding the company's planning and operational strategies. The framework established by the state legislature allows AES Indiana to address rising energy demands while maintaining a commitment to keeping energy costs low for existing customers.

Will Conkling, Region Head of Google Data Center Energy, highlighted the collaborative nature of this partnership, stating that it exemplifies how data centers and utilities can work together to foster economic development without burdening ratepayers. The agreement is expected to create new jobs and bolster infrastructure in Indiana, with Google covering all costs related to the energy demands of its data center, thus ensuring that families and small businesses in the area do not face additional financial strain.

The proposed investment will require approximately $1.3 billion in new energy infrastructure, which will support long-term construction jobs and enhance the local tax base. The HEA 1007 filing mandates that utilities demonstrate that large-load customers fully fund the infrastructure necessary to serve them. AES Indiana's proposal not only offers robust cost protections but also facilitates proactive infrastructure planning to enhance system reliability as demand grows.

All proposed investments will undergo review and approval by the IURC, with a decision anticipated in September. This partnership aligns with the White House Ratepayer Protection Pledge, which aims to safeguard ratepayers while promoting economic growth and maintaining a reliable energy grid as businesses expand. For more details about AES Indiana's initiatives to support responsible growth in Central Indiana, interested parties can visit the company's website.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 549 words

Fri Apr 17, 2026: AES Corporation Acquisition Highlights $33.4 Billion Infrastructure Megadeal

A landmark transaction is reshaping the global infrastructure landscape. A consortium led by Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP) and EQT has agreed to acquire AES Corporation in a deal valued at approximately $33.4 billion, marking one of the largest infrastructure buyouts of 2026 and signaling a new phase in the competition for strategic energy assets. The date of this significant announcement was the 17th of April 2026, underscoring a powerful convergence of themes currently driving alternative investments: the global transition toward renewable energy, the insatiable demand for power driven by artificial intelligence infrastructure, and the increasing role of private capital in funding critical energy systems. For institutional investors, the AES deal is more than just another megabuyout—it is a window into how capital is being deployed at scale in one of the most consequential sectors of the modern economy.

The acquisition of AES represents a defining moment for infrastructure investing in 2026. At $33.4 billion enterprise value, the deal ranks among the largest sponsor-led transactions globally this year, rivaling major buyouts across private equity and energy sectors. Global Infrastructure Partners, one of the most prominent infrastructure investors globally, brings deep experience in energy, transport, and digital infrastructure. Its partnership with EQT—another global powerhouse with a rapidly expanding infrastructure platform—signals a coordinated effort to consolidate and scale assets that are central to the future of energy generation and distribution. AES, historically known for its diversified power generation portfolio, has spent the past decade repositioning itself toward renewables, battery storage, and grid modernization, making it an attractive platform for long-term capital deployment.

The AES deal reflects broader structural shifts in infrastructure investing. Over the past decade, infrastructure has evolved from a niche allocation to a core component of institutional portfolios. Pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, and insurance companies have significantly increased their exposure to the asset class, attracted by its combination of yield, inflation protection, and long-duration cash flows. In 2026, this trend has accelerated. Several key drivers are fueling demand: the global push toward decarbonization necessitating massive capital expenditures in renewable energy; the rise of artificial intelligence reshaping energy markets, particularly in data centers; and the privatization of public assets due to fiscal pressures on governments, creating opportunities for private capital to step in and fund infrastructure development.

The involvement of GIP and EQT highlights the growing dominance of mega-funds in infrastructure investing. These firms have raised massive pools of capital, often exceeding tens of billions of dollars, enabling them to pursue transactions of unprecedented scale. This consolidation has implications, including pricing power that allows larger funds to outbid smaller competitors, operational expertise that drives efficiency, and global reach that enables capital deployment across regions. However, this concentration of capital raises questions about competition and potential overvaluation for high-quality assets, which could compress future returns.

Despite its strategic appeal, the AES acquisition is not without risks. Regulatory challenges may arise due to changing policies and environmental regulations across jurisdictions, and execution risks could emerge from integrating diverse energy assets. Market dynamics also pose threats, as shifts in energy prices and demand patterns could affect valuations. Lastly, increased competition for infrastructure assets may lead to higher acquisition prices. For institutional investors, the AES deal serves as both an opportunity and a challenge, emphasizing the importance of navigating the complexities of infrastructure investment.

urn:contentItem:6JHB-19T3-RVM1-00W7-00000-00The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:59:47ZLexisNexisWEB EXTRASThe Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?Juanpablo RamirezFranco for Grist

The Great Lakes are ideal for wind energy. So where is it?

There are no wind turbines on the Great Lakes and it isn't for lack of wind.

This titanic network of interconnected freshwater lakes with a surface area larger than New England, New York, and New Jersey combined is theoretically ideal for wind farms. Winds sweeping across the lakes are stronger, more consistent, and less turbulent than those over land. The National Laboratory of the Rockies has estimated that the Great Lakes states have enough offshore wind potential to generate more than three times their combined annual electricity consumption.

Plus, unlike proposed wind farms along the East Coast, where the federal government controls the seabed, Great Lakes states have jurisdiction over their lakebeds, Grist reports. While projects would still face federal oversight, some proponents see wind development in the Great Lakes as a potential workaround to President Trump's attacks on the industry.

"If it's done correctly and we're able to harness even a fraction of that, we could offset a lot of electricity demand," said Melissa Scanlan, director of the Center for Water Policy at the University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee.

Still, the vast inland lakes remain untapped largely due to a lack of streamlined permitting processes at the state level and economic hurdles. Offshore wind is expensive, and unlike the East Coast, where a mature wind industry has built specialized ships and ports to build turbines in the sea, the Great Lakes states lack the infrastructure. Ultimately, renewable energy experts say the region doesn't have the legal framework or technical capacity to support such massive projects.

So far, offshore wind power which involves installing turbines in large bodies of water, typically the ocean in the United States, has been concentrated along the Eastern seaboard. Projects are still very rare: Just three are currently operating, with several more under construction, all along the East Coast.

More than a decade ago, President Barack Obama's push for renewable energy buoyed a short-lived boom in offshore wind projects particularly in the Great Lakes. The New York Power Authority and cities such as Toronto explored boosting their energy supply with wind power from Lake Ontario and Lake Erie. However, the initial boost only propelled one project forward: Ohio's Icebreaker Wind in Lake Erie. After clashing with Ohio regulators over whether turbines could run through the night and battling a court case filed by local residents over impacts to bats and migratory birds, the developers eventually ran out of money and called the project off in 2023.

Building renewable energy offshore in federal waters has now become harder than ever. On his first day back in office, President Donald Trump signed a memorandum shutting down all offshore wind permitting, effectively suspending five large-scale East Coast projects. Although courts later overturned those cancellations, the uncertainty around offshore wind development under the Trump administration has persisted. And since any project on the Great Lakes would still need federal permits and reviews under the Clean Air Act and the Endangered Species Act, among others, wind developers have largely backed off their plans.

"That told us that this was not the time to reintroduce floating offshore wind in Illinois," said Jim Lanard, co-founder of Magellan Wind, an offshore wind developer focused on the Great Lakes. "Rather, we needed to face the existential threat to the industry."

Lanard said the regulatory uncertainty means it's a bad time for business. His company, which in the past has submitted comments to the Illinois power regulators related to the deployment of offshore wind on Lake Michigan, has temporarily paused pursuing work on the Great Lakes.

"Governors and legislatures are so focused on trying to figure out how to run their governments with the reduced support coming from WashingtonD.C.," Lanard said. "It's not time for them to add additional burdens onto their staff, who don't know how they're going to balance their budgets."

Though Illinois' jurisdiction over part of Lake Michigan presents some opportunities to work around Trump, Lanard said developers are not going to come to Illinois until there is a federal policy to protect the industry from whiplash elections. Moreover, Illinois and the other Great Lakes states have yet to establish a substantial legal framework to make local offshore wind farms a reality. Given these challenges, he estimated it will be some time before there are any wind farms on the Great Lakes.

"I'm looking at five to seven years from 2029," Lanard said.

For years, state Representative Marcus Evans Jr. has championed a plan to make Illinois' shores home to the first offshore wind farm in the Great Lakes. The legislation, known as the Illinois Rust Belt to Green Belt Pilot Program Act, was designed to direct state planners to solicit proposals for a new utility-scale offshore wind farm and begin purchasing offshore wind power. First introduced by Evans in 2022, the measure has long failed to pick up enough momentum to break out of the statehouse. In 2025, Illinois Senator Robert Peters refiled the bill, but it didn't receive a committee assignment. Evans said he decided against refiling the bill this year.

"I didn't have the support," said Evans, a South Side Chicago Democrat. "I used my energy this year to focus on some other items, but it's still something that I think is important," he added.

Since the bill would require a localized industry to build, transport, and upkeep the turbines, Evans saw the push as a huge boon for the region's clean energy workforce one that the state of Illinois has long been trying to prop up.

Illinois passed the Climate and Equitable Jobs Act in 2021, which committed the state to investing $80 million annually to build out workforce training hubs focused on clean energy-related jobs. The same bill also put the state on track to transition its energy sector to 100 percent clean energy by 2050. Evans had hoped his initiative could help achieve both goals at once.

He is still optimistic that Lake Michigan will be next.

"I will file [a bill] in the future," he said.

This story is a partnership between Grist and WBEZ, a public radio station serving the Chicago metropolitan region.

This story was produced by Grist and reviewed and distributed by Stacker.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: AES Corporation Secures Siting Permits for 343 MW Renewable Project in New York

The AES Corporation has successfully obtained siting permits for a significant renewable energy project in New York, encompassing 343 megawatts (MW) of wind and solar capacity. In a move that reinforces its commitment to sustainable energy solutions, the company has positioned itself to play a pivotal role in New York's clean energy landscape. The announcement comes on the heels of the permits being granted on the 27th of April, 2026.

The approved project aligns with New York's ambitious renewable energy goals, which aim to achieve 70% of the state's electricity from renewable sources by 2030. By integrating both wind and solar energy into its portfolio, the AES Corporation not only enhances its operational capabilities but also contributes to the state's effort to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and combat climate change.

The 343 MW project will involve a combination of onshore wind turbines and solar photovoltaic installations. AES Corporation's strategy reflects a broader trend in the energy sector, where companies are increasingly diversifying their renewable energy sources to meet rising demand. This dual approach is expected to create more resilient energy systems that can adapt to varying environmental conditions and energy needs.

In addition to contributing to New York's energy mix, the project is anticipated to create numerous jobs during the construction phase, further supporting local economies. AES Corporation has been vocal about its commitment to community engagement and the economic benefits that renewable energy projects can generate, particularly in areas where these developments are located.

The siting permits mark a significant milestone for AES Corporation as it continues to expand its footprint in the renewable energy market. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching the project's progression, as it could serve as a model for future initiatives in other regions. The company's proactive approach in securing these permits underscores its ambition to lead in the transition towards cleaner energy solutions.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 369 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: AES Indiana and Google Forge $770 Million Savings Pact for Central Indiana

AESIndiana, a subsidiary of The AES Corporation (NYSE: AES), has announced a significant partnership with Google that aims to deliver over $770 million in customer savings while powering growth in Central Indiana. The agreement, filed under HEA 1007, was officially disclosed on 24th April 2026. This initiative is poised to ensure that Google will pay for 100 percent of its energy consumption and the infrastructure required to support its proposed data center in Monrovia, thereby safeguarding residential and small business customers from any associated costs.

During a recent public meeting with the Indiana Utility Regulatory Commission (IURC), AES Indiana emphasized the importance of responsible growth that prioritizes customer protection. Brandi Davis-Handy, President of AES Indiana, stressed that affordability is one of Indiana's core energy pillars, guiding the company's planning and operational strategies. The framework established by the state legislature allows AES Indiana to address rising energy demands while maintaining a commitment to keeping energy costs low for existing customers.

Will Conkling, Region Head of Google Data Center Energy, highlighted the collaborative nature of this partnership, stating that it exemplifies how data centers and utilities can work together to foster economic development without burdening ratepayers. The agreement is expected to create new jobs and bolster infrastructure in Indiana, with Google covering all costs related to the energy demands of its data center, thus ensuring that families and small businesses in the area do not face additional financial strain.

The proposed investment will require approximately $1.3 billion in new energy infrastructure, which will support long-term construction jobs and enhance the local tax base. The HEA 1007 filing mandates that utilities demonstrate that large-load customers fully fund the infrastructure necessary to serve them. AES Indiana's proposal not only offers robust cost protections but also facilitates proactive infrastructure planning to enhance system reliability as demand grows.

All proposed investments will undergo review and approval by the IURC, with a decision anticipated in September. This partnership aligns with the White House Ratepayer Protection Pledge, which aims to safeguard ratepayers while promoting economic growth and maintaining a reliable energy grid as businesses expand. For more details about AES Indiana's initiatives to support responsible growth in Central Indiana, interested parties can visit the company's website.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 549 words

Fri Apr 17, 2026: AES Corporation Acquisition Highlights $33.4 Billion Infrastructure Megadeal

A landmark transaction is reshaping the global infrastructure landscape. A consortium led by Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP) and EQT has agreed to acquire AES Corporation in a deal valued at approximately $33.4 billion, marking one of the largest infrastructure buyouts of 2026 and signaling a new phase in the competition for strategic energy assets. The date of this significant announcement was the 17th of April 2026, underscoring a powerful convergence of themes currently driving alternative investments: the global transition toward renewable energy, the insatiable demand for power driven by artificial intelligence infrastructure, and the increasing role of private capital in funding critical energy systems. For institutional investors, the AES deal is more than just another megabuyout—it is a window into how capital is being deployed at scale in one of the most consequential sectors of the modern economy.

The acquisition of AES represents a defining moment for infrastructure investing in 2026. At $33.4 billion enterprise value, the deal ranks among the largest sponsor-led transactions globally this year, rivaling major buyouts across private equity and energy sectors. Global Infrastructure Partners, one of the most prominent infrastructure investors globally, brings deep experience in energy, transport, and digital infrastructure. Its partnership with EQT—another global powerhouse with a rapidly expanding infrastructure platform—signals a coordinated effort to consolidate and scale assets that are central to the future of energy generation and distribution. AES, historically known for its diversified power generation portfolio, has spent the past decade repositioning itself toward renewables, battery storage, and grid modernization, making it an attractive platform for long-term capital deployment.

The AES deal reflects broader structural shifts in infrastructure investing. Over the past decade, infrastructure has evolved from a niche allocation to a core component of institutional portfolios. Pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, and insurance companies have significantly increased their exposure to the asset class, attracted by its combination of yield, inflation protection, and long-duration cash flows. In 2026, this trend has accelerated. Several key drivers are fueling demand: the global push toward decarbonization necessitating massive capital expenditures in renewable energy; the rise of artificial intelligence reshaping energy markets, particularly in data centers; and the privatization of public assets due to fiscal pressures on governments, creating opportunities for private capital to step in and fund infrastructure development.

The involvement of GIP and EQT highlights the growing dominance of mega-funds in infrastructure investing. These firms have raised massive pools of capital, often exceeding tens of billions of dollars, enabling them to pursue transactions of unprecedented scale. This consolidation has implications, including pricing power that allows larger funds to outbid smaller competitors, operational expertise that drives efficiency, and global reach that enables capital deployment across regions. However, this concentration of capital raises questions about competition and potential overvaluation for high-quality assets, which could compress future returns.

Despite its strategic appeal, the AES acquisition is not without risks. Regulatory challenges may arise due to changing policies and environmental regulations across jurisdictions, and execution risks could emerge from integrating diverse energy assets. Market dynamics also pose threats, as shifts in energy prices and demand patterns could affect valuations. Lastly, increased competition for infrastructure assets may lead to higher acquisition prices. For institutional investors, the AES deal serves as both an opportunity and a challenge, emphasizing the importance of navigating the complexities of infrastructure investment.

Falling Relative Strength:

- The stock has a 6-month relative strength of 48 in the NYSE market of 1,840 stocks, 33 ADRs and 12 preference stocks which means it has underperformed 52% of the market.

- Also, the relative strength percentile is falling at 48 for 6 months, 40 for 3 months and 32 for 1 month.

But does not meet the following 6 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Return on Assets improvement.

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

- Improvement in gross margin.

- Improvement in asset turnover.

urn:contentItem:6JGY-1X03-RS58-G0VX-00000-00WEEKLY BRIEF: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisWEEKLY BRIEF: MILBANK LLP NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ MILBANK LLP [9 Press Releases]

+ MILBANK AGAIN RECOGNIZED AMONG TOP TEN MOST PRESTIGIOUS LAW FIRMS IN 2027 VAULT RANKINGS

Milbank LLP is ranked 9th for overall prestige in the 2027 Vault Law Rankings, which are based on an annual survey of more than 20,000 associates.

In addition to achieving the #9 overall ranking, Milbank is rated the #1 firm for Project Finance and is among the top 10 for Clean Tech/Renewable Energy (#2), Banking & Financial Services (#5), Bankruptcy/Restructuring (#6), Energy, Oil & Gas (#6), General Corporate Practice (#9), Securities/Capital Markets (#9), Mergers & Acquisitions (#10) and Private Equity (#10). The firm is also ranked in the Securities Litigation (#19), General Commercial Litigation (#21), Business Outlook (#30) and Formal Training (#29) categories.

Source: Company Website

+ EVENTS CALENDAR: MILBANK PARTNER COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK TO PARTICIPATE IN STANFORD LAW SCHOOL 2026 SUPREME COURT CONFERENCE

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak will participate in the Stanford Law School (SLS) annual Supreme Court at Midterm conference. The conference, which takes place from May 1 - 2, 2026, brings together elite Supreme Court practitioners to evaluate the judicial landscape, covering key Supreme Court cases and analyzing how they reflect broader legal trends.

Ms. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Supreme Court and has worked on major Supreme Court cases at Milbank, including VOS Selections v. Trump (the tariffs case) and Wolford v. Lopez (the defense of a Hawaii gun law against a Second Amendment challenge).

Source: Company Website

+ EVENTS CALENDAR: MILBANK PARTNER LAUREN DOYLE TO SPEAK ON THE CONFIRMATION ISSUE/RELEASE PANEL AT THE NEW YORK CITY BANKRUPTCY CONFERENCE

Milbank Financial Restructuring partner Lauren Doyle will participate as a panelist at the American Bankruptcy Institute's New York City Bankruptcy Conference on Thursday, May 21, 2026, at the Marriott Marquis, New York.

Ms. Doyle will speak on the panel titled "Confirmation Issues/Release" which will Examine recent developments shaping chapter 11 plan confirmation, including evolving standards for consensual releases, gatekeeper provisions, and lessons from the In re ConvergeOne Holdings Corp. decision. The panel will also address how market uncertainty is affecting business projections and analyze plan implications stemming from prepetition liability management exercises (LMEs).

The ABI New York City Bankruptcy Conference is a premier gathering of insolvency and restructuring professionals, bringing together leading practitioners, judges, financial advisors and academics to discuss the most significant developments shaping the restructuring landscape.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK PARTNER COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK DISCUSSES SUPREME COURT AT DRI'S 2026 LIFE, HEALTH, DISABILITY AND ERISA SEMINAR

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak spoke at the Defense Research Institute's (DRI) 2026 Life, Health, Disability and ERISA Seminar, held April 29 - May 1 in Atlanta.

Ms. Sinzdak provided an overview of recent Supreme Court decisions that are relevant to the field. A distinguished Supreme Court attorney, Ms. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Court and has briefed scores more, including matters related to ERISA and healthcare law.

The conference is a key event for lawyers in the health, insurance, and ERISA industry. You can learn more here.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANKS ANTITRUST TEAM PUBLISHES ARTICLE IN CPI ON DOJS RENEWED SCRUTINY OF ENERGY AND DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE MARKETS

Milbank LLP Antitrust partners Adam Di Vincenzo and Fiona Schaeffer and associate John Ceccio authored an article published by Competition Policy International (CPI) examining the recent signaling by the Department of Justice (DOJ) of heightened interest in electricity generation and data center transactions.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES LENDERS ON $1.25B TERM LOAN FOR OIL AND GAS COMPANY ECOPETROL

Milbank LLP advised the lenders in connection with the $1.25 billion senior unsecured term loan facility for Ecopetrol S.A. ("Ecopetrol") (NYSE: EC). The financing will be used to refinance existing indebtedness, including the credit facility used for the acquisition of Ecopetrol's interest in Interconexion Electrica S.A. E.S.P.

Source: Company Website

+ 53 MILBANK LAWYERS FEATURED IN 2026 PRO BONO RECOGNITION LIST OF ENGLAND & WALES

Congratulations to the 53 Milbank lawyers featured in the 2026 Pro Bono Recognition List of England & Wales. The list recognizes barristers and solicitors who have given 25 hours of their time to pro bono work in the previous calendar year.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK ADVISES SOCIEDAD TRANSMISORA METROPOLITANA ON $630M GREEN RULE 144A PROJECT BOND OFFERING

Milbank LLP advised Sociedad Transmisora Metropolitana S.A. ("STM") in connection with the $630 million issuance of 6.385% green senior secured project bonds due 2055. STM is a leading Chilean power transmission company and a subsidiary of Grupo Saesa (a JV between Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan ("OTTP") and Alberta Investment Management Corporation ("AIMCo")). The proceeds of the bonds were used by STM, together with the proceeds of a bond issuance in the Chilean market, to repay a $1.1 billion syndicated credit facility used to finance Grupo Saesa's acquisition of Enel Transmision Chile, the principal power transmission company in Chile's metropolitan region.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK TRUSTS & ESTATES GROUP RANKED BAND 1 IN 2026 LEGAL 500 US PRIVATE CLIENT GUIDE

Milbank LLP's Trusts, Estates and Exempt Organizations Group is top-ranked for Private Wealth in New York in the 2026 Legal 500 US Private Client Guide. In addition to the firm recognition, co-practice group leader Austin Bramwell is featured as a "Leading Partner," and co-practice group leader Jessica Soojian is "Recommended."

Source: Company Website
