value.Title: SWINERTON INCORPORATED - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH3-HP93-S4TB-X1N3-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING BALFOUR BEATTY PLC AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON APPOINTS VETERAN LEADER KRISTEN BEJARANO TO LEAD MISSION CRITICAL GROWTH

Apr. 8, 2026 — Swinerton Incorporated named Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical, reflecting the company's commitment to expanding its footprint in mission critical and data center infrastructure. Bejarano brings nearly a quarter century of experience delivering complex mission critical work for leading technology companies. She has launched and scaled a national mission critical and data center division and directed strategy and procurement for a major Americas data center program. In the newly created role, Bejarano will drive Swinerton's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate requirements into practical plans that emphasize speed to market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. She will also build on her track record of developing megaprojects for technology clients. Chany, Swinerton's Chief Revenue Officer, said Bejarano is well positioned to accelerate growth in the mission critical sector while enhancing the client experience. Her focus on cooling technologies and renewable energy integration is expected to yield innovative solutions that align with Swinerton's emphasis on sustainability and operational efficiency. Swinerton, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, includes Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, SAK Builders, and Lindgren Development, serving projects nationwide.

+ SWINERTON NAMES NEW LEADERS TO ACCELERATE NORTHEAST GROWTH

Swinerton Builders announced leadership changes in its Northeast Division, naming Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager, effective 16th March 2026. Nicholas Schifferle was appointed director of client relations and Robert Murphy was named project executive, as part of a broader plan to strengthen the firm's Northeast footprint.Quackenbush, a longtime New Yorker with nearly 30 years overseeing complex construction, brings a deep understanding of New York City's building conditions and strong client relationships that position him to lead initiatives from facility management to large-scale ground-up work.Ray Haj, Swinerton's chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, said Quackenbush’s leadership will expand the division’s capacity to meet client needs, from day-to-day facility services to large-scale projects.Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, brings more than 20 years of international business development experience, including managing high-profile design and construction projects across Asia and Australia, and founding a San Francisco design-build firm. His ongoing involvement with CoreNet Global underscores a commitment to the commercial real estate sector.Murphy, with his track record overseeing more than $650 million in commercial interiors work, will lead multiple project teams to ensure quality and client satisfaction. He will work alongside division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana as the Northeast continues to grow its interiors footprint.Since establishing a Northeast presence in 2021, Swinerton’s division has delivered a range of projects across office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up construction, with continued momentum into education, residential and public sectors.

+ DON ADAIR TO RETIRE; SWINERTON NAMES CHRIS CHANY TO LEAD REVENUE GROWTH

Swinerton announced that Executive Vice President and Chief Revenue Officer Don Adair will retire on January 9, 2026, marking the end of a nearly four-decade career in the construction industry. The company cited ongoing growth and strategic development under new leadership, and the retirement was communicated to Swinerton’s employee-owners on June 13, 2025.Adair joined Swinerton Builders in 1988 as a Project Engineer in Los Angeles and later led the San Diego office expansion before being named EVP and CRO in 2019. During his tenure, he helped shape Swinerton’s revenue-generation and client-experience strategies, contributing to overall growth.In his retirement remarks, Adair emphasized pride in Swinerton’s culture and the importance of building strong teams and lasting relationships that underpin long-term success for the company and its partners.Chris Chany, who has worked closely with Adair for nearly a decade, will succeed him. Chany joined Swinerton Builders in 2013 and is recognized for fostering partnerships and driving strategic growth. He said the company will continue to uphold core values while pursuing new growth opportunities.Chany added that the company would focus on strengthening relationships and delivering superior client experiences and reliable project execution, signaling Swinerton’s intent to sustain momentum in the construction sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ TRENT JOHNSON NAMED VICE PRESIDENT OF AVIATION OPERATIONS FOR BALFOUR BEATTY IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026. In the newly expanded role, he will lead and grow the company's aviation practice across North and South Carolina, leveraging nearly two decades in the construction industry. Johnson brings 12-and-a-half years with Balfour Beatty and a total of 19 years in the field. He has delivered 22 aviation projects, including targeted renovations and multi-phase programmes, with notable work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport, helping to solidify the company's aviation footprint in the region. In the new role, he will oversee the aviation segment across the Carolinas, continuing to support existing clients while pursuing opportunities at additional regional and international airports. His focus will include guiding project teams through active airport environments, prioritising safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination. Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, lauded Johnson for building a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner, and said his leadership will drive the next phase of growth for the company's aviation portfolio. Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and has been active in the community, notably with the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK SECURES ROLE ON BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK TO SUPPORT NETWORK RAIL

Mainmark UK has been awarded a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the geotechnical contractor to support Network Rail’s CP7 investment programme running from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an overall envelope of around £43-45 billion across Great Britain.CP7 is designed to maintain, renew, and modernise the railway, delivering a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable network. Investment priorities include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives to improve both freight and passenger performance.Within the CP7 Framework, contractors will undertake a mix of activities such as low complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will take on geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, commented, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans."Mainmark is renowned for its Teretek resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground and fills subsurface voids with minimal disruption. As heavier freight loads and severe weather stress railway assets, Teretek helps preserve structural integrity and asset resilience. The CP7 appointment positions Mainmark to contribute to a more resilient UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY POSTS 9% EPS RISE AS UK-US PROJECTS SUPPORT REVENUE GROWTH

Balfour Beatty PLC posted strong growth, powered by its construction arm, notably power-transmission work. The group’s market value has climbed about 66% over the past year as it rides a record order book, while underlying earnings per share rose 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the group said top-line growth was supported by UK demand for power transmission and a surge in US building activity. Operating profits in the support services segment jumped a third to £122m, and the underlying margin rose 80 basis points to 8.5%. The UK construction business also exceeded its margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, rising building volumes helped the group grow, though it faced cost overruns on a Civils project. The company maintains a dual-market strategy designed to sustain profitability amid these challenges. Analysts remain upbeat about the outlook, noting the large and expanding order book amid ongoing infrastructure investment. Balfour Beatty aims to improve margins and deliver projects on time, reinforcing its position in both the UK and US markets. Looking ahead, the company sees opportunity in expanding power grids and broader construction demand, while remaining mindful of rising costs and project-management challenges that could affect near-term performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ HENSEL PHELPS SIGNS MULTI-YEAR DEAL WITH TRACK3D TO STANDARDIZE CONSTRUCTION PROGRESS MONITORING ACROSS PORTFOLIO

Track3D and Hensel Phelps have signed a multi-year agreement to roll out Track3D's Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects, elevating a pilot into a portfolio-wide deployment as of Apr. 1, 2026. The deal underscores Hensel Phelps' commitment to leveraging AI-driven construction data across its operations.The arrangement envisions standardizing Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier identification of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex projects, while improving schedule reliability and accountability across diverse sites.A San Francisco International Airport case study showcased the platform's ability to move teams from reactive problem-solving to proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and active involvement by existing field personnel, minimizing workflow disruption.In its March update, Track3D introduced an admin dashboard, Groups functionality and capture-free progress tracking directly on floorplans, designed to give major contractors centralized visibility and easier user management.The company is expanding its market approach by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026 to demonstrate automation of production tracking and reduction of manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Taken together, the developments reinforce Track3D's competitive position and lay groundwork for future growth.

+ MICAH (PONO) CHOY NAMED PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT FOR HAWAII REGIONAL KITCHEN AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, the promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.Choy's experience spans aviation, wastewater, and federal market projects in Hawaii and Guam, where he has helped deliver complex assignments from planning to execution.Choy's collaborative leadership has allowed him to build strong partnerships across teams and consistently drive project outcomes. Hensel Phelps regards this promotion as part of its ongoing effort to develop talent from within; the Regional Kitchen project is essential for upgrading Hawaii's educational facilities and will benefit from his leadership.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ADOPTS TRACK3D REALITY INTELLIGENCE FOR PORTFOLIO-WIDE PROGRESS MONITORING

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable application, Track3D pointed to a San Francisco International Airport case study where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes.On the development front, Track3D disclosed a March release to boost enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency, including an admin dashboard and Groups functionality to centralize portfolio visibility and streamline user management, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans, enhancing operational flexibility for enterprise users.The company is also sharpening its go-to-market by targeting specialty contractors and will participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in the industry as it aims to automate production tracking and protect margins. Together with the Hensel Phelps deal and the SFO case study, the March upgrades mark meaningful commercial traction for Track3D.

+ QUANTA SERVICES [3 News That Matters]

+ QUANTA SERVICES COMPLETES TRI-CITY GROUP PURCHASE, BROADENING ELECTRIC INFRASTRUCTURE CAPABILITIES

Quanta Services, Inc. completed the Tri-City Group deal, expanding its footprint in electrical construction and industrial services. The Davenport-based contractor's acquisition, announced on 6th April 2026, strengthens Quanta's capacity to serve electric power and renewable energy programs across the United States.The Tri-City Group has a long history in the Quad Cities dating to 1895 and rebranded in 2024 to reflect its diversified services. It includes Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy, with a presence across 46 states and several countries.Lori Rochholz of Tri-City reaffirmed that the brand will remain unchanged, and leadership from the Palmer Family will stay in place, ensuring continuity and client service. The company remains committed to the local community.Quanta's CEO Duke Austin noted the acquisition, along with Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service, broadens its crafts-skilled platform to support electric utility programs nationwide. The deal expands Quanta’s 69,000-employee platform to meet growing demand in infrastructure markets.The deal is expected to bring operational synergies as Quanta integrates Tri-City's subsidiaries and regional capabilities, strengthening its leadership in electrical infrastructure.

+ QUANTA SEES GROWTH ACROSS CLEAN-ENERGY BUILDOUT, DETAILS PLAN AT JEFFERIES

Quanta Services used the Jefferies conference to lay out its strategic vision for 2026, presenting on Mar. 3, 2026 after a solid year. Executives highlighted favorable tailwinds from clean-energy deployment and infrastructure development as growth drivers and outlined steps to strengthen operations and broaden service offerings. The discussion touched on the data-center market, with the company pointing to partnerships that reflect a measured approach to risk management. By engaging with mid-size utilities such as NIPSCO, Quanta aims to tailor solutions that meet specific needs while mitigating counterparty risk. Management reiterated a disciplined focus on organic growth and capital allocation, noting that acquisitions will be selective and aimed at sustainable, long-term value. Overall, the company portrayed a proactive stance to navigate a rapidly changing industry and position for growth in 2026.

+ QUANTA SERVICES EMERGES AS THE CENTRAL PLATFORM FOR UTILITY MODERNIZATION AND GRID RESILIENCE

Quanta Services has established itself as the central infrastructure platform for utilities, renewables and grid modernization, with the company poised to be a key execution engine in North America's energy transition by year-end 2025. Its reach extends beyond consumer brands to power lines, substations, pipelines and fiber networks that underpin an increasingly electrified economy.The firm emphasizes engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) services, enabling utilities to outsource entire projects and reduce the complexity of coordinating multiple contractors. This EPC-led model is designed to accelerate modernization while simplifying project execution and risk allocation.Quanta operates through three core lines—electric power infrastructure, underground utility and energy infrastructure, and communications infrastructure—providing an integrated platform capable of handling high-voltage transmission, urban undergrounding and modern communications networks. The company leverages technologies such as LiDAR and grid digitalization to boost efficiency and strengthen risk management for its utility customers.In a competitive field that includes MasTec and MYR Group, Quanta differentiates itself through scale and focused specialization in utility and network infrastructure, enabling it to win large-scale modernization programs more consistently than peers.From an investor perspective, Quanta Services' stock (ISIN US74762E1029) carries a premium valuation relative to traditional engineering peers, reflecting confidence in its ability to deliver durable growth as utilities accelerate grid hardening and renewable integration.

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ BARTON MALOW, MARCH-WESTIN TO MANAGE MILAN PUSKAR STADIUM WEST TOWER

MORGANTOWN, W.Va. - West Virginia University's Department of Intercollegiate Athletics will partner with Barton Malow and March-Westin to serve as construction managers on the Milan Puskar Stadium West Tower Project, which is set to begin after the 2026 football season. The joint venture brings together a power team of national and local construction leadership to the project.As construction managers, Barton Malow and March-Westin will oversee all construction from conception to completion, ensuring that the project is finished safely, on time and within budget. By managing the daily on-site operations, the team will be the primary liaison between the university, architects and engineers, handling preconstruction planning, cost and schedule management, procurement, and coordination of onsite construction activities.The project team will draw on the experience of both firms to deliver a functional and iconic addition to Milan Puskar Stadium, with design and operations considerations aligned to the university's needs.The collaboration combines complementary expertise in the unique campus and stadium environments. March-Westin brings decades of experience delivering work at Milan Puskar Stadium and across the university's two main campuses, while Barton Malow contributes extensive experience leading collegiate and professional stadium renovations nationwide.Jamie Ridgeway, president of March-Westin, noted that the local knowledge paired with Barton Malow's national experience uniquely positions the team to successfully complete this project with minimal disruption to the fan experience.

+ DETROIT CITY FC NAMES BARTON MALOW CONSTRUCTION MANAGER FOR ALUMNIFI FIELD

Barton Malow has been selected as construction manager for AlumniFi Field, Detroit City FC's forthcoming 15,000-seat home stadium to be built on the former Southwest Detroit Hospital site. The project aims for a spring 2027 opening.Detroit City FC is expanding its presence in the USL Championship, with fan demand at the current 7,000-seat Keyworth Stadium regularly exceeding capacity.AlumniFi Field will sit between Detroit's Corktown and Mexicantown neighborhoods and is designed to function as a vibrant community hub capable of hosting soccer matches as well as concerts and other events.Design plans include a natural grass pitch, three permanent grandstands with canopies, and dedicated spaces for community activities and other uses of the multi-purpose venue.The hospital's legacy in promoting diversity in hiring since it opened in 1973 will be honored as part of the project, and officials say the stadium will reflect Detroit's character and strengthen ties with local supporters.

+ MACON ARENA SUBCONTRACTOR BRIEFING SET BY BARTON MALOW, SHERIDAN

Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction will host an information session for subcontractors and suppliers on the New Macon Arena project, with the aim of presenting the project scope, schedule, and available trade bid opportunities for firms including SLBE, MBE, WBE, and DBE participants. The session is set for 24th March 2026, from 11:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. at the Macon Centreplex, 200 Coliseum Drive, Macon.Attendees will hear a short presentation, followed by a Q&A and networking time with project leads. Registration is encouraged but not required; firms can register ahead of time through the Barton Malow website.The Office of Small Business Affairs (OSBA) in Macon-Bibb County supports the outreach and invites local vendors to participate. OSBA Director Charise Stephens-Merriweather notes that the project offers a unique opportunity for local, small, and minority businesses to bid from the outset.Mayor Lester Miller described the arena as more than a venue, calling it a destination and major economic engine that will attract visitors and help keep the city competitive for years to come.The new Macon Arena will be built on the current Macon Coliseum site, spanning more than 230,000 square feet with seating for about 8,500 for concerts. The design emphasizes flexibility to accommodate sports and other events, and Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction bring a long track record of delivering high-quality projects and community-focused development.

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. + TURNER CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF 72-MW ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER IN FREDERICK

Turner Construction Company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, signifying the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility. The milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after ground had been broken. To date, the project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours, with roughly 345 skilled workers contributing to progress. The 450,000-square-foot center sits on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize performance. Turner's leadership emphasized that topping out represents more than erecting the frame, underscoring the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the team’s commitment to safe, efficient delivery. As the project moves into interior fit-out and operational readiness, stakeholders will watch closely, anticipating the facility’s role in supporting the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ GEISINGER WYOMING VALLEY EXPANSION MOVES TO VERTICAL CONSTRUCTION AS TURNER BREAKS GROUND

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has advanced to vertical construction on Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center's $900 million expansion, moving forward after years of planning and foundation work. Steel now rises on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will lift beds from 356 to 500 and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. Officials say the addition will enlarge the emergency department and ensure all patient rooms are private, with completion expected in the first quarter of 2028. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, described the milestone as a key step toward broader health care access for families in northeastern Pennsylvania and a chance to partner with Geisinger on a project that strengthens the community for decades. The project is projected to create as many as 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Onsite employment is expected to rise from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak activity, with Geisinger planning to hire an additional 400 staff after opening.Geisinger Wyoming Valley's campus opened in 1981 and has since expanded to meet rising demand for medical services. The new tower will bring advanced capabilities comparable to those typically found in major academic medical centers serving large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION NAMED BUILDER FOR WESTCOURT ORLANDO'S AMBITIOUS MIXED-USE DISTRICT

Turner Construction has been selected to build Westcourt Orlando, the long-planned mixed-use district just north of the Kia Center, with enabling work already underway as of 22nd April 2026.The decision followed an extensive preconstruction phase. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, credited Turner's track record on complex builds, particularly in the Space Coast, as a key factor in securing the contract. "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us and build this project," Chapman remarked, underscoring confidence in Turner's capabilities.Turner has been involved in pre-construction activities since 2022, recently launching site operations including utility mapping and surveying.With a strong local footprint dating to 1984, Turner’s Orlando office and regional leadership under vice president Jeff Justen were cited by developers as important factors in the decision.Westcourt is envisioned as a sprawling 900,000 to 1,000,000-square-foot district that will integrate hotel, residential, office, retail, and live event spaces adjacent to the Kia Center. The plans include a 265-key Kimpton hotel, approximately 269 high-rise residences, around 300,000 square feet of Class A office space, 125,000 square feet of retail, a 3,500-capacity events venue, and roughly 1,140 parking stalls.While a firm groundbreaking date has yet to be confirmed, published timelines indicate a projected start in Q1 2026, potentially completing by late 2028, although local sources suggest delays could push mobilization to the end of 2026. The development team anticipates that Turner Construction will support approximately 800 workers on-site at peak construction, contributing around 1,600 direct jobs throughout the project’s lifecycle. As office leasing for the Class A spaces has already commenced, developers report that a significant portion is pre-leased, which could facilitate financing and scheduling as they continue to update the public on the progress of the site.

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been named to Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For for 2026, with the official announcement dated 2nd April 2026. The recognition signals PCL's ongoing emphasis on a high-trust workplace, employee engagement and satisfaction.This year marks the 17th time PCL has earned a place on the Fortune list, ranking No. 91. The designation relates to its employee-owned structure, which helps cultivate a culture where staff feel valued and motivated.Industry observers note that Fortune's list serves as a benchmark for organizational health and employee experience, and high rankings often align with stronger business performance.Earlier in 2025, PCL also ranked No. 11 on Engineering News-Record's Top 400 Contractors list, reflecting leadership in delivering complex projects across North America while maintaining a focus on people.The combination of these recognitions underscores PCL's strategy of balancing project excellence with employee well-being and satisfaction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES WATER-REUSE INITIATIVES AMID COLORADO RIVER CRISIS

On the 26th of March 2026, the Western United States confronted a mounting Colorado River crisis as drought tightens water supplies.Lake Powell sits 162 feet below full and holds only 26% of its capacity; the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns Mead could reach record lows within two years, forcing a reevaluation of basin-wide management.PCL Construction is advocating for larger investments in water reuse infrastructure to bolster resilience. They note that projected U.S. investments in water reuse are averaging about $47.1 billion annually over the next decade, underscoring the potential for municipalities to capture and reuse water.Adoption of reuse varies across the seven basin states, underscoring the need for scalable infrastructure and coordinated policy.As the fall 2026 deadline for water-management rules approaches, PCL points to its Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix as a tangible model for maintaining supply for a growing city, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade. At the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference, industry leaders called for a multifaceted strategy to address climate change and aging infrastructure.

+ JE DUNN LAUNCHES FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ACCELERATE CONSTRUCTION MANUFACTURING

JE Dunn Construction has launched Form Off-Site Solutions, its off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. The program, announced with a launch date of 6th April 2026, builds on the company’s century-long Kansas City heritage while extending capabilities nationwide.Form Off-Site Solutions structures its offerings into four product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, designed to meet the varied needs of owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers.The end-to-end service model covers estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, all aimed at improving installation efficiency, safety, and quality, while providing greater cost certainty and helping shorten project timelines amid labor shortages.Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim emphasizes collaboration: "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart."JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford says the Form launch marks a major step in the firm’s ongoing push to drive innovation for clients as Form’s production facilities in Kansas City and Nashville position the unit to serve a national client base.

+ STEEL BEAM PLACED AS NORTHLAND CENTER REACHES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — The topping-out of the Northland Workforce Development Center was celebrated on 19th March 2026, as JE Dunn Construction placed the final structural steel beam for the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.Before the beam was hoisted into place, a beam-signing ceremony brought together the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, project stakeholders, and community members who signed the beam bearing project and partner logos. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, said, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is being built to house programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partner institutions while serving both high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplaces.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening expected after the milestone. Executive Director Brian Noller of Platte County School District noted that state, local government, business, investor, and community foundation support have enabled a long-term investment in students and the region.JE Dunn is serving as construction manager, working in partnership with the school district and Clark & Enersen, with a focus on safety, schedule adherence, and coordination while maintaining open communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities.

+ JE DUNN REINSTATES CHRIS CHELETTE AS SENIOR VP, HEALTHCARE, TO EXPAND REGIONAL PRESENCE

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

+ CALIFORNIA TOWER TOPPING OUT SIGNALS PROGRESS IN UC DAVIS HEALTH MASTER PLAN

The California Tower is a 14-story hospital with a five-story pavilion designed to house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities for pharmacy and burn care, and will add about 334 inpatient beds. + MCCARTHY HOLDINGS DETAILS 2024 EMISSIONS FOOTPRINT IN COMPREHENSIVE GHG REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction companies in the United States, released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, published on 16th February 2026. Developed in partnership with Gravity, the report offers a thorough view of the company’s carbon footprint, covering Scope 1, Scope 2 and key Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 undergoing independent auditing and limited assurance.The publication aligns with McCarthy’s mission to strengthen sustainability across its operations and subsidiaries and to uphold the standard of being the best builder in America. The company’s sustainability framework rests on three priorities: understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly for people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption throughout projects and operations.McCarthy also describes how emissions data informs broader responsible practices, including biodiversity initiatives and responsible fuel management. Clark Construction Group appointed Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026, with a mandate to advance national campus recruiting initiatives and strengthen early-career talent pipelines for its broad portfolio of projects.Caceres arrives from Proper, where she served as Vice President of People Operations, shaping HR strategy and operations in a hybrid workplace. urn:contentItem:6JH4-65J3-RRKY-D3J5-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ACE US SPACE TECH ACTIVE ETF TOPS 100 BILLION WON IN NET ASSETS AS EARLY INTEREST GROWS

As the space sector evolves, the ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capture emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth space technologies.

+ KIC ANNOUNCES 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY TO STRENGTHEN FINANCE TALENT

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, set to begin on 20th April 2026, with a mission to raise finance professionals' expertise in both public and private sectors.The program comprises eight lectures over four days, starting with a 2026 outlook on the U.S. stock market and covering alternative investments such as infrastructure and private debt, plus post-investment portfolio management from both management and operational perspectives.It will also tackle topics including the U.S. interest-rate environment and the institutionalization of stablecoins, with applications already received from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, said the organization will continue to share its overseas investment experience and global network to foster domestic growth, noting that the International Finance Academy has been expanding since its 2022 inception to strengthen local investment professionals.

+ JAEHYUN JOO APPOINTED CRO OF KIC TO STRENGTHEN RISK FRAMEWORK AND GLOBAL INVESTING

Korea Investment Corporation has appointed Jaehyun Joo as Chief Risk Officer and Executive Vice President of its Investment Management Division. The decision, announced on April 17, 2026 after an Executive Committee review, establishes a three-year term for Joo.Joo brings extensive experience from his work at Korea Ratings KR since 1999 and at the Bank of Korea from December 2005, where he held key roles including Head of the Investment Strategy Team, Head of the Investment Planning Team, and Head of the Reserve Management Group.In his new post, he plans to strengthen KIC’s investment-management framework and support long-term, stable asset management to navigate complex global markets. The appointment is viewed as a crucial step in advancing KIC’s risk-management capabilities and strategic ambitions in the international arena.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ARDIAN AND GOLDMAN EXPAND SECONDARY FOOTPRINT WITH CIC PURCHASE

On 15th April 2026, Goldman Sachs and Ardian acquired a $1 billion CIC portfolio at notable discounts, with Ardian obtaining a double-digit markdown and Goldman a single-digit discount.CIC is reshaping its holdings to reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. By 2024, nearly half of its assets were allocated to alternatives, and about 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally.The sale follows CIC's earlier pause on divesting stakes in Carlyle, KKR and TPG and underscores the buyers' continued expansion of the secondary market. Ardian has just raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions totaling over $80 billion. The move comes as institutions such as Harvard and various Asian managers increasingly turn to secondaries for liquidity and rebalancing in a market where concentration and illiquidity concerns persist.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES GP POSITION IN CICC–HBIS FUND, AIMING TO ACCELERATE CHINA’S ADVANCED MATERIALS, NEW ENERGY AND IT INVESTMENTS

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The announcement was made at a ceremony at HBIS Group’s R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds plans to raise RMB32 billion over a 15-year term and will deploy capital through a blend of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS’s industrial priorities, including traditional sectors and emerging fields such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.Structured around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the vehicle is designed to connect local governments with international capital and drive industrial upgrading. BlueFive will work with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to support the fund’s long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, said the team is bullish on China’s long-term growth potential and sees opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group ranks among the world’s largest steel producers and has appeared on the Fortune Global 500 list for 17 consecutive years, while CICC provides a comprehensive financial services platform in Beijing. The partnership is expected to bolster investment opportunities and regional growth.

+ INVEST ALBERTA NOTES RENEWED CHINESE INTEREST AS CANADA ATTRACTS $60 BILLION IN FDI

China and Hong Kong’s foreign direct investment into Canada surpassed $60 billion by the end of 2023, making them the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta attracted roughly half of the total, with China at $24.9 billion and Hong Kong at $32.8 billion, according to Statistics Canada, placing them behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.The Globe and Mail, in a December 30, 2025 report, highlighted sustained Alberta–China/Hong Kong engagement despite federal tensions with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse noted rising talk with federal authorities and described the period as 'harvest time' for investors who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta says the investments are centered on oil and gas, carbon capture, green-energy projects and tourism in Alberta, with China serving as a key customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline. The relationship has shifted since the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians from 2018 to 2021, with policy steps such as a 2024 visit to Beijing by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly and a potential 2026 visit linked to a broader diplomatic reset. While ties evolve, Canada remains far more exposed to the United States for FDI (about 45.7% in 2023) than to Asia-Oceania (11.8%), where Japan, Hong Kong and China play lead roles.

value.Title: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS

urn:contentItem:6JH4-65J3-RRKY-D3TF-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES $96 MILLION LOAN TO SUPPORT JASPER'S CLASS A APARTMENT COMMUNITY IN DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES

Fortress Investment Group closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily community in DowntownLos Angeles. The financing was finalized on Feb. 9, 2026, as Fortress continues to back high-quality urban housing developments near major university campuses.Jasper is developed by Cityview and situated near the University of Southern California. The property comprises studios, and one- and two-bedroom units with upscale finishes and a broad amenity package that includes affordable housing units. Proceeds will refinance construction debt and support leasing progress for the new asset.Cityview's Damian Gancman praised Fortress for a smooth, solution-driven process and seamless execution from start to finish. Fortress's Noah Shore emphasized Jasper's fit with the firm's focus on backing well-capitalized, newly built multifamily projects with experienced sponsors, reflecting Fortress's broader investment approach.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) TIES WITH ALTERRA TO EXPAND CLIMATE TRANSITION FUNDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Ties With ALTERRA to Expand Climate Transition FundingKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) partners with ALTERRA, the UAE’s $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to support its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made on Apr. 22, 2026. ALTERRA launched at COP28 and aims to mobilize up to $250 billion in climate finance by 2030, with a significant portion directed to developing markets. ALTERRA already counts BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG as partners.KKR’s climate strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, covers renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. KKR manages over $100 billion in assets and has invested more than $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable investments include Zenobe, EGC, Avantus and IGNIS P2X. The collaboration with ALTERRA could help scale technologies and improve energy security and decarbonization.Gailliot and Lagarrigue see strong potential to combine ALTERRA’s reach with KKR’s infrastructure know-how to advance decarbonization across sectors.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) FUNDS STARITAS AS IT BECOMES STANDALONE GROWTH VEHICLE

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through Accel-KKR to support the spin-out from ECR as it pursues growth opportunities in digital technology markets. Staritas will operate as an independent company, receiving resources and guidance from Accel-KKR and KKR to accelerate development and market entry in the digital space. The move highlights investor interest in technology-driven firms able to adapt quickly and scale within competitive markets.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT TO LEAD GLOBAL REAL ESTATE CREDIT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut to Lead Global Real Estate CreditTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending within KKR's real estate division, effective Apr. 20, 2026, as the firm scales its real estate credit platform. Traut has led the unit since 2015, growing it from a seed investment of $400 million to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by end-2025.Traut's leadership comes as KKR invests nearly $15 billion annually across loans and securities in the U.S. and Europe and expands its European real estate credit business with support from Ali Imraan and a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.Paul Fine is named head of originations, succeeding Traut. Fine, a former managing director, will work to deepen client relationships and widen KKR's capital pools. Since 2017 the firm has expanded its capital pools from two to eight, including the $850 million closed for the KKR Opportunistic Real Estate Credit Fund II in February 2025.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) APPOINTS TRIO AS CO-PRESIDENTS TO DRIVE UNIFIED DECISION-MAKING

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) appoints three senior executives as co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective after 22nd April 2026, to accelerate decision-making and align operations with its evolving strategy.The co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and day-to-day management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s businesses, with the goal of improving execution as market conditions shift.Schwartz, who assumed leadership during a transitional period, seeks clearer accountability and faster decisions, a model that aligns with practices at other major firms to maintain momentum.The firm will assess the impact on investment activity and financial performance in forthcoming quarters.

+ CARLYLE STRENGTHENS WEALTH-MANAGEMENT FOOTPRINT WITH MAI CAPITAL ACQUISITION

Carlyle Group is set to expand its wealth-management footprint by acquiring a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at over $2.8 billion. The deal was announced on 31st March 2026.As part of the agreement, MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake, keeping MAI independent under Rick Buoncore, who leads as chairman and chief executive.The investment aims to bolster MAI's capital base and growth in financial planning, investment management and family-office services. MAI reports $72.6 billion in assets under management or administration as of Jan 1 across 40 U.S. offices.Closing is expected in the second quarter of 2026, subject to standard regulatory approvals, with Carlyle leveraging its global platform to support continuity for clients and advisors.

+ MAI CAPITAL TO SELL MAJORITY OWNERSHIP TO CARLYLE FOR ABOUT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has reached an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The move will give Carlyle majority ownership as Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit, while MAI employees retain a significant minority stake. Carlyle had previously invested in MAI in 2021 via Galway Holdings. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI oversees $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 U.S. offices and employs over 700 people. The deal underscores Carlyle's continued expansion even as its stock has been volatile; the firm reports strong liquidity and a 15-year dividend history at about 3%. Analysts rate Carlyle’s overall financial health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, MAI’s chairman and CEO, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, added, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

+ OAKTREE, NORTH MILL EXECUTE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization, OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026, reflecting healthy investor demand for quality equipment finance assets.The securitized assets originated from Midland Equipment Finance in a November 2025 deal. The notes were rated AAA through A and drew strong demand, about 6.3 times subscriptions, while delivering a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a 4.7% yield.Truist Securities led structuring with Deutsche Bank as co-manager. Brendan Beer of Oaktree noted the market reception as evidence of investor confidence in the strategy and in North Mill's role.North Mill's David Lee emphasized asset quality and the servicing platform, reiterating that NMEF will continue as the primary servicer for the assets, ensuring ongoing management.

+ OAKTREE NAVIGATES PRIVATE CREDIT PRESSURES BY FULFILLING $7.7B REDEMPTIONS AND CUTTING DIVIDEND

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would fulfill all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion, allowing withdrawals up to 8.5% of net assets.Brookfield will contribute roughly $80 million to support the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund will repurchase 6.8% of its shares, with Brookfield buying an additional 1.7% from a single investor; the fund's dividend will be cut by two cents to 16 cents.As of 23rd March 2026, liquidity stood at $1.8 billion from cash and undrawn facilities, while the firm has sold publicly traded loans and bonds to reduce exposure to software investments. The broader private credit sector remains under pressure, with peers such as Blackstone raising buyback terms to 7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC AT $9.62 BILLION POST-MONEY VALUATION WITH $1.25 BILLION FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC secures $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., delivering a 13% stake via convertible preferred shares. The investment was formalized on April 20, 2026 and is contingent on regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The post-money valuation stands at about $9.62 billion, and the capital will be used to bolster operations and expand the company’s market footprint as Apollo provides strategic backing through its funds.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY INVESTMENT FOR 13% MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS STAKE

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The capital supports McKesson's planned separation of the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and funds strategic initiatives within the business. The transaction reflects a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the latter moves toward separating its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and concentrating on core operations. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating market expectations for continued growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will watch the arrangement for potential impacts on McKesson's separation plan.

+ APOLLO BROADENS UK HOUSING FOOTPRINT VIA GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP PURCHASE

Apollo Global Management Inc. has acquired Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, completed on 1st April 2026, bringing Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties into its UK housing platform.Financial terms were not disclosed.GLG is a Build-to-Rent single-family housing leader with more than 10,000 properties and 5,000 homes acquired, and will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell with Ascend Properties handling external portfolio management.The deal strengthens Apollo's UK strategy and follows its existing interests in Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans, while Gatehouse Bank focuses on Shariah-compliant financing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) APPOINTS ASIA LEADERS AND ACQUIRES WHITESTONE REIT TO DIVERSIFY REAL ASSETS

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a US$1.7 billion transaction, taking private a retail-focused REIT and expanding essential consumer-focused centers across U.S. metros. On Apr. 14, 2026, E.G. Morse was named Partner and Head of Asia Credit, with Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong leading the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring Ares' intent to bolster Asia-focused credit strategies. The Whitestone acquisition broadens Ares' real estate, private credit, and real assets exposure and supports diversification across geographies. Investors should monitor integration progress and Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape, while considering risks such as margin pressure and elevated debt; the 5.38% yield is not fully covered by earnings

+ WHITESTONE REIT TO BECOME PRIVATE AS ARES COMPLETES $1.7B ACQUISITION

On Apr. 10, 2026, Ares Management announced plans to acquire Whitestone REIT in an all-cash deal valued at about $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. The transaction will render Whitestone private and remove it from the NYSE. Whitestone’s 56 properties total around 4.9 million square feet across Sun Belt markets such as Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. The move aims to broaden Ares Real Estate’s footprint with necessity-based retail centers in high-demand Arizona and Texas markets. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman stated the company’s strategy centers on high-return, smaller spaces with a diversified tenant base, adding that the agreement with Ares validates this approach for shareholders. Ares’ global head of real estate strategy and growth, David Roth, emphasized the appeal of New Economy real estate and convenient consumer experiences, highlighting the growing demand for grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness and dining options.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT DEPLOYS $5.4 BILLION TO ACCELERATE VALUE-ADD REAL ESTATE RECOVERY

Ares Management Corporation has secured about $5.4 billion for value-add real estate investments in the United States and Europe, a move aimed at capitalizing on supply-constrained markets likely to rebound soon, with a focus on logistics, multifamily, and self-storage sectors.The fundraising includes $1.9 billion for the EPEP IV fund and roughly $3.5 billion for the U.S. value-add program, with initial investments identified or deployed totaling around $1.1 billion on each track.Analysts are broadly supportive; about 75% are bullish, and the consensus price target sits near $161, suggesting roughly 60% upside. However, on 24th March 2026, BMO Capital reduced its target to $112 from $140 and issued a Market Perform rating amid concerns about BDC redemptions and broader market volatility.Ares, founded as a global alternative investment manager, had BlackRock as a notable backer with approximately 7.12% stake at end-2025, underscoring strong institutional support. The note also reminds investors that AI-related equities may offer greater upside in some scenarios, reinforcing the case for diversification.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESTARTS FREIGHTER AND PASSENGER LINKS TO UAE ON EXPANDED NETWORK

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed both freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after safety concerns. The new schedule confirms freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly from 26th April and to Sharjah (SHJ) three times weekly from 1st May, while daily passenger services to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with freighter reach planned to exceed 50 destinations by 1st May and the overall passenger network expected to top 150 by 16th June.Urgent freight restrictions have been lifted on routes such as Victoria, Australia, with the carrier introducing special handling codes QRLIV for fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, and LHO for live human organs and blood, effective 1st May.Reservations remain accessible via the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, alongside standard sales channels. The resumption is described as a key milestone for regional logistics and supports ongoing expansion.Industry stakeholders are watching the pace of growth, as the UAE freight market benefits from improved connectivity and service levels.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS INTRODUCES NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE, AIMING TO SUSTAIN PREMIUM EDGE

Qatar Airways announced the Qsuite Next Generation, with a launch planned later this year on the Airbus A350-1000 after delays with the 777-9, as of 23rd April 2026. The Next Generation builds on the iconic Qsuite introduced in 2017, maintaining sliding doors, double beds, and modular seating while adding a movable 4K OLED screen and stronger privacy options. The initiative reinforces Qatar Airways' premium strategy and its Doha hub, where exclusive check-in, fast-track security, and premium lounges contribute to a seamless travel experience. By investing in product development, the airline seeks to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure customers as competition in luxury cabins intensifies.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS SETS OUT PHASED EMBARGO LIFTS DURING RECOVERY

Qatar Airways is steadily expanding its operations as it rebuilds its network, with passenger routes restored to more than 150 destinations and freighter coverage over 50 locations as of April 20, 2026.The restoration is part of a wider plan to increase capacity amid rising air freight demand. Some shipments remain embargoed, with AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes restricted, and live organs and blood not accepted.QRLIV shipments can be booked under different codes, as long as Doha transit time stays within 24 hours. Other products remain bookable via standard sales channels, and the carrier emphasizes adaptability and a commitment to service as it expands.

urn:contentItem:6JH6-0NJ3-SHTX-R4VH-00000-00Letters for Tuesday, May 5, 20262026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisOPINIONLetters for Tuesday, May 5, 2026Staff Writer

Protect health with heat action plan

The April 26 Sunday edition published an article, ("Distressing reversal,") about the American Lung Association's 2026 "State of the Air" report that recommends government public health policies that protect our neighbors from respiratory illnesses.

The article included a warning from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) 2023 assessment that, "climate change is projected to worsen air quality in many U.S. regions thereby harming human health and increasing premature death."

I think our state and county health departments need to implement the NYS 2024-2030 Extreme Heat Action Plan by, "addressing extreme heat and mitigating its impacts across New York state, and especially in vulnerable and disadvantaged communities."

The Healthy Capital District's 2025 Community Health Needs Assessment identifies some of our neighbors suffer with chronic obstructive pulmonary diseases including asthmatic illnesses.

To address the assessment municipal EMS agencies could submit a regional mutual-aid proposal through the state consolidated funding application process to enact the 2025-2028 Strategic Plan Goals of the NYS Division of Homeland Security and Emergency Services (DHSES).

With the award of state funds, the EMS designated facilities could purchase and install critical renewable energy systems with energy-efficient medical equipment.

I agree with Tom Ellis' April 26 letter, ("Congress must act to improve health care,") advocating us to persuade our government officials to protect public health issues with essential improvements of health care services.

I hope we will persuade our public health officials to implement the state Extreme Heat Action Plan at all EMS community shelters and medical services facilities.

Michael McGlynn

Watervliet

Solar legislation will have big cost impact

New York is considering the ASAP Act the Accelerate Solar for Affordable Power Act which would build 20 gigawatts of distributed solar by 2035.

It's worth paying attention to, because it has real implications for energy bills right here in Columbia County.

When someone mentions solar, it's easy to picture panels blanketing our hillsides. That concern is legitimate but it's worth separating from the real question.

The solar that delivers the biggest ratepayer benefit isn't utility-scale projects on open farmland. It's rooftop solar and community solar on already-disturbed land parking lots, brownfields, low-value parcels. If you're worried about landscape impacts, that fight is worth having. But it's a different fight.

On costs: I hear people say clean energy will raise their bills. It's an understandable worry, but the data says otherwise.

Solar is now the cheapest source of electricity generation in the world and already New York's most successful clean energy sector, hitting state targets ahead of schedule and under budget.

Synapse Energy Economics found that meeting New York's solar and storage targets would save ratepayers more than $1 billion every single year, flowing through the wholesale market to everyone not just households with rooftop panels.

Compare that to fossil fuels, where every global conflict spikes our heating bills and electricity costs. Solar runs on sunlight. No foreign government controls it and the fuel is free.

Columbia County doesn't have to choose between protecting what makes this place special and lowering energy costs.

Cara Humphrey

Chatham

Letters

The Gazette welcomes letters from all political points of view.

Regular letters must be no longer than 250 words. Election-related letters are limited to 200 words.

Letter writers may only submit one letter every 30 days.

We reserve the right to edit for grammar, style, length, libel, accuracy and offensive language.

urn:contentItem:6JH6-BCP3-RWXD-81F4-00000-00Letters for Tuesday, May 5, 20262026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisOPINIONLetters for Tuesday, May 5, 2026Staff Writer

Protect health with heat action plan

The April 26 Sunday edition published an article, ("Distressing reversal,") about the American Lung Association's 2026 "State of the Air" report that recommends government public health policies that protect our neighbors from respiratory illnesses.

The article included a warning from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) 2023 assessment that, "climate change is projected to worsen air quality in many U.S. regions thereby harming human health and increasing premature death."

I think our state and county health departments need to implement the NYS 2024-2030 Extreme Heat Action Plan by, "addressing extreme heat and mitigating its impacts across New York state, and especially in vulnerable and disadvantaged communities."

The Healthy Capital District's 2025 Community Health Needs Assessment identifies some of our neighbors suffer with chronic obstructive pulmonary diseases including asthmatic illnesses.

To address the assessment municipal EMS agencies could submit a regional mutual-aid proposal through the state consolidated funding application process to enact the 2025-2028 Strategic Plan Goals of the NYS Division of Homeland Security and Emergency Services (DHSES).

With the award of state funds, the EMS designated facilities could purchase and install critical renewable energy systems with energy-efficient medical equipment.

I agree with Tom Ellis' April 26 letter, ("Congress must act to improve health care,") advocating us to persuade our government officials to protect public health issues with essential improvements of health care services.

I hope we will persuade our public health officials to implement the state Extreme Heat Action Plan at all EMS community shelters and medical services facilities.

Michael McGlynn

Watervliet

Solar legislation will have big cost impact

New York is considering the ASAP Act the Accelerate Solar for Affordable Power Act which would build 20 gigawatts of distributed solar by 2035.

It's worth paying attention to, because it has real implications for energy bills right here in Columbia County.

When someone mentions solar, it's easy to picture panels blanketing our hillsides. That concern is legitimate but it's worth separating from the real question.

The solar that delivers the biggest ratepayer benefit isn't utility-scale projects on open farmland. It's rooftop solar and community solar on already-disturbed land parking lots, brownfields, low-value parcels. If you're worried about landscape impacts, that fight is worth having. But it's a different fight.

On costs: I hear people say clean energy will raise their bills. It's an understandable worry, but the data says otherwise.

Solar is now the cheapest source of electricity generation in the world and already New York's most successful clean energy sector, hitting state targets ahead of schedule and under budget.

Synapse Energy Economics found that meeting New York's solar and storage targets would save ratepayers more than $1 billion every single year, flowing through the wholesale market to everyone not just households with rooftop panels.

Compare that to fossil fuels, where every global conflict spikes our heating bills and electricity costs. Solar runs on sunlight. No foreign government controls it and the fuel is free.

Columbia County doesn't have to choose between protecting what makes this place special and lowering energy costs.

Cara Humphrey

Chatham

Letters

The Gazette welcomes letters from all political points of view.

Regular letters must be no longer than 250 words. Election-related letters are limited to 200 words.

Letter writers may only submit one letter every 30 days.

We reserve the right to edit for grammar, style, length, libel, accuracy and offensive language.
