urn:contentItem:6JK7-N563-RRKF-P2DS-00000-00GNW : Casella Waste Systems and Waga Energy Unveil RNG Facility at Ribbon2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:36ZLexisNexisADHOC-NEWSGNW : Casella Waste Systems and Waga Energy Unveil RNG Facility at Ribbon

Cutting

RUTLAND, Vt., May 14, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Casella Waste Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: CWST), a regional solid waste, recycling and resource management services company in the Eastern United States, and Waga Energy (EPA: WAGA), a global expert in the production of Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) from landfills, held a ribbon-cutting ceremony on May 14, 2026, to celebrate the opening of the RNG production facility at the Chemung County Landfill in Elmira, New York. The facility, which has been successfully turning landfill gas into RNG since January 2026, represents a key milestone in Casella's efforts to create valuable resources from waste.

"We're excited to celebrate the opening of this facility as it represents a key piece of our strategy coming to life," saidNed Coletta, President and CEO ofCasella Waste Systems, Inc. "We made the decision several years ago to find great partners to develop these RNG projects at our facilities rather than invest the capital and embark on projects that are outside the scope of our expertise. This project is a great example of the benefits of that approach as we see the collective expertise of Chemung County, Waga Energy, and our landfill operations team coming together to create value out of the waste we manage."

The facility leverages the WAGABOX(R) technology developed and patented by Waga Energy to upgrade landfill gas into pipeline-quality RNG. The RNG produced on-site is injected directly into the Valley Energy natural gas distribution network, supplying the region with a renewable alternative to fossil natural gas. The facility is designed to generate up to 610,000 MMBtu of renewable gas annually and is expected to avoid more than 47,000 tons of CO2-equivalent emissions each year, according to U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) standards1. Through its first four months, the facility has performed well through its ramp up phase, and is projected to produce 340,000 MMBtu in year one which is equivalent to 2.4 million gallons of diesel fuel.

"We are honored to celebrate this inauguration alongside Casella, a company whose commitment to sustainability and environmental stewardship is truly exemplary," said Guénaël Prince, Chief Executive Officer of Waga Energy Inc. "The successful commissioning of the Chemung facility demonstrates the strength of our partnership and Casella's leadership in renewable energy development. This project showcases how innovative collaboration can turn waste into a valuable resource for the energy transition, benefiting both the environment and local communities."

Under the terms of the agreement, Waga Energy deployed the capital required to fully fund the construction of the facility and will own and operate the facility for 20 years, while Casella and Waga Energy share the revenue generated from RNG sales.

_______________ 1Landfill Gas Energy Benefits Calculator | US EPA

About Casella Waste Systems, Inc.

Casella Waste Systems, Inc., headquartered in Rutland, Vermont, provides resource management expertise and services to residential, commercial, municipal, institutional and industrial customers, primarily in the areas of solid waste collection and disposal, transfer, recycling and organics services in the eastern United States. For more information, visit www.casella.com.

About Waga Energy

Waga Energy produces competitively priced Renewable Natural Gas (RNG, also known as biomethane) by upgrading landfill gas using a patented purification technology called WAGABOX(R). The RNG produced is injected directly into the gas grids that supply individuals and businesses, providing a substitute for natural fossil gas. Waga Energy currently operates 36 RNG production units in France, Spain, Canada and the USA, representing an installed capacity of more than 6.5 million MMBtu (1.9 TWh) per year. To date, WagaEnergy has 19 RNG production units under construction worldwide. Each project initiated by WagaEnergy contributes to the fight against global warming and helps the energy transition. Waga Energy is listed on Euronext Paris (FR001253281 EPA: WAGA).

SAFE HARBOR STATEMENT

Certain matters discussed in this press release, including but not limited to, the statements regarding our intentions, beliefs or current expectations concerning, among other things, projections as to the anticipated benefits of the commercial agreement, the anticipated amounts of renewable natural gas to be produced and the anticipated impact of the commercial agreement and the renewable natural gas facilities on the Company's business and future financial and operating results are "forward-looking statements". These forward-looking statements can generally be identified as such by the context of the statements, including words such as "believe," "expect," "anticipate," "plan," "may," "would," "intend," "estimate," "will," "guidance" and other similar expressions, whether in the negative or affirmative. These forward-looking statements are based on current expectations, estimates, forecasts and projections about the industry and markets in which the Company operates and management's beliefs and assumptions. The Company cannot guarantee that it actually will achieve the financial results, plans, intentions, expectations or guidance disclosed in the forward-looking statements made. Such forward-looking statements, and all phases of the Company's operations, involve a number of risks and uncertainties, any one or more of which could cause actual results to differ materially from those described in its forward-looking statements.

Such risks and uncertainties include or relate to, among other things, the following: project development timelines may extend past anticipated schedules; the Company may not fully recognize the expected financial benefits from the RNG facilities due to operational challenges, gas production levels, market or economic factors outside its control which may impact revenues and costs, or for other reasons; and potential regulatory changes could adversely impact operations.

There are a number of other important risks and uncertainties that could cause the Company's actual results to differ materially from those indicated by such forward-looking statements. These additional risks and uncertainties include, without limitation, those detailed in Item 1A. "Risk Factors" in the Company's most recently filed Form 1K for the fiscal year endedDecember 31, 2025, and in other filings that the Company may make with theSecurities and Exchange Commissionin the future.

The Company undertakes no obligation to update publicly any forward-looking statements whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by law.

urn:contentItem:6JK4-CP13-RS50-G30T-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ PILLSBURY OPENS BOSTON OFFICE, EXPANDING NATIONAL FOOTPRINT

On 12th May 2026, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman announced it would open an office in Boston, expanding its footprint in one of the United States' largest legal and business markets. The launch will commence with a team of five senior lawyers, including a litigator recruited from Latham & Watkins.Robert Gilbert, who joined Pillsbury after seven years at Latham, specializes in representing policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters. He has advised Boston-area clients such as Harvard University, UniFirst Corporation, and Northland Investment Corporation. Gilbert will help launch the Boston office alongside Pillsbury leaders Michael Zeliger, Andrew Troop, Don Lonczak, and David Simons.David Dekker, chair and CEO of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, underscored the strategic value of the Boston outpost, citing the city's dynamic startup ecosystem, leading research institutions, and a dense network of financial services and private capital. He said, "Pillsbury has long represented clients headquartered in the Boston area and those connected to the Boston-Cambridge innovation ecosystem. Opening a Boston office is a natural next step given our capabilities and relationships in the tech, life sciences, healthcare, and financial industries in Boston and around the world." This move marks Pillsbury’s 17th location in the United States and its 25th globally. The Boston launch places the firm among other major law firms expanding in the city, including Reed Smith, Freshfields, and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett, as Boston broadens its powerhouse mix of technology, life sciences, and finance. With the Boston office, Pillsbury aims to deepen engagement with local clients and industries, reinforcing its role in the evolving regional legal landscape.

+ MORGAN LEWIS LANDS PILLSBURY VETERAN TO STRENGTHEN EMPLOYEE BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team, a move designed to address increasing complexities in benefits law and the growing need for specialized guidance.The attorney brings broad experience in compliance and regulatory matters across employee benefits, and is expected to enhance Morgan Lewis' ability to provide comprehensive services to clients. Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire reinforces the firm's ongoing strategy to expand its practice areas as client needs evolve.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST-PERKINS COIE MERGER SECURES STRONG PARTNER BACKING

The merger of Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie has won overwhelming support from the partnerships of both firms. The decision was confirmed after a consultation period, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026. The deal is expected to close in the third quarter of the year, subject to customary closing conditions.Perkins Coie's Mark Birnbaum said the approval reflects partners' commitment to creating a forward looking firm and a shared confidence in their ability to grow and deliver high quality legal services in a changing global market. Ashurst chief executive Paul Jenkins stressed the alignment between the two practices and that combining strengths will benefit clients.The merged firm will focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services. Perkins Coie firmwide managing partner Bill Malley expressed optimism that the combination will improve the firms capacity to handle complex cross border matters. The new entity will count roughly 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York.The merger first announced last year brings together two law firms with combined revenues of around USD 2.8 billion. Their lawyers have wide international experience, having worked in Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The leadership of the new firm will be shared by Malley and Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Ashurst global chair Karen Davies and Perkins Coie corporate practice chair Brian Eiting will serve as co-chairs.

+ PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST SEAL MERGER TO CREATE ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union with complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will emerge as a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion. The new entity will count roughly 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, with flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. It will be noted for strengths in technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services sectors—industries pivotal to the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, said, 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform—one with the scale, sector depth, and technological leadership to meet our clients' increasingly cross-border needs.' Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, added, 'This vote confirms the strong alignment between our firms and our joint ambition for the future,' highlighting the complementary expertise across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. The merger will create a compelling platform for top legal talent.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026, positioning the new firm to navigate the complexities of the global market and deliver enhanced legal services.

+ PERKINS COIE OPENS SHENZHEN OFFICE, SIGNALING RETURN TO CHINA'S MARKET

Perkins Coie has opened a representative office in Shenzhen, marking its comeback in the Chinese market. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026, following the closure of its Beijing and Shanghai outposts in 2024 and a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to tap the city’s rapid growth as a technology and manufacturing hub. Leading the Shenzhen office are Bing Ai, chief representative and managing partner, and Wei Yuan, representative and litigation partner. Both are US-trained and bring substantial experience in advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, said the Shenzhen move reinforces the firm's commitment to providing US legal expertise across borders and helping clients navigate global markets amid rising cross-border challenges. The development also unfolds as several international firms push deeper into Shenzhen's Greater Bay Area, even as Perkins Coie weighs a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst that could create a larger global platform.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY SECURES $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING FROM COOLEY LLP AND LEADING FIRMS

Crosby, a contract review start-up, has raised $60 million in its latest Series B, marking the company’s third capital raise in under a year. The funding announcement was made on April 3, 2026, with Cooley LLP among the investors backing Crosby.The round was co-led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors and Bain Capital Ventures, along with angel investor Elad Gil. The fresh capital will support product development and scale Crosby’s platform to serve clients such as Clay, Rogo and Ramp, a financial services operations platform.Crosby fuses artificial intelligence with legal expertise to accelerate contract reviews. The company says the value of negotiated contracts has surpassed $1 billion, a marked jump from roughly $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby noted, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has rolled out simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate on behalf of clients, and a dashboard that lets clients oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and tech lead for Ramp.

+ COOLEY LLP EXPANDS TECHNOLOGY PRACTICE WITH FRESHFIELDS ALUMNI

Cooley LLP has announced a significant expansion of its technology practice, bringing on board several attorneys who previously worked at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. This strategic move, aimed at bolstering Cooley's capabilities in the tech sector, highlights the firm's commitment to remaining competitive in an increasingly dynamic legal landscape.As of 23rd November 2025, Cooley's latest recruitment drive underscores the firm's ongoing efforts to enhance its service offerings in technology, a sector that continues to evolve rapidly. The arrival of these high-profile Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to not only strengthen Cooley's existing practice but also attract new clients seeking expert legal guidance in technology-related matters.The new team members bring a wealth of experience, having advised prominent clients on a range of legal issues, including intellectual property, data protection, and corporate transactions in the tech industry. Their expertise is anticipated to complement Cooley’s established reputation in venture capital and startup advisory services, further positioning the firm as a leading player in the technology legal sector.Cooley LLP’s decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader trend within the legal industry, where firms are actively seeking to acquire top talent to respond to the demands of a fast-paced, technology-driven market. As legal challenges in the tech sector become more complex, the need for specialized knowledge and skills is paramount, making these strategic hires particularly timely.With this expansion, Cooley LLP aims to solidify its standing as a go-to destination for clients in the technology field, reinforcing its capabilities in an area that is critical for innovation and growth. As the firm integrates its new attorneys, stakeholders will be watching closely to see how these changes impact Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH ENABLES £11 MILLION ACQUISITION OF SOFTWARE FIRM LED BY FOUNDER

Reed Smith LLP guided the founder through an £11 million acquisition of a software business, with completion confirmed on 8th of May 2026. The arrangement underscores the firm's expertise in corporate law and its advisory reach within technology ventures.The founder aims to strengthen operations and spur innovation, while Reed Smith ensured regulatory compliance and facilitated negotiations between the parties. The firm's involvement signals its commitment to supporting entrepreneurial deals in the fast moving software sector.

+ REED SMITH HIRES STINSON REAL ESTATE FINANCE ATTORNEY TO BOOST DC PRACTICE

ReedSmith LLP has announced the hire of a seasoned real estate finance attorney from Stinson LLP to its Washington, D.C. office, a move designed to strengthen its capabilities in a competitive market. The new hire brings deep experience in financing, acquisition and development, which will bolster the firm's real estate and finance practices and help clients navigate complex transactions.The firm says the recruit aligns with its strategy to grow the capital's real estate practice, with the D.C. office already a focal point for property work. The addition is expected to broaden Reed Smith's client roster and project portfolio while enabling more integrated solutions for borrowers, lenders and developers.With regulatory scrutiny rising and market dynamics evolving, Reed Smith says the hire will help the firm respond more effectively to client needs. The firm continues to emphasize its commitment to attracting top talent and expanding capabilities in real estate and finance as of 7th May 2026.

+ REED SMITH ADDS PRIVATE EQUITY PARTNER IN LOS ANGELES TO EXPAND PRACTICE

ReedSmith LLP has added a new private equity partner in its Los Angeles office, reinforcing its presence in a key market for investments. The hire broadens the firm's private equity capabilities and aligns with its long-term strategy to serve investment firms and corporate clients involved in mergers and acquisitions.The partner joins on 4th May 2026 and brings extensive experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services. The addition is designed to help Reed Smith navigate complex deals and deliver tailored legal solutions as demand in the region continues to rise.This appointment underscores Reed Smith's commitment to high-quality service and enduring client relationships as private equity activity grows on the West Coast.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOORBIT SECURES $13.2M SEED ROUND; WILSON SONSINI ADVISES SPACE PHARMA STARTUP

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. advised BioOrbit on its $13.2 million seed financing, announced on 30th April 2026. The round was co-led by LocalGlobe and BREEGA, with participation from Seedcamp, Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund, Type One Ventures, 7percent Ventures and other investors.BioOrbit, led by Cambridge scientist Dr. Katie King and oncology researcher Dr. Leonor Teles, is aiming to build a space-based pharmaceuticals factory within the next decade. The company is developing hardware to enable large-scale crystallization of protein drugs, a key step in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.Wilson Sonsini's team — Daniel Glazer, Jose Campos, Rashad Mohammed and Sophia Kunthara — provided corporate and emerging-company counsel to BioOrbit throughout the financing, helping position the startup for its ambitious plans in space manufacturing.

+ WILSON SONSINI GUIDES OFF PISTE CAPITAL THROUGH INAUGURAL FUND LAUNCH

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. announced its advisory role in the launch of Off Piste Capital's inaugural fund. Off Piste Fund I closed on 29th April 2026 with backing from strategic limited partners including Edition Ventures.The fund targets companies building the systems and infrastructure of the future and has already invested in four startups: Stendr in Norway, Objection in the UK, Beyond Reach Labs in the US, and Octet in the UK.Wilson Sonsini's deal team including Bradley Doline, Alice Cao, and Stephanie Yasay guided fund formation and closing. The collaboration underscores the firm's commitment to supporting emerging venture activity, and with Fund I now live Off Piste plans to back more innovative solutions. Details are available via Off Piste's LinkedIn and EU Startups coverage.

+ WILSON SONSINI EXPANDS ENERGY AND CLIMATE SOLUTIONS TEAM WITH KEN SANOCKI

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati named Ken Sanocki a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice, reinforcing the firm's capacity to advise on energy infrastructure financing amid the transition to cleaner power.Sanocki comes from Sunrun, where he served as vice president of legal and oversaw all project-finance matters, including tax equity financings and complex debt structures for large distributed-energy projects. He led more than 50 tax equity financings totaling more than $10 billion.Doug Clark, Wilson Sonsini's managing partner, said, "Ken has operated at the center of the energy transition, helping shape the financing structures that have enabled distributed energy to scale into mainstream infrastructure." In his new role, Sanocki will counsel clients on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure, and assist with capital formation, strategic transactions, and potential IPOs or M&A.Sanocki began his career at Milbank and most recently was senior counsel for global structured and project finance at SunEdison. He holds a J.D. from NYU School of Law and a B.A. from Boston College. His arrival, alongside recent partner additions, positions the firm to lead energy-transition finance and clean-energy advisory.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN ADVISES ON CLOSURE OF $1.3 BILLION MULTIFAMILY S3 FUND

Sidley Austin has advised on the closure of a $1.3 billion multifamily-focused S3 fund, marking a notable milestone for the real estate investment sector. The announcement, dated 7th May 2026, underscores growing demand for multifamily housing driven by demographic shifts and urbanization. The S3 vehicle is designed to target multifamily properties and is expected to attract robust investor interest as housing needs evolve. With urban areas seeing higher population density, multifamily assets are viewed as stable and lucrative, and Sidley's work on its formation and compliance highlights the firm's strengths in real estate financing. The firm has built a reputation for addressing the unique challenges of real estate investments, guiding clients through complex regulatory requirements and capital-raising processes. The successful close of the $1.3 billion fund reinforces Sidley's position in the market as it continues to advise on high-value deals in the multifamily sector.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN ADVISES TELIX IN USD 2.1 BILLION REGENERON COLLABORATION

Sidley Austin has advised Telix Pharmaceuticals on its strategic collaboration with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion. The agreement will have Telix and Regeneron co-developing and co-commercialising next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies under a 50:50 cost- and profit-sharing model, with Telix receiving USD 40 million upfront for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the partnership emphasizes joint governance and shared development and commercialization responsibilities designed to leverage both companies' oncology platforms. The collaboration aims to speed the advancement of radiopharmaceuticals in cancer treatment by combining Regeneron's oncology capabilities with Telix's radiopharmaceutical pipeline. Joshua Hofheimer, a partner at Sidley Austin, remarked that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The deal underscores Telix's expansion strategy and reflects the growing role of law firms like Sidley Austin in guiding complex, cross-border biopharma collaborations as regulatory landscapes evolve.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN REPORTS 33% CITY REVENUE INCREASE ON STRATEGIC HIRING

Sidley Austin has reported a 33% rise in City revenue, a result the firm attributes to its strategic hiring, with the trend noted as of 14th April 2026. Management says the recruitment of top-tier talent has broadened the firm's City teams, expanding service offerings and driving higher billable hours and client retention. Analysts say the move reflects a broader shift toward investing in human capital to stay competitive, and stakeholders will watch how the strategy translates into sustained performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ MORRISON FOERSTER EXPANDS TECH M&A TEAM WITH THREE PARTNER ADDITIONS

Morrison Foerster announced the addition of partners Mike Pierides, Will Holder, and Oliver Bell to its global technology transactions and technology-focused M&A platform, bringing more than 55 years of combined experience across outsourcing, data privacy and cybersecurity, GPU-as-a-service agreements, licensing, and technology-as-a-service mandates. The firm made this announcement on the 9th of May, 2026.Pierides focuses on technology and commercial transactions, advising clients across AI, data centers, fintech, aviation, telecommunications, and financial services. His practice covers outsourcing, data privacy and cybersecurity, strategic restructurings, and technology-as-a-service mandates.Holder strengthens Morrison Foerster's technology and media-focused M&A practice, spanning public and private M&A, private equity transactions, joint ventures, and corporate matters, with work across technology, music, sports, and media, fintech, and consumer sectors. Bell will join in the coming weeks, bringing experience in technology-led transactions, large-scale IT outsourcing agreements, and cybersecurity. The hires are part of the firm's strategy to bolster cross-border technology transactions and AI-related capabilities and support its London office growth strategy. They mark the eighth, ninth, and tenth partner additions in Morrison Foerster's London office over the past year. Eric McCrath, Chair of Morrison Foerster, said the hires reflect continued investment in core tech M&A capabilities, and Andrew Boyd, the London office managing partner, highlighted the synergies with the firm's technology strengths.

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER ANALYZES SENATE BAN ON PREDICTION MARKET TRADING

The Senate unanimously approved a resolution on 29th April 2026 banning Senators and their staff from participating in prediction markets. Morrison & Foerster notes that the move is part of a broader effort to regulate these markets, which have drawn both enthusiasm and skepticism.Hours after the resolution, Senators Kristen Gillibrand (D-NY) and Dave McCormick (R-PA) unveiled legislation to bar government officials from trading on prediction markets. The proposed bill would set standards for insider trading, introduce anti-money-laundering measures, and create an independent Office of the Retail Advocate within the CFTC, signaling a tighter regulatory environment. In parallel, Kalshi announced features intended to restrict underage access, including mandatory face ID and two-factor authentication for parents, aligning with the proposed framework and aiming to bolster consumer trust.A Senate hearing on prediction markets and sports betting is scheduled for 20th May 2026, with Former Representative Patrick McHenry slated to testify alongside industry representatives. CFTC Chairman Michael Selig has defended the commission’s exclusive jurisdiction in a Wall Street Journal letter, describing them as innovative financial products rather than gambling sites, a stance Morrison & Foerster says is critical to sustaining legitimacy amid heightened scrutiny.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISES ENTER ON $100 MILLION SERIES B, PAVING THE WAY TO LATIN AMERICA'S AI UNICORN STATUS

SAO PAULO, May 8, 2026 — Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented Enter, a Brazil-based artificial intelligence legal technology company, in its Series B financing that raised over $100 million and valued Enter at approximately $1.2 billion, effectively making it the first AI unicorn in Latin America. The financing was led by Founders Fund, with significant participation from Sequoia Capital, Ribbit Capital, Kaszek, Atlantico and ONEVC. Enter, founded in 2023, has grown to serve more than 40 clients, including Airbnb, Nubank, Mercado Libre and Latam Airlines. Enter uses AI to automate litigation tasks—from evidence discovery to drafting responses and negotiating settlements—and these processes are reviewed by Enter’s legal professionals. The Greenberg Traurig deal team was led by Matthew Squires, and included Maurice Guttmann, L. Frank Cordero, Agata Jacqueline Paramesvari and Nathalya Zara. Greenberg Traurig, with about 3,100 lawyers in 51 offices, is consistently recognized in the Am Law Global 100 and emphasizes philanthropy, culture and pro bono work.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG BOLSTERS CHICAGO FINANCE TEAM WITH GERARD C. MARTIN

On 7th May 2026, Greenberg Traurig, LLP announced the expansion of its Chicago office with the appointment of Gerard C. Martin as a shareholder in the Restructuring & Special Situations Practice. Martin joins from Reed Smith LLP, bringing extensive experience in financing transactions and debt restructurings. His practice spans cash-flow and asset-based loans, leveraged finance, acquisition financings, complex intercreditor arrangements, and equipment and aviation finance, including aircraft and engine financings and project finance matters. He also focuses on financial restructurings, advising secured creditors and debtors in in-court and out-of-court matters, including debtor-in-possession financing and distressed investing. Martin said: "Greenberg Traurig is an ideal fit for my practice, and the firm’s platform aligns with my work in financing and restructuring." Co-chairs Shari L. Heyen and Oscar N. Pinkas added that his arrival amplifies the firm’s capabilities, and noted the Chicago office of over 200 attorneys is well positioned to serve clients through the credit cycle.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG REPRESENTS SELLER IN MAJOR KEIHANNA HYPERSCALE DATA CENTER SALE IN JAPAN

Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP advised the seller in the sale of a stabilized 40-megawatt hyperscale data center located in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. The deal closed on 7th May 2026 and stands among the largest asset-level sales of a fully leased hyperscale facility in the Japanese market. The data center is leased to a single US hyperscale cloud provider. The seller was a joint venture between a fund sponsored by a US institutional manager and a fund sponsored by a Japanese trading company. The buyer is a consortium led by CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, which acquired a 49% interest, with the remaining 51% going to a fund managed by an affiliate of the Japanese trading company. This is the second Japanese hyperscale data center sale in which Greenberg Traurig has advised this client, following a prior transaction completed in November. The firm's Digital Infrastructure Industry Group, with more than 110 attorneys worldwide, continues to position the firm as a leader in this rapidly evolving sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESPERION THERAPEUTICS TO GO PRIVATE IN $1.1 BILLION DEAL, WITH SIDLEY AND GOODWIN LEADING THE TRANSACTION

Esperion Therapeutics has agreed to a $1.1 billion take-private transaction, with Sidley Austin LLP and Goodwin Procter LLP leading the legal work. The deal, announced on 1st May 2026, aims to shift the lipid-management specialist away from public market volatility and toward focus on its core programs. The arrangement is expected to reshape Esperion's operating framework as it pivots toward private ownership.The move reflects a broader trend in biotech toward private ownership as a means to streamline research and development and accelerate commercialization. Sidley and Goodwin's involvement underscores the deal's complexity and the firms' strength in mergers and acquisitions as Esperion transitions to a private structure.

+ FBT GIBBONS EXPANDS VENTURE CAPITAL PRACTICE WITH HIRE FROM GOODWIN PROCTER

FBT Gibbons announced the addition of a venture capital attorney from Goodwin Procter LLP to its New Jersey office, with the hire confirmed on 21st April 2026. The move is intended to strengthen the firm's venture capital capabilities and better serve clients in a competitive market. The attorney brings extensive experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, a recognized leader in venture capital work for startups and established companies alike, and FBT Gibbons plans to leverage that expertise to guide clients through fundraising and complex investment environments. This development aligns with a broader industry trend of law firms expanding niche practices in venture capital.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER GUIDES TOPSPIN TO $328 MILLION CONSUMER FUND CLOSE

Goodwin Procter LLP helped close Topspin Consumer Partners' latest fundraising, securing $328 million for a new consumer-focused vehicle, a notable development in private equity. On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the close, noting that Goodwin Procter provided essential legal support throughout the process.The fund is designed to capitalize on opportunities across the consumer sector, with Topspin aiming to identify and back innovative companies positioned to thrive in a dynamic market. Goodwin Procter’s guidance on regulatory matters and partnership structures is expected to enhance operational efficiency and investor confidence.This collaboration underscores Goodwin Procter’s strength in private equity and signals a continuing trend toward consumer-focused investments as the market expands.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK EXPANDS TECH COMPANIES PLATFORM WITH NEW EMPLOYMENT PARTNER

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has named Sarah Tauman a partner in its California Employment team, based in Sacramento. The appointment takes effect on 12th May 2026, and Tauman will focus on advising venture-backed technology companies on employment matters as they scale operations and expand their businesses. Tauman's practice spans health tech, fintech and consumer tech, and she counsels clients on the employment aspects of mergers and acquisitions and initial public offerings. Her arrival marks the third major addition to Orrick's Tech Companies platform in 2026, following Agatha Kluk in New York and Gina Marek in Silicon Valley, underscoring the firm's commitment to meeting rising demand for employment guidance among growth-stage tech firms. The California Employment practice is rated Band 1 by Chambers. Julie Totten, Leader of Global Employment Law and Litigation Practice at Orrick, noted the demand for employment counseling at all stages of growth, and Tauman said, 'Orrick is known for leadership in Tech and Employment, and particularly for innovation. I’m excited to be joining such a highly regarded team, and I look forward to collaborating to serve our clients.'

+ ORRICK BOLSTERS IP BENCH WITH SENIOR USPTO ATTORNEY HIRE

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has bolstered its IP practice by recruiting a senior attorney from the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). The hire strengthens the firm's ability to handle complex patent issues as Orrick expands in technology and telecommunications sectors.The new attorney will join on the 28th of April, 2026, bringing extensive experience from the USPTO in patent examination and litigation, aligning with Orrick's aim to deliver comprehensive legal solutions for clients in fast-moving industries.Orrick's managing partner framed the hire as a signal of the firm's commitment to top-tier IP services and a deep understanding of patent law. The move fits within a broader strategy to adapt to the evolving landscape of intellectual property rights in the digital era.

+ ORRICK WELCOMES NICOLE LOPEZ, A LEADER IN YOUTH ONLINE SAFETY POLICY AND LITIGATION

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe announced the addition of Nicole Lopez to its team, naming her a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation. Lopez previously held a senior role at Meta and will help bolster the firm's capabilities as online safety challenges evolve. On Apr. 28, 2026, she expressed enthusiasm for the new role and the opportunity to broaden her impact.Lopez's background spans policy, litigation and regulatory strategy, which Orrick says will benefit clients facing heightened scrutiny over online safety. "I am so immensely proud of the pioneering work we did at Meta as a team, where I worked with incredibly talented colleagues who cared so deeply about getting it right," she said. Jeremy Kudon, who leads Orrick's State Legislative practice, noted escalated legislative activity with 25 state legislatures considering measures to safeguard minors online. "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JK4-WBY3-S0GM-T01Y-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREAN ASSET MANAGER'S NEW SPACE TECH ETF REACHES 100 BILLION WON AUM, HIGHLIGHTING NEWSPACE TILT

The fund has posted a 7.63% return, the best among Korea’s nine space and aerospace ETFs. Individual investors have shown robust demand, net purchasing 52.6 billion won over the six-session window after launch. Portfolio manager Kim Hyuntae emphasized agility in active management, noting that the team can quickly adapt to market developments and incorporate new listings like SpaceX as opportunities arise.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION UNVEILS 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) said it will host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, launching on 20th April 2026, to deepen the capabilities of finance professionals across public and private sectors.Over four days the program will deliver eight lectures, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for 2026.Participants will engage in discussions of alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, and will review post-investment portfolio management from both investment-management and operational perspectives.The curriculum will address key market issues, such as the evolving U.S. interest-rate environment and the implications of the institutionalization of stablecoins. Interest in the academy is high, with applications from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, KIC's CEO, stressed that the organization will continue to develop domestic finance by sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network, and that the academy will nurture global investment professionals through initiatives like this program. Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals.

+ KIC SPOTLIGHTS NORTH AMERICAN MID-MARKET INFRASTRUCTURE AT NYC FORUM

On April 16, 2026, the Korea Investment Corporation hosted the 28th New York Finance Forum at the Korea Society in New York, drawing more than 40 participants from government, public investment bodies and financial services to discuss mid-market infrastructure opportunities in North America.Northleaf Capital Partners shared insights on the mid-market landscape, stating that deals typically involve companies valued around USD 1 billion or less, with the threshold now expanding to as much as USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics and asset-manager criteria.Jared Waldron, Co-Head of Infrastructure at Northleaf, noted that roughly 86% of North American infrastructure transactions occur in the mid-market, but institutional funds remain heavily concentrated in larger funds, resulting in a less competitive environment and potentially better entry points for medium-sized deals.Jessica Kennedy, Managing Director at Northleaf, stressed that mid-market infrastructure can deliver differentiated results through active value creation and multiple exit strategies, implying attractive risk-adjusted returns for participants. Yuryoung Kim, Head of KIC's New York office and organizer of the forum, said the discussions could help domestic institutional investors refine overseas alternative investment strategies, as KIC maintains a presence in major hubs including New York, San Francisco, London, Singapore and Mumbai to facilitate information exchange and collaboration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CIC SELLS $1 BILLION PORTFOLIO TO GOLDMAN AND ARDIAN AT MIXED DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs Asset Management and Ardian have agreed to acquire a $1 billion CIC portfolio at discounts, marking a significant entry in the secondary market. Ardian secured a double-digit markdown on its portion, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, according to people familiar with the matter, with the deal dated 15th April 2026.The seller, China Investment Corporation, is moving to rebalance its holdings and reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. As of 2024, about 48.5% of CIC's assets were in alternatives, and around 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally. CIC had previously explored offloading stakes in funds managed by Carlyle, KKR and TPG, paused the process, and then reopened discussions that led to the sale to Goldman and Ardian.For Goldman and Ardian, the acquisition expands their scale in the highly active secondary market. Ardian has recently raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion. The deal also mirrors a broader trend among large investors—Harvard and several Asian asset managers among them—turning to secondaries to obtain liquidity and rebalance portfolios amid concerns about prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk.

+ BLUEFIVE TO LEAD GP ROLE IN CICC-HBIS FUND OF FUNDS TARGETING CROSS-BORDER INDUSTRIAL UPGRADES

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The appointment was formalized at a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds aims to raise RMB32 billion over 15 years and will invest through a mix of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS's industrial priorities, spanning traditional sectors and emerging areas such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.The vehicle will focus on cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading, with its governance built around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—intended to connect local governments, international capital, and industrial developers. BlueFive will partner with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to help ensure long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, noted the firm’s optimism about China’s long-term growth potential and highlighted opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion, especially in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group is among the world’s largest steel materials producers and has been a Fortune Global 500 member for 17 consecutive years. CICC, based in Beijing, offers a full suite of financial services including investment banking, asset management, and private equity, and the partnership with BlueFive aims to boost regional investment opportunities and industrial growth.

+ CANADA SEES $60 BILLION IN FDI FROM CHINA AND HONG KONG; US REMAINS MAJOR SOURCE

Canada's foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong exceeded $60 billion through 2023, making these regions the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta accounted for roughly half of the total, with Statistics Canada recording $24.9 billion from China and $32.8 billion from Hong Kong, behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.As of December 30, 2025, The Globe and Mail reported that Alberta’s engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors remained strong despite strained federal relations with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse said federal-provincial discussions had intensified, describing the moment as 'harvest time' for those who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta notes that the investments focus on Alberta’s traditional oil and gas sector, carbon capture projects, green-energy initiatives and tourism. China remains a major customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline, highlighting ongoing economic interdependence even as Ottawa and Beijing sought to reset ties after past tensions from the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians between 2018 and 2021. Diplomatic engagement has continued, including a July 2024 visit by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly to Beijing, and a visible thaw under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met Xi Jinping in October 2025 and is being considered for a 2026 visit. Christiaanse added that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials for engaging with China, arguing that disengaging from China 'simply does not exist.' Canada’s broader FDI profile shows a heavy reliance on the United States (about 45.7% in 2023) with Asia-Oceania accounting for 11.8% (led by Japan, Hong Kong and China).
