urn:contentItem:6JK7-JTR3-RSBB-S10G-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ PILLSBURY ANNOUNCES BOSTON OFFICE AS PART OF GLOBAL EXPANSION

On 12th May 2026, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman announced plans to open an office in Boston, expanding its presence in a major U.S. legal market with a team of five senior lawyers, including a litigator hired from Latham & Watkins.Robert Gilbert, who arrives from Latham after seven years, focuses on policyholder insurance recovery and brings experience with Harvard University, UniFirst Corporation, and Northland Investment Corporation. He will help establish the Boston office alongside Michael Zeliger, Andrew Troop, Don Lonczak, and David Simons.Chair and CEO David Dekker said the Boston outpost aligns with the city’s startup and research strengths, noting Pillsbury’s long history of serving Boston-area clients. He added, "Pillsbury has long represented clients headquartered in the Boston area and those connected to the Boston-Cambridge innovation ecosystem. Opening a Boston office is a natural next step given our capabilities and relationships in the tech, life sciences, healthcare, and financial industries in Boston and around the world." This expansion marks Pillsbury’s 17th U.S. location and 25th globally, with Boston joining a wave of firms increasing their Boston presence.The move underscores Pillsbury’s intent to deepen ties with local clients and industries while leveraging its global platform in technology, life sciences, healthcare, and finance.

+ MORGAN LEWIS TAPS PILLSBURY TALENT TO DEEPEN BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has added a prominent Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP attorney to its Employee Benefits team, reflecting a strategic response to rising complexity in benefits law.The attorney's background encompasses compliance and regulatory issues across employee benefits, with expectations to strengthen the firm's ability to provide comprehensive legal services.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire supports Morgan Lewis' ongoing plan to expand its benefits practice in a changing regulatory environment, while Pillsbury has seen departures to other firms.The move underscores the competitive talent market in corporate law and signals Morgan Lewis' commitment to maintaining state-of-the-art capabilities for its diverse client base.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE LAUNCH GLOBAL FIRM ASHURST PERKINS COIE TO EXPAND CROSS-BORDER OFFERINGS

On 13th April 2026 the partnership vote to merge Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie was approved, with a closing target in the third quarter subject to customary conditions.The leadership structure will feature Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Karen Davies of Ashurst and Brian Eiting of Perkins Coie will serve as co-chairs.The merged group will comprise about 3,000 lawyers in 52 offices across 32 countries, with core hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The combined revenues are around USD 2.8 billion, reflecting extensive cross-border experience across cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The aim is to sharpen competitive edge and position the firm for ongoing growth in the global market.

+ GLOBAL LAW FIRMS PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE UNDER ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union that combines geographic reach with sector depth and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will become a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and about 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. The combined firm will be noted for its technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services capabilities, aligned with the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked that the partnership's approval signals a belief that the merger will deliver a differentiated global platform with scale and leadership in technology to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed the sentiment, emphasizing alignment between the firms and their joint future ambitions. 'This vote confirms our strong alignment and the compelling platform it creates for clients and talent alike,' he said. The parties noted that the merger will enhance cross-border service capability across key sectors.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET THROUGH SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has opened a Shenzhen representative office, signaling a renewed foothold in China. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026. The move follows the 2024 closure of the firm's Beijing and Shanghai offices and reflects a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to leverage the city's growing tech and manufacturing base in the Greater Bay Area. Bing Ai and Wei Yuan will lead the office as chief representative and managing partner and as representative and litigation partner, respectively; both are US-trained and bring deep experience advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management for semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen launch reinforces the firm's cross-border US legal capabilities and its aim to help clients navigate global markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY GRABS $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING

Crosby has raised $60 million in a Series B, a mark of its rapid fundraising that was announced on April 3, 2026.The round was led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil, with Cooley LLP listed among the backers.Funds will be used to expand Crosby’s technology and support a growing client base that includes Clay, Rogo and Ramp.The company blends artificial intelligence with legal know-how to speed contract reviews and says the total value of its negotiated contracts now exceeds $1 billion, up from about $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby wrote, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has introduced simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate for clients, and a dashboard for clients to oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead.

+ COOLEY WELCOMES FRESHFIELDS VETERANS TO BOLSTER TECHNOLOGY GROUP

Cooley LLP announced a substantial extension of its technology practice through the addition of several attorneys formerly with Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. The move is designed to enhance Cooley's tech capabilities and underscores the firm's aim to remain competitive amid a dynamic legal environment.As of 23rd November 2025, the recruitment drive underscores the firm's continued push to expand technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The infusion of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to strengthen the existing practice and draw new clients needing technology-focused legal counsel.The incoming team members come with extensive experience advising leading clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their expertise is expected to augment Cooley's well-established venture capital and startup advisory services, further cementing the firm’s leadership in technology law.Cooley's decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader market trend in the legal industry: firms actively pursue top talent to meet demands of a fast-moving, tech-centric market. As tech-related legal challenges grow more complex, the need for specialized knowledge becomes critical, making these hires timely.With this growth, Cooley aims to solidify its status as a preferred destination for technology clients, reinforcing its capabilities in an area essential for innovation and growth. As integration proceeds, observers will monitor the impact on Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FOUNDER COMPLETES £11 MILLION SOFTWARE COMPANY ACQUISITION WITH REED SMITH COUNSEL

Reed Smith LLP supported an £11 million software company acquisition by its founder, with completion announced on 8th of May 2026, reflecting the firm's strength in navigating complex tech transactions.The founder aims to enhance operations and pursue growth, with Reed Smith delivering regulatory compliance guidance and aiding negotiations to close the deal.Analysts say the move aligns with a broader trend of strategic software acquisitions, and Reed Smith's advisory role is likely to attract investor confidence as the business enters a new chapter.

+ REED SMITH ADDS EXPERIENCED REAL ESTATE FINANCE LAWYER FROM STINSON TO DC OFFICE

ReedSmith LLP has hired a real estate finance attorney from Stinson LLP for its Washington, D.C. office to bolster its real estate and finance practice. The attorney's background includes financing, acquisition and development on significant projects, complementing Reed Smith's existing offerings.The move comes as the DC real estate market remains active and regulatory scrutiny grows, a context the firm says the new hire can help navigate.As of 7th May 2026, the firm emphasizes its commitment to growth and client service in real estate and finance.

+ REED SMITH BOLSTERS PRIVATE EQUITY PRACTICE IN LOS ANGELES WITH NEW PARTNER

Reed Smith has announced the addition of a private equity partner in its Los Angeles office, strengthening its capability in a market that remains pivotal for private capital flows.The new partner joins on 4th May 2026 and brings deep experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services, aligning with the firm's plan to broaden its private equity practice and serve investment firms and corporate clients involved in M&A.The appointment underscores Reed Smith's aim to provide strategic insights and tailored solutions as the private equity landscape evolves, supporting sophisticated investment structures and regulatory considerations.This strategic hire positions Reed Smith to pursue growth opportunities in private equity and continue delivering high-quality service and durable client relationships.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ SPACE BIOTECHNOLOGY STARTUP BIOORBIT CLOSES $13.2M SEED ROUND; WSGR HELPS GUIDE FINANCING

BioOrbit has announced a seed round of $13.2 million, co-led by LocalGlobe and BREEGA, with contributions from Seedcamp, Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund, Type One Ventures, 7percent Ventures and others. The financing was announced on 30th April 2026.BioOrbit aims to establish a space-based pharmaceuticals factory within the next decade and is developing hardware to enable large-scale crystallization of protein drugs, a core step in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.The company is led by Cambridge scientist Dr. Katie King and oncology researcher Dr. Leonor Teles, who are spearheading this space-focused platform.Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. provided corporate governance and emerging-company counsel to BioOrbit during the financing, with the team including Daniel Glazer, Jose Campos, Rashad Mohammed and Sophia Kunthara.The deal underscores rising investor interest in space-enabled life sciences, and BioOrbit will share updates on LinkedIn and in UKTN coverage as plans progress.

+ WILSON SONSINI ELEVATES ENERGY TRANSITION CAPABILITIES WITH KEN SANOCKI APPOINTMENT

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati has added Ken Sanocki as a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice, aimed at expanding the firm's ability to help clients finance energy infrastructure through the transition to cleaner power.Sanocki comes from Sunrun, where as vice president of legal he oversaw all project-finance matters, including tax equity financings and complex debt structures for distributed-energy projects. He led more than 50 tax equity financings totaling over $10 billion.Doug Clark, the firm's managing partner, said that Sanocki has operated at the center of the energy transition and will bring deep knowledge of tax equity and structured financings to clients navigating energy financing.In his new role, Sanocki will counsel on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure, assist with capital formation and strategic transactions, and help clients develop revenue-generating asset portfolios and prepare for IPOs or M&A. He began his career at Milbank after serving as SunEdison senior counsel for global structured and project finance; he earned a J.D. from NYU and a B.A. from Boston College.

+ WILSON SONSINI SUPPORTS OFF PISTE CAPITAL IN LANDMARK FUND DEBUT BACKED BY EDITION VENTURES

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. advised Off Piste Capital in forming its inaugural fund, Off Piste Fund I, which closed on 29th April 2026 with backing from strategic limited partners including Edition Ventures.The fund targets investments in companies building the systems and infrastructure of the future. Off Piste has already invested in four startups: Stendr, a Norway-based firm delivering AI-driven counter-drone technology; Objection, a UK startup creating accountability infrastructure for public information; Beyond Reach Labs, a US YC-backed venture developing deployable space structures; and Octet, a UK company specializing in cryptographic proofs of physical location.The deal team at Wilson Sonsini included Bradley Doline, Alice Cao, and Stephanie Yasay, whose work on capital markets and fund formation helped navigate the complexities of launching a VC fund. This collaboration underscores the firm's ongoing support for emerging venture activity and its expanding footprint in the tech investment landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN HELPS CLOSE $1.3 BILLION MULTIFAMILY-FOCUSED S3 VEHICLE

Sidley Austin has advised on the closure of a $1.3 billion multifamily-focused S3 fund, a milestone for the real estate investment sector. The announcement, dated 7th May 2026, highlights increasing demand for multifamily housing driven by demographic shifts and urbanization. The S3 vehicle targets multifamily properties and is positioned to attract investor interest as housing needs evolve. Urban density and solid rental fundamentals underpin the appeal of such assets, with Sidley guiding the fund's formation and regulatory compliance. Over recent years, Sidley has built a track record of delivering legal counsel tailored to real estate investments, assisting clients through fund formation and regulatory requirements. The successful close reinforces the firm's market standing as it continues to advise on high-value transactions. Looking ahead, Sidley remains focused on delivering comprehensive legal solutions as the market evolves, with this closing serving as evidence of its capacity to facilitate substantial real estate investments.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN GUIDES TELIX IN 50:50 CO-DEVELOPMENT AND CO-COMMERCIALISATION DEAL WITH REGENERON WORTH USD 2.1 BILLION

Telix Pharmaceuticals has enlisted Sidley Austin to guide its collaboration with Regeneron, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion that covers the co-development and co-commercialisation of next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies. The arrangement provides for a 50:50 split of costs and profits and includes an upfront USD 40 million payment for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the agreement is built to pool the companies' strengths in oncology and radiopharmaceuticals, aiming to move candidates through development more efficiently while sharing regulatory and governance responsibilities. Sidley partner Joshua Hofheimer noted that governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property are central to these collaborations, stating that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance reflects a broader push in the biopharma space toward strategic collaborations designed to accelerate innovation in cancer care, a trend that law firms like Sidley are well-placed to support given the increasing complexity of cross-border deals.

+ RECRUITMENT PUSH DRIVES 33% GROWTH IN SIDLEY AUSTIN'S CITY OPERATIONS

Sidley Austin reported a 33% rise in City revenue, attributed to its hiring strategy as of 14th April 2026. Management credits a focused approach to identifying and integrating skilled professionals that expanded the City teams and diversified practice areas. The improvements in service have helped lift billable hours and client retention, reinforcing confidence in the hiring model. Analysts say the move demonstrates a willingness to invest in human capital to keep pace with a shifting legal market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ LONDON OFFICE GROWS TECH M&A CAPABILITIES AS THREE PARTNERS JOIN MORRISON FOERSTER

Morrison Foerster says it has expanded its tech transactions team with three London-based partners: Mike Pierides, Will Holder, and Oliver Bell, adding to its global technology transactions and AI-focused M&A platform. The trio brings more than 55 years of combined experience. The firm announced this on the 9th of May, 2026.Pierides concentrates on technology and commercial transactions, including outsourcing, data privacy and cybersecurity, GPU-as-a-service agreements, licensing, and technology-as-a-service mandates, serving clients in AI, data centers, fintech, aviation, telecommunications, and financial services.Holder broadens Morrison Foerster's tech and media M&A practice across public and private M&A, private equity, joint ventures, and corporate matters, with work spanning technology, music, sports, media, fintech, and consumer sectors. Bell is set to join in the coming weeks with experience in technology-led transactions, large-scale IT outsourcing deals, and cybersecurity.The hires are intended to strengthen cross-border technology transactions and AI-related capabilities and align with Morrison Foerster's London growth strategy. They mark the eighth, ninth, and tenth partner additions in the firm's London office in the past year, reflecting rising demand for AI, fintech, data centers, and digital transformation. Eric McCrath, Chair, said the firm's investment in core tech M&A capabilities remains central, while London partner Andrew Boyd highlighted the synergies with the practice areas.

+ MORRISON & FOERSTER REVIEWS SENATE ACTION RESTRICTING PREDICTION MARKET PARTICIPATION

The Senate moved on 29th April 2026 to ban Senators and their staff from trading in prediction markets, a resolution Morrison & Foerster says reflects growing regulatory focus on these platforms.Hours later, Gillibrand and McCormick introduced legislation to bar government officials from prediction market trading, proposing insider trading standards, AML measures, and an independent Retail Advocate within the CFTC.Kalshi announced underage- restriction updates, including face ID and parent-based two-factor authentication, aligning with the proposed framework and aiming to boost consumer trust.A Senate hearing on prediction markets and sports betting is planned for 20th May 2026, with Patrick McHenry testifying alongside industry representatives; Selig defended CFTC jurisdiction in a Wall Street Journal letter, stressing the markets as innovative financial products.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FIRST AI UNICORN IN LATIN AMERICA EMERGES AS ENTER SEALS $100 MILLION SERIES B

SAO PAULO, May 8, 2026 — Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented Enter, a Brazil-based AI legal-technology company, in its Series B that raised over $100 million and valued Enter at about $1.2 billion, marking Enter as Latin America’s first AI unicorn. The round was led by Founders Fund with significant participation from Sequoia Capital, Ribbit Capital, Kaszek, Atlantico and ONEVC. Enter, founded in 2023, serves major clients including Airbnb, Nubank, Mercado Libre and Latam Airlines, among more than 40 partners. Enter uses AI to automate key litigation tasks, from evidence discovery to drafting responses and negotiating settlements, with human review by Enter’s legal network. Greenberg Traurig’s deal team was led by Matthew Squires and included Maurice Guttmann, L. Frank Cordero, Agata Jacqueline Paramesvari and Nathalya Zara, underscoring the firm’s cross-border capabilities across 51 offices and a 3,100-lawyer platform.

+ GERARD C. MARTIN JOINS GREENBERG TRAURIG AS CHICAGO SHAREHOLDER IN RESTRUCTURING PRACTICE

On 7th May 2026, Greenberg Traurig, LLP announced the expansion of its Chicago office with Gerard C. Martin joining as a shareholder in the Restructuring & Special Situations Practice, coming from Reed Smith LLP. His practice includes cash-flow and asset-based lending, leveraged finance, acquisition financings, intercreditor arrangements, and equipment and aviation finance, along with project finance matters. He also handles financial restructurings, advising secured creditors and debtors in in-court and out-of-court matters, including debtor-in-possession financings and distressed investing. His industry experience spans manufacturing, energy, aviation, technology, health care and financial services, expanding Greenberg Traurig's cross-sector capabilities. Martin said the firm’s platform aligns with his work, adding that Greenberg Traurig is an ideal fit, and Shari L. Heyen and Oscar N. Pinkas noted the Chicago office will leverage his expertise.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG NAVIGATES SECOND JAPANESE HYPERSCALE DEAL FOR CLIENT, HIGHLIGHTING DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE EXPERTISE

Greenberg Traurig, LLP advised the seller in the sale of a stabilized 40-megawatt hyperscale data center in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. The closing occurred on 7th May 2026 and is among the largest asset-level sales of a fully leased hyperscale facility in Japan. The buyer is a CapitaLand Ascendas REIT-led consortium, purchasing a 49% stake with the remaining 51% going to a fund managed by an affiliate of a Japanese trading company. The seller was a joint venture between a US institutional manager’s fund and a fund sponsored by a Japanese trading company; the center is leased to a single US hyperscale cloud provider. This transaction marks the second Japanese hyperscale data center sale for this client, following a deal completed in November. Greenberg Traurig’s Digital Infrastructure Industry Group, with over 110 attorneys worldwide, supports clients across data center and telecom transactions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESPERION'S $1.1B PRIVATISATION SIGNALS PRIVATE-EQUITY PULL IN BIOTECH, UNDER LEGAL HELM OF SIDLEY AND GOODWIN

Esperion Therapeutics Inc. has agreed to a $1.1 billion take-private, with Sidley Austin LLP and Goodwin Procter LLP leading the legal work. The announcement, made on 1st May 2026, positions the lipid-management specialist to focus on its programs outside the public markets.Private ownership is gaining traction in biotech as firms seek to streamline R&D and accelerate commercialization.Sidley and Goodwin's involvement reflects their strength in complex M&A deals and signals rigorous governance as Esperion transitions to a private structure.Analysts note that the move could enable longer-term strategic planning and potentially faster growth, while investors monitor the company's performance in its new privately held state.

+ FBT GIBBONS TAPS GOODWIN PROCTER VETERAN TO STRENGTHEN VC CAPABILITIES

FBT Gibbons is expanding its capabilities in venture capital by hiring from Goodwin Procter LLP for its New Jersey office. On 21st April 2026, the firm confirmed the recruitment of a venture capital attorney. The attorney comes with extensive venture capital experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, a leader in the field, and the firm plans to leverage that expertise for clients seeking guidance on capital raises. Industry observers note the hire reflects a broader trend of law firms sharpening niche practices to compete in a growing market.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER ENABLES CLOSURE OF $328 MILLION CONSUMER-FOCUSED FUND FOR TOPSPIN

Goodwin Procter LLP helped close Topspin Consumer Partners' latest fundraising, securing a $328 million commitment for a consumer-focused fund. The deal underscores a growing trend in private equity toward consumer-oriented strategies, with Goodwin Procter providing crucial legal support throughout the process.On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the fund’s closure, designed to pursue opportunities in the consumer sector. The firm’s counsel was instrumental in navigating fundraising requirements, maintaining regulatory compliance, and simplifying investor workflows.The fund intends to target a broad spectrum of opportunities across consumer industries, emphasizing segments with strong growth trajectories. With Goodwin Procter’s guidance, Topspin Consumer Partners aims to back innovative ventures well positioned to compete in a dynamic market.This partnership reinforces Goodwin Procter’s leadership in private equity work and its dedication to client success, with potential implications for investor confidence as the fund progresses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK TAPS EMPLOYMENT EXPERT TO BOLSTER CALIFORNIA TECH PLATFORM

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has named Sarah Tauman as a partner in its California Employment team based in Sacramento, effective 12th May 2026. Tauman will advise venture-backed technology companies on employment matters as they scale. Her practice spans health tech, fintech and consumer tech, including guidance on employment issues in mergers and acquisitions and initial public offerings, supporting growth trajectories for tech firms. This marks the third key addition to Orrick's Tech Companies platform in 2026, following Agatha Kluk in New York and Gina Marek in Silicon Valley. The California Employment practice is ranked Band 1 by Chambers. Totten noted the ongoing demand for employment counseling across all growth stages, and Tauman said, 'Orrick is known for leadership in Tech and Employment, and particularly for innovation.'

+ ORRICK WELCOMES SENIOR USPTO LAWYER AS IP PRACTICE GROWS

Orrick has expanded its IP practice by hiring a senior attorney from the USPTO, aiming to enhance capabilities in patent matters as the firm grows in tech and telecom.The new attorney will join on the 28th of April, 2026, and brings extensive patent examination and litigation experience.Orrick's leadership described the hire as evidence of the firm's commitment to top-tier IP services and to navigating the evolving IP rights landscape in the digital age.With demand for technology-focused counsel rising, the firm expects this addition to support clients facing complex patent challenges and strengthen its competitive standing.

+ FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY POLICY LEAD JOINS ORRICK

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick announced the addition of the former Meta youth safety policy head to its team, a move designed to expand its digital safety and compliance capabilities.The new hire comes with a strong track record in policy design and implementation, particularly around privacy and the protection of young users online.Orrick frames the appointment as part of its proactive response to evolving regulatory requirements governing youth online safety.The firm expects the hire to strengthen its advisory services as tech companies face heightened scrutiny and regulatory complexity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJX-X4N3-S254-Y4SS-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - THE RECENT NEWS OF KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - THE RECENT NEWS OF KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART EXPANDS FORTRESS PARTNERSHIP WITH $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW AGREEMENT

Upstart Holdings announced a forward-flow commitment from Fortress affiliates to buy up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated on its platform over 15 months, with the disclosure on 5th May 2026.The deal expands a partnership that began in 2025 and reflects Upstart’s effort to broaden its network of capital partners. Fortress and its affiliates join more than 100 banks and credit unions using Upstart’s AI-driven models and cloud applications to originate loans.Upstart’s platform covers a broad suite of products, including personal loans, automotive finance and refinance loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product.Executives described the arrangement as a win for both sides, noting that a larger base of capital partners helps reduce borrowing costs and complexity for borrowers, while Fortress seeks differentiated, high-quality consumer credit opportunities for its investors. The collaboration signals a broader industry shift toward technology-driven credit solutions and positions both companies to pursue opportunities in the evolving lending landscape.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH USD 180 MILLION PREFERRED-NOTE FINANCING

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is designed to support the construction of the land-based facility and to help meet growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores Fortress Investment Group’s longstanding presence in Japan and its willingness to provide both capital and strategic guidance to Pure Salmon Japan. The closing signals strong institutional backing for the project and aligns with Fortress’s broader focus on sustainable infrastructure.The project targets a production capacity of 10,000 tonnes and aims to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will use advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to minimize imports and bolster food security.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress officials also noted the value of the partnership; Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, called the investment a meaningful step in strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure. As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan intends to pursue additional long-term equity partners to enhance operations.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST DEAL, AGEWELL TO OPERATE AS PART OF GROWTH IN SENIOR LIVING PORTFOLIO

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida. The transaction, funded by Fortress affiliates, was completed on 12th March 2026, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care.Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, serving an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast.Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, said: \"We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families.\"The acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) JOINS HASI IN $508 MILLION PRIVATE NOTE OFFERING FOR CARBONCOUNT HOLDINGS 1

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI) Raise $508 Million Through a private offering of senior unsecured notes for CarbonCount Holdings 1 (CCH1), as of 11th May 2026. The proceeds will be deployed to acquire or invest in both new and existing sustainable infrastructure projects across the United States.CarbonCount Holdings 1 was created in 2024 as a strategic alliance between KKR and HASI to provide long-term capital solutions for renewable-energy initiatives, including behind-the-meter, grid-connected, RNG, and transportation infrastructure projects. The platform began with commitments of $1 billion from each sponsor, and in December 2025 the firms added another $500 million. This issuance marks CCH1's second senior-note issuance, following a $592 million offering completed in June 2025, and lifts the platform’s total investment capacity toward $5 billion.Cecilio Velasco, Managing Director at KKR, noted the strong investor reception for the latest issuance and highlighted that with over $4 billion of investment capacity the platform is well-positioned to meet the growing demand for sustainable energy infrastructure. Dan McMahon, Senior Managing Director of Syndications at HASI, expressed enthusiasm about expanding CCH1’s investment capacity and its role in supporting rising activity in sustainable infrastructure. Morgan Stanley and HASI Securities acted as Joint Lead Placement Agents for the transaction

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) EXITS DANISH RESIDENTIAL PORTFOLIO THROUGH SALE TO PEARS GLOBAL

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) has sold its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, a move that aligns with the firm's ongoing portfolio rationalization. The sale was announced on 11th May 2026 at 21:07:57.692343.The Danish portfolio comprises several residential units located in key urban areas, giving Pears Global Real Estate an opportunity to expand its Northern European footprint.KKR cited its plan to optimize asset management by divesting non-core holdings and reallocating capital toward higher-return opportunities, with private credit highlighted as a core area.Pears Global Real Estate has a track record of strategic acquisitions in high-value markets and intends to leverage its expertise to enhance the properties and expand its Europe-focused portfolio, where demand for residential properties is rising amid urbanization.Investors will monitor the implications for both entities as they continue to adjust to evolving market dynamics and global risks.

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) AND SEI EXTEND COLLABORATION TO DELIVER INSTITUTIONAL-STYLE PRIVATE MARKET SOLUTIONS FOR ADVISORS

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) and SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) broaden their partnership to develop private market investment offerings for wealth and retirement clients, reflecting growing demand for alternative assets across advisory channels. On the 9th of May, 2026, the firms said their enhanced collaboration would widen access to institutional-style private market strategies, including model portfolios and defined contribution retirement products. The collaboration builds on a multi-year relationship focused on fund administration and technology services, combining Carlyle’s private markets platform with SEI’s research, implementation, and client servicing. The integration aims to give advisors and investors a more straightforward path to private market exposure, with tailored solutions designed to navigate this complex asset class. Michael Lane, SEI’s head of asset management, said, "One of the most common questions we hear from clients is how to allocate to private markets. Our objective with Carlyle is to help simplify that decision by providing more streamlined access to a broader range of strategies." Jeff Nedelman, Carlyle’s co-president and global head of client business, remarked that the partnership responds to rising demand from individual investors and retirement savers for private market investments. As of March 31, 2026, SEI reported about $1.9 trillion in assets under management, administration, or advisement, and Carlyle reported $477 billion under management as of December 31, 2025.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) UNITES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO EXPAND DIGITAL HEALTHCARE SERVICES PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is integrating Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a unified platform designed to boost digital capabilities across healthcare services. The move addresses ongoing structural changes in the U.S. healthcare system, including administrative complexities, workforce challenges, and evolving care delivery models. The combined platform will support physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers requiring tailored operational assistance, leveraging Knack's globally distributed operational framework and Equalize's tools for predictive analytics and optimization. The integration aims to advance revenue cycle management by enhancing billing, claims processing, and payer coordination, potentially improving efficiency and regulatory compliance. The initiative aligns with market trends as providers seek technology-driven solutions to navigate operational complexities, and Carlyle positions itself to participate in a sector undergoing substantial transformation.

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) EXPANDS HEALTHCARE REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT CAPABILITIES WITH KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM DEAL

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, expanding its healthcare portfolio. The terms of the deal are not disclosed in a release dated May 4, 2026. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the new RCM platform, aligning with Carlyle's plan to fuse Knack's analytics with Rao's global operations to bolster advisory servicesKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM count physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals among their clients. The collaboration targets rural cost reports and complex anesthesia cases, as noted by Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle frames the move as a platform for broader participation in the RCM market and signals intent to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementCarlyle sets its course to strengthen capabilities in healthcare management and revenue cycle technology, with the expectation of delivering innovative solutions that support providers in navigating a changing healthcare landscape

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ INTER MILAN SECURES €5 MILLION TOP-UP FROM OAKTREE CAPITAL

Oaktree Capital has injected €5 million into Inter Milan, lifting the total cash infusion into the club's equity reserves to €52 million, according to a semiannual statement released on 11th May 2026.Having taken ownership of Inter nearly a year ago after purchasing the club from Suning following a loan default, Oaktree has pursued a strategy balancing stability with future growth while keeping CEO Giuseppe Marotta in place.The Nerazzurri have retained much of last season’s Serie A title-winning core but aim to rejuvenate the squad by bringing in younger players with resale value, aligning with a longer-term horizon.Plans for a new stadium to replace San Siro are moving forward, a move expected to significantly boost revenue, supported by the latest funding as part of a broader plan to keep Inter competitive on and off the pitch.The €5 million tranche follows a similar injection in December last year, underscoring Oaktree Capital’s ongoing commitment to the club’s financial health and strategic objectives. As Inter continues to evolve under Oaktree, eyes will be on how these investments translate into on-field performance and commercial prospects.

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL PROVIDES $300 MILLION FINANCING TO ESTABLISHMENT LABS

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan from Oaktree Capital Management. The facility, finalized on 3rd May 2026, refinances existing debt through 2031 and reserves US$35 million for growth initiatives.The loan carries a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure with flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets, enhancing funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues its operational and expansion plans.The investment thesis centers on the Motiva implant platform and minimally invasive procedures expected to drive broad adoption.Management guidance for 2026 projects revenue of US$264-266 million, implying roughly 25-26% growth from the prior year. The financing should ease refinancing pressure and provide liquidity to address losses and cash burn without an immediate equity raise.While the capital raise improves flexibility, there are risks linked to ongoing cash burn and negative free cash flow. Some analysts call for around 30% revenue growth with earnings near US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could reinforce or temper optimism about profitability. The agreement marks a strategic shift in its financing approach.

+ BIOXCEL AND OAKTREE EXTEND REGISTRATION RIGHTS, ISSUE PRIVATE WARRANTS

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed in an 8-K filing that it has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and a group of institutional holders. The move aligns with the company's ongoing efforts to optimize its capital structure and provides a framework for potential future equity transactions under the arrangement disclosed on 18 April 2026. Key elements of the deal include amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was signed on 27 March 2026, setting the stage for these warrants. On 15 April 2026, the company issued the unregistered warrants in a private placement, a mechanism that avoided immediate Securities Act registration as part of the financing strategy. BioXcel said it would file registration statements with the SEC to enable possible resale of the warrants' underlying shares, a move designed to improve liquidity and maintain investor confidence. Lenders have indicated they intend to hold the securities for investment purposes rather than seek rapid resale, which could moderate near-term volatility in the stock price. Market participants should note the potential dilution risk if warrants are exercised, and the registration process could increase the total share count. The financing approach signals the company's liquidity needs and strategic objectives, rather than an implicit endorsement of aggressive share issuance. BioXcel also clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting and regulatory requirements.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) EXPLORES STRATEGIC SALE OF MIDCAP FINANCIAL INVESTMENT IN A $3 BILLION DEAL

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) is exploring a strategic sale of its publicly traded private credit fund MidCap Financial Investment Corp (NYSE:MFIC) in a deal valued around $3 billion. The discussions, held on 11th May 2026, indicate a potential buyer that could be a business development company and may involve some level of stock consideration rather than a cash-only transaction.The contemplated sale reflects Apollo Global Management's efforts to recalibrate its asset mix amid ongoing headwinds in the private credit market. MidCap Financial Investment posted a default rate of 5.3% in the first quarter of 2026, up from 3.9% at the end of 2025, and reported a quarterly loss of $61 million. Apollo Global Management remains one of the largest alternative asset managers with a market capitalization of about $76.79 billion and assets totaling $938.4 billion, including $709.1 billion in fee-earning assets. The stock trades at 84.84x trailing earnings, close to a five-year high, underscoring investor expectations for continued profitability.Investors should consider the GF Score of 80, profitability rating of 8/10, and financial strength rating of 6/10, along with an Altman Z-score of 0.04. Insider activity shows five insider sales totaling $127.57 million over the past 12 months and one insider buy, which may influence risk assessments as the potential deal progresses.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) ACQUIRES MAJORITY STAKE IN PROSOL GROUP TO GROW FRANCE FRESH FOOD NETWORK

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Acquires Majority Stake In Prosol Group To Grow France Fresh Food NetworkApollo closes the purchase of a majority stake in Prosol Group through its managed funds, with Ardian selling its stake. The deal was finalized on May 7, 2026. Prosol Group operates nearly 450 stores across France under Grand Frais and fresh brands and runs a vertically integrated supply chain centered on quality and freshness. Jean-Paul Mochet will remain Chief Executive Officer, leading the company as it integrates into Apollo’s portfolio.The investment aligns with Apollo’s strategy to broaden its footprint in consumer-focused platforms. Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) reports approximately $1.03 trillion in assets under management, underscoring its scale to support Prosol’s expansion plans. UBS AG provided financial advisory services, with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard also advising.The deal terms were not disclosed, and potential risks include supply chain volatility and changing consumer tastes. + APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS RECORD AUM AT MORE THAN $1 TRILLION AS IT PILOTS DAILY PRIVATE ASSET PRICING

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) reported that total assets under management surpassed $1 trillion for the first time as it advances a daily pricing pilot for its private assets. On the 6th of May 2026, during the first-quarter results call, Marc Rowan, chairman and chief executive, described activity as reflecting solid demand and noted record fee-related earnings. Total AUM reached $1.03 trillion as of March 31, 2026, up 31 percent from a year earlier. The company posted first-quarter inflows of $115 billion and inflows of $300 billion in the trailing twelve months. The asset management business brought in $95 billion of inflows, supported by Athora's acquisition of the Pension Insurance Corporation in the UK, along with institutional and global wealth inflows into credit-focused strategies. Retirement services added $20 billion in inflows. The Financial Stability Board released a report highlighting vulnerabilities in private credit on the same day, and Apollo defended its approach, citing prudent underwriting. The group also described its ongoing daily valuation pilot, which produced estimated daily values for investment-grade fixed income products last year and will provide daily pricing for corporate investment-grade assets by 6:30 PM ET and for direct lending and asset-backed finance by 9:30 PM ET. Looking ahead, Apollo sees momentum into 2026 with targets for 20 percent growth in fee-related earnings and 10 percent growth in SRE, supported by a strong origination pipeline and disciplined capital management, as institutional demand for private credit remains elevated.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO REOPENS DOHA–BAGHDAD FREIGHT LINK WITH HIGHER CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, reestablishing the Doha to Baghdad cargo link. Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will launch a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad, paired with twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route from 10th May to provide additional belly-hold capacity.The combined cargo capacity is set to exceed 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad from 10 May, reflecting an integrated approach that blends dedicated freighter uplift with passenger capacity.The move comes as Qatar Airways Cargo continues scaling its network. The freighter network now reaches more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while the passenger network is expected to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June. Bookings are open via Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales channels. For shipments, contact cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or your local representative. The resumption sits within a broader network strategy, supported by a fleet of 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, and a road feeder network as the airline continues its investments in digitalization, products, and infrastructure.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES ERBIL ROUTE IN PHASE, EXPANDS IRAQI CONNECTIVITY

Qatar Airways will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the carrier 's return to direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a phased reinstatement of its Iraq operations. The airline will restart services to three Iraqi cities — Erbil, Baghdad (BGW) and Basra (BSR) — in a staged rollout aimed at rebuilding its regional network and reconnecting key Middle Eastern destinations.Freighter operations to Baghdad will resume on 7th May, reinforcing cargo and trade links with Iraq and supporting broader network restoration. These resumptions follow earlier reinstatements to Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus and Kozhikode as part of a wider strategy to restore its global network.By mid-June 2026, the carrier expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide, with Erbil travellers gaining access to the Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, which the airline describes as a pivotal connector linking six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESTARTS DUBAI, BAHRAIN AND KOZHIKODE SERVICES, TARGETS 150-DESTINATION EXPANSION

Qatar Airways is resuming a broad set of routes as part of a measured rebound in regional travel, with key services in the Middle East, Asia, and Europe back on the timetable. As of 5th May 2026, the carrier confirmed the reinstatement of flights to Dubai and Sharjah, marking a milestone in stabilizing operations in the region.In addition, daily flights to Bahrain (BAH) and Kozhikode (CCJ) will resume, beginning 1st May 2026. The airline outlined a gradual plan to rebuild its global network, aiming to operate more than 150 destinations starting 16th June 2026. The extended schedule will be in effect through 15th September 2026, reflecting responsiveness to evolving travel demand.Qatar Airways emphasized passenger preparation during this transition, urging travelers to check its website or mobile app for updates and verify contact details. Those with confirmed bookings will receive direct notices if any changes occur, reflecting the carrier's customer service commitment.The restored routes include connections to several in-demand destinations, catering to both business and leisure travelers. The broader recovery trend in aviation is underscored by the carrier's proactive network expansion, and analysts will be watching Qatar Airways' performance as it navigates a changing travel landscape.

urn:contentItem:6JJX-X4N3-S254-Y3HB-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT NEWS OF CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT NEWS OF CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREAN ASSET MANAGER'S NEW SPACE TECH ETF REACHES 100 BILLION WON AUM, HIGHLIGHTING NEWSPACE TILT

The fund has posted a 7.63% return, the best among Korea’s nine space and aerospace ETFs. Individual investors have shown robust demand, net purchasing 52.6 billion won over the six-session window after launch. Portfolio manager Kim Hyuntae emphasized agility in active management, noting that the team can quickly adapt to market developments and incorporate new listings like SpaceX as opportunities arise.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION UNVEILS 2026 INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) said it will host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, launching on 20th April 2026, to deepen the capabilities of finance professionals across public and private sectors.Over four days the program will deliver eight lectures, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for 2026.Participants will engage in discussions of alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, and will review post-investment portfolio management from both investment-management and operational perspectives.The curriculum will address key market issues, such as the evolving U.S. interest-rate environment and the implications of the institutionalization of stablecoins. Interest in the academy is high, with applications from 65 financial and investment institutions.Park Ilyoung, KIC's CEO, stressed that the organization will continue to develop domestic finance by sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network, and that the academy will nurture global investment professionals through initiatives like this program. Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals.

+ KIC SPOTLIGHTS NORTH AMERICAN MID-MARKET INFRASTRUCTURE AT NYC FORUM

On April 16, 2026, the Korea Investment Corporation hosted the 28th New York Finance Forum at the Korea Society in New York, drawing more than 40 participants from government, public investment bodies and financial services to discuss mid-market infrastructure opportunities in North America.Northleaf Capital Partners shared insights on the mid-market landscape, stating that deals typically involve companies valued around USD 1 billion or less, with the threshold now expanding to as much as USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics and asset-manager criteria.Jared Waldron, Co-Head of Infrastructure at Northleaf, noted that roughly 86% of North American infrastructure transactions occur in the mid-market, but institutional funds remain heavily concentrated in larger funds, resulting in a less competitive environment and potentially better entry points for medium-sized deals.Jessica Kennedy, Managing Director at Northleaf, stressed that mid-market infrastructure can deliver differentiated results through active value creation and multiple exit strategies, implying attractive risk-adjusted returns for participants. Yuryoung Kim, Head of KIC's New York office and organizer of the forum, said the discussions could help domestic institutional investors refine overseas alternative investment strategies, as KIC maintains a presence in major hubs including New York, San Francisco, London, Singapore and Mumbai to facilitate information exchange and collaboration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ CIC SELLS $1 BILLION PORTFOLIO TO GOLDMAN AND ARDIAN AT MIXED DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs Asset Management and Ardian have agreed to acquire a $1 billion CIC portfolio at discounts, marking a significant entry in the secondary market. Ardian secured a double-digit markdown on its portion, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, according to people familiar with the matter, with the deal dated 15th April 2026.The seller, China Investment Corporation, is moving to rebalance its holdings and reduce exposure to illiquid USD-denominated assets. As of 2024, about 48.5% of CIC's assets were in alternatives, and around 62.5% of its global portfolio was managed externally. CIC had previously explored offloading stakes in funds managed by Carlyle, KKR and TPG, paused the process, and then reopened discussions that led to the sale to Goldman and Ardian.For Goldman and Ardian, the acquisition expands their scale in the highly active secondary market. Ardian has recently raised a record $30 billion for its secondary fund, while Goldman Sachs Asset Management has completed more than 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion. The deal also mirrors a broader trend among large investors—Harvard and several Asian asset managers among them—turning to secondaries to obtain liquidity and rebalance portfolios amid concerns about prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk.

+ BLUEFIVE TO LEAD GP ROLE IN CICC-HBIS FUND OF FUNDS TARGETING CROSS-BORDER INDUSTRIAL UPGRADES

BlueFive Capital has been appointed as the first General Partner for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation, in partnership with HBIS Group. The appointment was formalized at a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing on January 6, 2026.The Fund of Funds aims to raise RMB32 billion over 15 years and will invest through a mix of fund investments and direct holdings aligned with HBIS's industrial priorities, spanning traditional sectors and emerging areas such as advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology.The vehicle will focus on cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading, with its governance built around three pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—intended to connect local governments, international capital, and industrial developers. BlueFive will partner with leading industrial and financial institutions and local partners to help ensure long-term success.Hazem Ben-Gacem, founder and CEO of BlueFive Capital, noted the firm’s optimism about China’s long-term growth potential and highlighted opportunities for HBIS’s cross-border expansion, especially in high-end manufacturing and new building materials. HBIS Group is among the world’s largest steel materials producers and has been a Fortune Global 500 member for 17 consecutive years. CICC, based in Beijing, offers a full suite of financial services including investment banking, asset management, and private equity, and the partnership with BlueFive aims to boost regional investment opportunities and industrial growth.

+ CANADA SEES $60 BILLION IN FDI FROM CHINA AND HONG KONG; US REMAINS MAJOR SOURCE

Canada's foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong exceeded $60 billion through 2023, making these regions the third-largest overseas capital source. Alberta accounted for roughly half of the total, with Statistics Canada recording $24.9 billion from China and $32.8 billion from Hong Kong, behind only the United States and Europe in overall capital inflows.As of December 30, 2025, The Globe and Mail reported that Alberta’s engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors remained strong despite strained federal relations with Beijing. Invest Alberta chief executive Rick Christiaanse said federal-provincial discussions had intensified, describing the moment as 'harvest time' for those who endured earlier political turbulence.Invest Alberta notes that the investments focus on Alberta’s traditional oil and gas sector, carbon capture projects, green-energy initiatives and tourism. China remains a major customer for the Trans Mountain pipeline, highlighting ongoing economic interdependence even as Ottawa and Beijing sought to reset ties after past tensions from the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadians between 2018 and 2021. Diplomatic engagement has continued, including a July 2024 visit by Foreign Minister Melanie Joly to Beijing, and a visible thaw under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met Xi Jinping in October 2025 and is being considered for a 2026 visit. Christiaanse added that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials for engaging with China, arguing that disengaging from China 'simply does not exist.' Canada’s broader FDI profile shows a heavy reliance on the United States (about 45.7% in 2023) with Asia-Oceania accounting for 11.8% (led by Japan, Hong Kong and China).

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: May 04, 2026: Ontario Teachers’ Appoints Feifei Wu to Head Investment Technology and Applied Intelligence

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-K1T3-RVC5-M0TY-00000-00May 04, 2026: Ontario Teachers’ Appoints Feifei Wu to Head Investment Technology and Applied Intelligence2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 04, 2026: Ontario Teachers’ Appoints Feifei Wu to Head Investment Technology and Applied Intelligence

TORONTO, May 4th, 2026 – Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board ("Ontario Teachers’") today announced the appointment of Feifei Wu as Senior Managing Director, Investment Technology & Applied Intelligence, effective immediately. In this newly established role, Ms. Wu will partner with investment leaders to ensure technology enables key business outcomes while leading Ontario Teachers’ AI strategy to strengthen governance, accelerate adoption, and unlock new opportunities.

Ms. Wu brings over 25 years of global experience from leading investment and wealth management firms, with deep expertise in technology, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and cloud transformation. "I am pleased to welcome Feifei to Ontario Teachers’ in this important role at a pivotal time in our technology journey," said Chief Technology Officer Terry Hickey. "She brings a strong combination of technical expertise and proven leadership across global financial institutions. Her experience building high-performing technology teams and her forward-looking approach to applied intelligence will help accelerate innovation, deepen investment insights, and deliver long-term value for our members."

Before joining Ontario Teachers’, Ms. Wu was at Macquarie Group in New York, where she served as Managing Director, Global Head of Engineering of Macquarie Asset Management. Her previous roles include General Partner, Digital & Technology at Edward Jones, Global Chief Information Officer at Brown Brothers Harriman, and Managing Director at RBC Capital Markets and BNY. With such a diverse background, she is expected to drive significant advancements in Ontario Teachers’ technological capabilities.

Ms. Wu's academic credentials further bolster her qualifications; she holds an MS and PhD in Computer Science, with a specific focus on artificial intelligence and machine learning, from Rutgers University. Additionally, she earned her ME in Computer Engineering from Zhejiang University and a BE in Computer Engineering from Northeastern University in China.

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board ("Ontario Teachers’") is a global investor with net assets of $279.4 billion as of December 31, 2025. As a fully funded defined benefit pension plan, Ontario Teachers’ invests across a broad array of asset classes to ensure retirement security for its 346,000 working members and pensioners. For more information, visit otpp.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

SECTION 1 ONTARIO TEACHERS PENSION PLAN (OTPP) PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Ontario Teachers Pension Plan (OTPP), founded in 1990, is a pension fund manager based in Toronto, Canada. With around 1,300 employees, it manages approximately $250 billion in assets. OTPP invests in infrastructure, real estate, and private equity globally. In 2024, it expanded AI and renewable energy portfolios. Competing with CPPIB, its mission is to deliver sustainable pension returns through diversified, innovative investments.
