urn:contentItem:6JJP-XYY3-S4CC-F2F4-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY NEWS: INSIGHTS FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND OTHERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY NEWS: INSIGHTS FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ UPSTART EXPANDS FORTRESS PARTNERSHIP WITH $1.25 BILLION FORWARD-FLOW AGREEMENT

Upstart Holdings announced a forward-flow commitment from Fortress affiliates to buy up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated on its platform over 15 months, with the disclosure on 5th May 2026.The deal expands a partnership that began in 2025 and reflects Upstart’s effort to broaden its network of capital partners. Fortress and its affiliates join more than 100 banks and credit unions using Upstart’s AI-driven models and cloud applications to originate loans.Upstart’s platform covers a broad suite of products, including personal loans, automotive finance and refinance loans, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product.Executives described the arrangement as a win for both sides, noting that a larger base of capital partners helps reduce borrowing costs and complexity for borrowers, while Fortress seeks differentiated, high-quality consumer credit opportunities for its investors. The collaboration signals a broader industry shift toward technology-driven credit solutions and positions both companies to pursue opportunities in the evolving lending landscape.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH USD 180 MILLION PREFERRED-NOTE FINANCING

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is designed to support the construction of the land-based facility and to help meet growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores Fortress Investment Group’s longstanding presence in Japan and its willingness to provide both capital and strategic guidance to Pure Salmon Japan. The closing signals strong institutional backing for the project and aligns with Fortress’s broader focus on sustainable infrastructure.The project targets a production capacity of 10,000 tonnes and aims to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will use advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to minimize imports and bolster food security.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress officials also noted the value of the partnership; Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, called the investment a meaningful step in strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure. As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan intends to pursue additional long-term equity partners to enhance operations.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST DEAL, AGEWELL TO OPERATE AS PART OF GROWTH IN SENIOR LIVING PORTFOLIO

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida. The transaction, funded by Fortress affiliates, was completed on 12th March 2026, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care.Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, serving an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast.Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, said: \"We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families.\"The acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE: KKR) PARTNERS WITH XPV WATER PARTNERS TO TRANSFER AXIUS WATER TO CRH

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and XPV Water Partners announce the divestment of Axius Water to CRH, the global building materials group. The deal was confirmed on 6th May 2026 at 18:26:48. The transaction aims to maximize value for stakeholders as CRH expands its water management capabilitiesThe sale is expected to strengthen CRH's ability to offer innovative water solutions, a growing area in today’s environmental landscape. KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and XPV Water Partners, known for their water investments, have positioned Axius Water for a smooth transition under CRH's stewardshipAxius Water's technologies and solutions are recognized for advancing performance in the water sector, aligning with CRH's sustainability objectives. The deal underscores KKR's strategy to focus on high-potential sectors while XPV Water Partners realizes value from their investment. Financial details of the transaction have not been disclosedThe arrangement reflects a broader trend in private equity toward streamlining operations and focusing on core competencies, enabling CRH to leverage Axius Water's strengths in the market

+ VERTIS INFRASTRUCTURE TRUST (NYSE:VIT) TARGETS UP TO ₹3,000 CRORE VIA IPO AND SECONDARY SALE

Vertis Infrastructure Trust (NYSE:VIT) plans to file a draft red herring prospectus by end May 2026 to raise up to ₹3,000 crore through a combination of primary and secondary share sales. The InvIT portfolio centers on toll and annuity road projects across India, built largely through acquisitions by KKR's Asian infrastructure fund which launched the platform in 2021 and expanded in 2024-25 with the acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The trust oversees 28 road projects across nine states, valued at ₹26,500 crore.Insiders note that the final offer size could be trimmed as the trust has limited requirements for new primary capital. The pricing and scale will reflect this cautious assessment within a competitive InvIT landscape. Investors have shown continued interest in InvITs as a way to access long-duration, infrastructure-backed yields.Recent sector activity underscores the momentum in InvIT fundraising, with NHAI raising over ₹9,000 crore through two InvIT offerings in March and Citius Transnet Investment Trust raising ₹1,105 crore in April, reinforcing demand despite market volatility. Vertis Infrastructure Trust remains focused on executing a disciplined and scalable IPO strategy

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) SETS $10 BILLION BUDGET FOR AI INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) will invest $10 billion toward AI-focused power plants and data centers, a move that expands capital dedicated to AI infrastructure in the United States.On 3rd May 2026, the commitment sits within a broader industry effort, with Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta collectively directing more than $100 billion worldwide into similar projects.The investment is intended to secure long-term power agreements, giving KKR leverage in power generation as demand from AI data centers climbs.The plan also intersects geopolitics, as Canada and Germany consolidate activity to counterbalance U.S.–China AI influence; KKR's participation could influence this evolving landscape.Market signals connect the investment to Google's AI development trajectory, and analysts weigh the potential for Google's stock to rise toward $310 as the infrastructure builds out.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) AND SEI EXTEND COLLABORATION TO DELIVER INSTITUTIONAL-STYLE PRIVATE MARKET SOLUTIONS FOR ADVISORS

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) and SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) broaden their partnership to develop private market investment offerings for wealth and retirement clients, reflecting growing demand for alternative assets across advisory channels. On the 9th of May, 2026, the firms said their enhanced collaboration would widen access to institutional-style private market strategies, including model portfolios and defined contribution retirement products. The collaboration builds on a multi-year relationship focused on fund administration and technology services, combining Carlyle’s private markets platform with SEI’s research, implementation, and client servicing. The integration aims to give advisors and investors a more straightforward path to private market exposure, with tailored solutions designed to navigate this complex asset class. Michael Lane, SEI’s head of asset management, said, "One of the most common questions we hear from clients is how to allocate to private markets. Our objective with Carlyle is to help simplify that decision by providing more streamlined access to a broader range of strategies." Jeff Nedelman, Carlyle’s co-president and global head of client business, remarked that the partnership responds to rising demand from individual investors and retirement savers for private market investments. As of March 31, 2026, SEI reported about $1.9 trillion in assets under management, administration, or advisement, and Carlyle reported $477 billion under management as of December 31, 2025.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) UNITES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO EXPAND DIGITAL HEALTHCARE SERVICES PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is integrating Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a unified platform designed to boost digital capabilities across healthcare services. The move addresses ongoing structural changes in the U.S. healthcare system, including administrative complexities, workforce challenges, and evolving care delivery models. The combined platform will support physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers requiring tailored operational assistance, leveraging Knack's globally distributed operational framework and Equalize's tools for predictive analytics and optimization. The integration aims to advance revenue cycle management by enhancing billing, claims processing, and payer coordination, potentially improving efficiency and regulatory compliance. The initiative aligns with market trends as providers seek technology-driven solutions to navigate operational complexities, and Carlyle positions itself to participate in a sector undergoing substantial transformation.

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) EXPANDS HEALTHCARE REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT CAPABILITIES WITH KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM DEAL

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, expanding its healthcare portfolio. The terms of the deal are not disclosed in a release dated May 4, 2026. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the new RCM platform, aligning with Carlyle's plan to fuse Knack's analytics with Rao's global operations to bolster advisory servicesKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM count physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals among their clients. The collaboration targets rural cost reports and complex anesthesia cases, as noted by Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle frames the move as a platform for broader participation in the RCM market and signals intent to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementCarlyle sets its course to strengthen capabilities in healthcare management and revenue cycle technology, with the expectation of delivering innovative solutions that support providers in navigating a changing healthcare landscape

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL PROVIDES $300 MILLION FINANCING TO ESTABLISHMENT LABS

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan from Oaktree Capital Management. The facility, finalized on 3rd May 2026, refinances existing debt through 2031 and reserves US$35 million for growth initiatives.The loan carries a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure with flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets, enhancing funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues its operational and expansion plans.The investment thesis centers on the Motiva implant platform and minimally invasive procedures expected to drive broad adoption.Management guidance for 2026 projects revenue of US$264-266 million, implying roughly 25-26% growth from the prior year. The financing should ease refinancing pressure and provide liquidity to address losses and cash burn without an immediate equity raise.While the capital raise improves flexibility, there are risks linked to ongoing cash burn and negative free cash flow. Some analysts call for around 30% revenue growth with earnings near US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could reinforce or temper optimism about profitability. The agreement marks a strategic shift in its financing approach.

+ BIOXCEL AND OAKTREE EXTEND REGISTRATION RIGHTS, ISSUE PRIVATE WARRANTS

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed in an 8-K filing that it has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and a group of institutional holders. The move aligns with the company's ongoing efforts to optimize its capital structure and provides a framework for potential future equity transactions under the arrangement disclosed on 18 April 2026. Key elements of the deal include amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was signed on 27 March 2026, setting the stage for these warrants. On 15 April 2026, the company issued the unregistered warrants in a private placement, a mechanism that avoided immediate Securities Act registration as part of the financing strategy. BioXcel said it would file registration statements with the SEC to enable possible resale of the warrants' underlying shares, a move designed to improve liquidity and maintain investor confidence. Lenders have indicated they intend to hold the securities for investment purposes rather than seek rapid resale, which could moderate near-term volatility in the stock price. Market participants should note the potential dilution risk if warrants are exercised, and the registration process could increase the total share count. The financing approach signals the company's liquidity needs and strategic objectives, rather than an implicit endorsement of aggressive share issuance. BioXcel also clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting and regulatory requirements.

+ OAKTREE PARTNERS WITH NORTH MILL ON $355.8 MILLION EQUIPMENT FINANCE SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management, in partnership with North Mill Equipment Finance, has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization called OAKEF 2026-1. The closing occurred on 8th April 2026 and signals strong investor appetite for quality equipment finance assets.The collateral consists of equipment finance assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal completed in November 2025.The offering was rated from AAA to single A and attracted peak subscriptions of approximately 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack. The deal delivered a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%.Truist Securities served as the lead structuring agent, with Deutsche Bank acting as co-manager.Brendan Beer, portfolio manager for Oaktree's asset-backed finance and structured credit platform, said the market response reflected investor confidence in Oaktree's equipment strategy and highlighted the North Mill collaboration as a key driver.David Lee, chairman and CEO of North Mill Equipment Finance, stressed that the partnership demonstrates asset quality and the servicing platform's strength, noting that NMEF will continue as primary servicer for the assets.The securitization reinforces Oaktree's position in asset-backed finance and shows its ability to attract significant investor interest and structure deals that meet partner financing objectives.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) ACQUIRES MAJORITY STAKE IN PROSOL GROUP TO GROW FRANCE FRESH FOOD NETWORK

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Acquires Majority Stake In Prosol Group To Grow France Fresh Food NetworkApollo closes the purchase of a majority stake in Prosol Group through its managed funds, with Ardian selling its stake. The deal was finalized on May 7, 2026. Prosol Group operates nearly 450 stores across France under Grand Frais and fresh brands and runs a vertically integrated supply chain centered on quality and freshness. Jean-Paul Mochet will remain Chief Executive Officer, leading the company as it integrates into Apollo’s portfolio.The investment aligns with Apollo’s strategy to broaden its footprint in consumer-focused platforms. Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) reports approximately $1.03 trillion in assets under management, underscoring its scale to support Prosol’s expansion plans. UBS AG provided financial advisory services, with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard also advising.The deal terms were not disclosed, and potential risks include supply chain volatility and changing consumer tastes. Apollo will monitor integration milestones and ensure alignment with its broader investment objectives while pursuing growth in France’s fresh food sector.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS RECORD AUM AT MORE THAN $1 TRILLION AS IT PILOTS DAILY PRIVATE ASSET PRICING

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) reported that total assets under management surpassed $1 trillion for the first time as it advances a daily pricing pilot for its private assets. On the 6th of May 2026, during the first-quarter results call, Marc Rowan, chairman and chief executive, described activity as reflecting solid demand and noted record fee-related earnings. Total AUM reached $1.03 trillion as of March 31, 2026, up 31 percent from a year earlier. The company posted first-quarter inflows of $115 billion and inflows of $300 billion in the trailing twelve months. The asset management business brought in $95 billion of inflows, supported by Athora's acquisition of the Pension Insurance Corporation in the UK, along with institutional and global wealth inflows into credit-focused strategies. Retirement services added $20 billion in inflows. The Financial Stability Board released a report highlighting vulnerabilities in private credit on the same day, and Apollo defended its approach, citing prudent underwriting. The group also described its ongoing daily valuation pilot, which produced estimated daily values for investment-grade fixed income products last year and will provide daily pricing for corporate investment-grade assets by 6:30 PM ET and for direct lending and asset-backed finance by 9:30 PM ET. Looking ahead, Apollo sees momentum into 2026 with targets for 20 percent growth in fee-related earnings and 10 percent growth in SRE, supported by a strong origination pipeline and disciplined capital management, as institutional demand for private credit remains elevated.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) PARTNERS WITH ANTHROPIC, BLACKSTONE AND GOLDMAN SACHS ON $1.5 BILLION AI VENTURE

On the 6th of May 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) launches a $1.5 billion enterprise AI services venture with Anthropic, Blackstone and Goldman Sachs to deliver AI tools for mid-market businesses and private equity portfolio companies. The venture aims to bolster operational efficiency for mid-sized clients that lack resources to build AI capabilities in-house.The collaboration signals a strategic shift toward collaboration over traditional balance sheet capital as Apollo seeks to capture productivity gains and build new fee-based revenue streams within its portfolio.In parallel, Apollo is raising capital for Hybrid Value Fund III and Accord Fund VII, which have closed at approximately $6.5 billion and $1.9 billion, respectively. The firm’s profitability data show a margin of 11% versus 17.9% a year earlier, and its dividend history remains unstable. The stock trades about 25.2% below one estimate of fair value, suggesting potential upside if the AI project and funds meet expectations.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) RAISES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS FUND; FIRST CLOSE EXPECTED IN SPRING

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, reflecting ongoing investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The fund’s first close is expected to occur in spring, per remarks from Chief Executive Michael Arougheti on an earnings callArougheti described the fund as having "very strong demand" after the solid performance of prior vintages. The fundraising comes more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares Management completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalBeyond Japan, Ares has advanced other capital initiatives, including Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets that include a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, co-president of Ares, said the firm is fundraising to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, largely tied to commitments to its seventh special situations fund, SSG Fund VIIReturning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is reviewing a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account sharing a mandate similar to the SSG series, focused on non performing loans and single-asset distress in Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) REPORTS $53.0 BILLION OF U.S. DIRECT LENDING CLOSED ACROSS 348 TRANSACTIONS OVER THE 12 MONTHS THROUGH MARCH 31, 2026

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update on the 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors and highlights the breadth of the platform.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, the firm Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, supported Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's credit facility backing the North American Science Association.As of March 31, 2026, the global platform Totals more than $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) EXPANDS RETAIL PLATFORM WITH US$1.7 BILLION WHITESTONE REIT PURCHASE

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has agreed to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding its footprint in necessity-based retail centers across major U.S. metro areas. The deal signals progress in Ares' strategy to deepen exposure to essential consumer needs within real estate and related assets. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, and appointed Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong to lead the Asia Special Situations team, reflecting the firm's push to grow credit and special situations in Asia. Whitestone's inclusion strengthens Ares' position in retail real estate and aligns with a broader plan to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types, including private credit and real assets. Investors will assess how Whitestone integrates with Ares' existing operations and the progress of Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape. The deal introduces potential risks such as profit margin compression and higher leverage, while the dividend yield of 5.38% is not fully supported by earnings

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO REOPENS DOHA–BAGHDAD FREIGHT LINK WITH HIGHER CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly-hold operations to Iraq, reestablishing the Doha to Baghdad cargo link. Effective 7th May 2026, the carrier will launch a weekly Boeing 777 freighter service to Baghdad, paired with twice-weekly passenger flights on the same route from 10th May to provide additional belly-hold capacity.The combined cargo capacity is set to exceed 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad from 10 May, reflecting an integrated approach that blends dedicated freighter uplift with passenger capacity.The move comes as Qatar Airways Cargo continues scaling its network. The freighter network now reaches more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while the passenger network is expected to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June. Bookings are open via Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales channels. For shipments, contact cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa or your local representative. The resumption sits within a broader network strategy, supported by a fleet of 30 Boeing 777 freighters and more than 230 passenger aircraft, and a road feeder network as the airline continues its investments in digitalization, products, and infrastructure.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESUMES ERBIL ROUTE IN PHASE, EXPANDS IRAQI CONNECTIVITY

Qatar Airways will resume passenger flights to Erbil (EBL) on 10th May 2026, marking the carrier 's return to direct air connectivity between the Kurdistan Region and Doha as part of a phased reinstatement of its Iraq operations. The airline will restart services to three Iraqi cities — Erbil, Baghdad (BGW) and Basra (BSR) — in a staged rollout aimed at rebuilding its regional network and reconnecting key Middle Eastern destinations.Freighter operations to Baghdad will resume on 7th May, reinforcing cargo and trade links with Iraq and supporting broader network restoration. These resumptions follow earlier reinstatements to Dubai, Sharjah, Bahrain, Damascus and Kozhikode as part of a wider strategy to restore its global network.By mid-June 2026, the carrier expects to serve more than 150 destinations worldwide, with Erbil travellers gaining access to the Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, which the airline describes as a pivotal connector linking six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS RESTARTS DUBAI, BAHRAIN AND KOZHIKODE SERVICES, TARGETS 150-DESTINATION EXPANSION

Qatar Airways is resuming a broad set of routes as part of a measured rebound in regional travel, with key services in the Middle East, Asia, and Europe back on the timetable. As of 5th May 2026, the carrier confirmed the reinstatement of flights to Dubai and Sharjah, marking a milestone in stabilizing operations in the region.In addition, daily flights to Bahrain (BAH) and Kozhikode (CCJ) will resume, beginning 1st May 2026. The airline outlined a gradual plan to rebuild its global network, aiming to operate more than 150 destinations starting 16th June 2026. The extended schedule will be in effect through 15th September 2026, reflecting responsiveness to evolving travel demand.Qatar Airways emphasized passenger preparation during this transition, urging travelers to check its website or mobile app for updates and verify contact details. Those with confirmed bookings will receive direct notices if any changes occur, reflecting the carrier's customer service commitment.The restored routes include connections to several in-demand destinations, catering to both business and leisure travelers. The broader recovery trend in aviation is underscored by the carrier's proactive network expansion, and analysts will be watching Qatar Airways' performance as it navigates a changing travel landscape.

value.Title: May 04, 2026: Ontario Teachers’ Welcomes Feifei Wu to Lead Investment Technology Innovations

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJN-M7W3-S8VK-W15D-00000-00May 04, 2026: Ontario Teachers’ Welcomes Feifei Wu to Lead Investment Technology Innovations2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 04, 2026: Ontario Teachers’ Welcomes Feifei Wu to Lead Investment Technology Innovations

TORONTO, May 4th, 2026 – Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board (“Ontario Teachers’”) today announced the appointment of Feifei Wu as Senior Managing Director, Investment Technology & Applied Intelligence, effective immediately. In this newly established role, Ms. Wu will partner closely with investment leaders to ensure technology enables key business outcomes, while leading Ontario Teachers’ AI strategy to strengthen governance, accelerate adoption, and unlock new opportunities.

Ms. Wu brings over 25 years of global experience from leading investment and wealth management firms, with deep expertise in technology, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and cloud transformation. "I am pleased to welcome Feifei to Ontario Teachers’ in this important role at a pivotal time in our technology journey," said Chief Technology Officer Terry Hickey. "She brings a strong combination of technical expertise and proven leadership across global financial institutions. Her experience building high-performing technology teams and her forward-looking approach to applied intelligence will help accelerate innovation, deepen investment insights, and deliver long-term value for our members."

Before joining Ontario Teachers’, Ms. Wu was at Macquarie Group in New York, where she served as Managing Director, Global Head of Engineering of Macquarie Asset Management. Her previous roles include General Partner, Digital & Technology at Edward Jones, Global Chief Information Officer at Brown Brothers Harriman, and Managing Director at RBC Capital Markets and BNY. With such a diverse background, she is expected to drive significant advancements in Ontario Teachers’ technological capabilities.

Ms. Wu's academic credentials further bolster her qualifications; she holds an MS and PhD in Computer Science, with a specific focus on artificial intelligence and machine learning, from Rutgers University. Additionally, she earned her ME in Computer Engineering from Zhejiang University and a BE in Computer Engineering from Northeastern University in China.

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board (Ontario Teachers') is a global investor with net assets of $279.4 billion as of December 31, 2025. As a fully funded defined benefit pension plan, Ontario Teachers’ invests across a broad array of asset classes to ensure retirement security for its 346,000 working members and pensioners. For more information, visit otpp.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

SECTION 1 ONTARIO TEACHERS PENSION PLAN (OTPP) PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Ontario Teachers Pension Plan (OTPP), founded in 1990, is a pension fund manager based in Toronto, Canada. With around 1,300 employees, it manages approximately $250 billion in assets. OTPP invests in infrastructure, real estate, and private equity globally. In 2024, it expanded AI and renewable energy portfolios. Competing with CPPIB, its mission is to deliver sustainable pension returns through diversified, innovative investments.

value.Title: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - UPDATES FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-S443-S3V9-01KY-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - UPDATES FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - UPDATES FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXTENDS FORWARD-FLOW COMMITMENT WITH UPSTART AT $1.25 BILLION

Upstart Holdings said Fortress Investment Group will purchase up to $1.25 billion of consumer loans originated through its platform over 15 months, a forward-flow commitment disclosed on 5th May 2026.The deal expands a partnership that began in 2025 and comes as Upstart continues to broaden its network of capital partners, now counting more than 100 banks and credit unions using its AI-driven models and cloud applications. The Upstart platform offers personal loans, auto financing, home equity lines of credit, and a Cash Line revolving credit product.Executives framed the arrangement as a scalable opportunity for both firms: a stronger funding base for Upstart and access to differentiated consumer credit opportunities for Fortress, with Upstart’s data-driven platform enabling efficient, scalable origination and the potential for durable returns across market conditions. The collaboration signals a wider trend of technology-enhanced consumer credit solutions.

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) EXTENDS SENIOR NOTES FINANCING WITH $508 MILLION FOR CARBONCOUNT HOLDINGS 1

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI) Raise $508 Million Through a private offering of senior unsecured notes for CarbonCount Holdings 1 (CCH1) as of 11th May 2026. Proceeds will fund acquisitions or investments in new and existing sustainable infrastructure projects across the United States.CarbonCount Holdings 1 was created in 2024 as a strategic alliance between KKR and HASI to provide long-term capital solutions for renewable-energy initiatives, including behind-the-meter, grid-connected, RNG, and transportation infrastructure projects. The platform started with commitments of $1 billion from each sponsor, and December 2025 added $500 million. This issue marks CCH1's second senior-note issuance, following a $592 million issuance in June 2025, expanding total investment capacity toward $5 billion. Velasco of KKR cited strong investor reception and noted the asset base’s quality and diversity underpin the platform’s ability to deploy more than $4 billion of investment capacity. McMahon of HASI highlighted the expansion of CCH1’s investment capacity to meet growing sustainable-infrastructure activity. Morgan Stanley and HASI Securities served as Joint Lead Placement Agents.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) DIVESTS DANISH RESIDENTIAL ASSETS AS PEARS GLOBAL EXPANDS NORTHERN EUROPEAN FOOTPRINT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) has sold its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, with the transaction announced on 11th May 2026 at 21:07:57.692343.The portfolio consists of several residential units located in key urban areas, and Pears Global Real Estate intends to expand its Northern European footprint.KKR frames the sale as part of its asset-management optimization, reallocating capital toward higher-return opportunities, including private credit.Pears Global Real Estate will apply its regional expertise to enhance the properties and support its European expansion amid rising housing demand.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) TRANSFERS DANISH RESIDENTIAL PORTFOLIO TO PEARS GLOBAL REAL ESTATE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) transfers its Danish residential portfolio to Pears Global Real Estate, a move disclosed on May 11, 2026 at 21:07:57.692343. The sale aligns with KKR's plan to optimize holdings and concentrate on core investments within the real estate sector. The transaction underscores KKR's disciplined asset-management approach amid global economic uncertainty and reinforces its emphasis on sustainability as reflected in its MSCI ESG score. Stakeholders will monitor potential effects on future strategy and overall portfolio performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) AND SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) EXPAND PARTNERSHIP TO SCALE PRIVATE MARKET OFFERINGS FOR RETIREMENT PLANS

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ: CG) and SEI (NASDAQ: SEIC) expanded their partnership to provide private market investment solutions for wealth and retirement investors, as demand for alternatives grows across advisory and retirement channels. On the 9th of May, 2026, the collaboration aims to broaden access to institutional-style private market strategies, including model portfolios and defined contribution offerings. The initiative builds on a long-standing relationship centered on fund administration and technology services, pairing Carlyle’s private markets platform with SEI’s research, implementation, and client servicing to offer tailored solutions for navigating private market investments. Michael Lane of SEI emphasized the need for simpler access to private markets, while Carlyle’s Jeff Nedelman highlighted the rising role of private markets in wealth and retirement planning. As of March 31, 2026, SEI reported roughly $1.9 trillion in assets under management, administration, or advisement, and Carlyle reported $477 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025. The partners expect the expanded collaboration to enhance the delivery of private market strategies to a broader investor base.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ADVANCES REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT THROUGH INTEGRATED HEALTHCARE PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is combining Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a single platform focused on advanced digital capabilities in healthcare services. The integrated platform targets physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers, aiming to address administrative complexity and evolving care models. Knack supplies a globally distributed operational framework while Equalize adds predictive analytics and process optimization tools to improve revenue cycle management, billing, and payer coordination. The move reflects a broader trend toward technology-driven healthcare services as automation and data-driven systems become central to clinical administration.

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) ACQUIRES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO BUILD INTEGRATED RCM PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, broadening its healthcare portfolio. The May 4, 2026 release notes the transaction includes a majority stake in both firms, with terms undisclosed. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the platform to support ongoing developmentKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM serve physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals, and the partnership aims to address rural cost reports and intricate anesthesia cases, according to Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle views this as a step to deepen its presence in the RCM sector and to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementThe deal underscores Carlyle's focus on technology-enabled revenue cycle management and the potential to deliver enhanced, analytics-driven solutions to providers

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL EXPANDS SUPPORT FOR INTER MILAN WITH €5 MILLION FUNDING

Oaktree Capital has injected €5 million into Inter Milan, lifting total cash infusion to €52 million, according to a semiannual statement released on 11th May 2026.Oaktree took control of Inter nearly a year ago, acquiring the club from Suning after the Chinese group defaulted on a loan. The new owners emphasize both stability and growth, and they have kept CEO Giuseppe Marotta in place.Inter has preserved most of the squad that won last season’s Serie A title but is looking to rejuvenate the team by adding younger players with resale value.The group is also pursuing a new stadium to replace San Siro, a move expected to lift revenue. The €5m funding sits within a broader plan, with a similar tranche provided in December.

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS INKS $300 MILLION TERM LOAN WITH OAKTREE TO BACK MOTIVA PLATFORM EXPANSION

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility from Oaktree Capital Management, completed on 3rd May 2026, with refinancing through 2031 and US$35 million reserved for growth.The loan offers fixed-rate, no-amortization terms and a first-priority lien on assets, designed to boost funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues expansion.Analysts view Motiva as a driver of global adoption for its implants and minimally invasive procedures. Management projects 2026 revenue of US$264-266 million, up about 25-26% year over year. The facility provides liquidity to address losses and cash burn without forcing an immediate equity raise, though ongoing cash burn remains a risk.Some watchers project roughly 30% revenue growth with earnings around US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could underpin optimism about growth while highlighting the path to profitability.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) WEIGHS ASSET SALE AS MIDCAP FINANCIAL INVESTMENT FACES RISING DEFAULTS

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) is weighing a sale of its publicly traded private credit fund MidCap Financial Investment Corp (NYSE:MFIC) in a roughly $3 billion transaction announced on 11th May 2026. The negotiations contemplate a business development company as a potential buyer and could include shares as part of the consideration.The planned sale comes as MidCap Financial Investment contends with rising credit stress, reporting a 5.3% default rate in Q1 2026 after 3.9% at year-end 2025 and a quarterly loss of $61 million. Apollo Global Management, with a market capitalization near $76.79 billion and assets under management of $938.4 billion (including $709.1 billion in fee-earning assets), continues to be a central player in the alternative investment landscape. Its stock trades at 84.84x earnings, near its five-year peak.GF Score stands at 80, with profitability at 8/10 and financial strength at 6/10; Altman Z-score remains a concern at 0.04. Insider activity shows five insider sells totaling $127.57 million over the last 12 months and one insider buy, highlighting potential near-term volatility as the deal evolves.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) PARTNERS WITH PROSOL GROUP TO EXPAND FRANCE GROCERY NETWORK

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Partners With Prosol Group To Expand France Grocery NetworkThe acquisition closes a majority stake in Prosol Group through Apollo-managed funds, with Ardian serving as the seller. The transaction is dated May 7, 2026. Prosol Group operates about 450 stores across France under Grand Frais and fresh banners and maintains a vertically integrated approach to sourcing and distribution. Jean-Paul Mochet will continue as CEO, guiding the business under Apollo’s stewardship.This partnership taps into Prosol’s established network and customer base to extend Apollo’s presence in the French fresh food market. Apollo manages roughly $1.03 trillion in assets, and the deal was advised by UBS AG with Royal Bank of Canada and Lazard participating in the advisory process.Terms of the transaction were not disclosed. Investors should consider risks such as supply chain disruption and shifting consumer preferences as the integration progresses, even as the collaboration aims to enhance long-term returns.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) GUIDES ON PRIVATE-ASSET PRICING PILOT AFTER CROSSING $1 TRILLION AUM

Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) posted that total assets under management surpassed $1 trillion for the first time and outlined a daily pricing pilot for private assets. On the 6th of May 2026, during the first-quarter results call, Marc Rowan highlighted the impact of strong performance and cited record fee-related earnings contributing to the growth. AUM stood at $1.03 trillion as of March 31, 2026, up 31 percent year over year. First-quarter inflows were $115 billion, with $300 billion in inflows over the trailing twelve months. Asset management inflows totaled $95 billion, aided by Athora's UK Pension Insurance Corporation acquisition, while retirement services added $20 billion in inflows. Apollo noted a broader context with the Financial Stability Board report on private credit vulnerabilities issued the same day and reiterated its emphasis on prudent underwriting. The daily valuation pilot, already in place for investment-grade fixed income products, is expanding to daily pricing for direct lending and asset-backed finance by 9:30 PM ET following a 6:30 PM ET window for investment-grade assets. The company signaled continued progress toward its 2026 goals, including 20 percent growth in fee-related earnings and 10 percent growth in SRE, supported by a healthy origination pipeline and a steady approach to capital management.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT CORP. (NYSE:ARES) REAFFIRMS $125 BILLION WEALTH TARGET AMID MARKET UNCERTAINTY

On 9th May 2026, Ares Management Corp. (NYSE:ARES) reiterates its target to raise $125 billion from wealth clients by 2028, a goal increased by $25 billion last September. Redemption activity comes primarily from smaller family offices and certain overseas institutions, while 95% of the BDC investor base did not redeem.The technology sector pullback has raised concerns among retail investors about private credit exposure. In response, Ares Management and other alternative credit managers have taken measures to limit redemptions in non-traded BDCs, underscoring a focus on sustainable, long-term growth.The firm relies on diversified investment strategies and strong client relationships to pursue its wealth targets. It continues to engage with investors to demonstrate the resilience of its investments, especially in alternative assets, and sees a robust underlying market for alternatives despite volatility in some areas.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) SECURES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS DEVELOPMENT FUND WITH BROAD INVESTMENT MOMENTUM

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, signaling continued investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The first close is expected in spring, as noted during an earnings call by chief executive Michael AroughetiThe fund follows more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalThe firm’s broader capital activity includes Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets including a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, Ares' co-president, said the fundraising aims to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares also raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, supported by commitments to SSG Fund VIINotable returning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is considering a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account with a mandate aligned to the SSG series, targeting non performing loans and single-asset distress across Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) FACILITATES GROWTH THROUGH DIRECT LENDING WITH ENCORE FIRE PROTECTION LOENBRO AND ARCHIMED

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update dated 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors. The firm also highlights notable Q1 activity.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, Ares Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions, underscoring the breadth of its direct lending platform. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, led Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's credit facility backing the North American Science Association.As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management L.P.'s global platform Totals over $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO EXPANDS IRAQ CORRIDOR WITH FREIGHTER AND BELLY-HOLD CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freight services to Iraq, restoring the Doha to Baghdad link with both freighter and belly-hold capacity. Effective 7th May 2026, a weekly Boeing 777 freighter will serve Baghdad, complemented by twice-weekly passenger flights on the route from 10th May to provide additional belly-hold capacity.Together, these services will deliver a combined cargo capacity of more than 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad from 10th May.Qatar Airways Cargo notes the broader expansion of its network: the freighter network has grown to more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while the passenger network is scheduled to reach over 150 destinations from 16th June as operations scale up. Bookings are available through the Digital Lounge and local sales teams; inquiries can be directed to cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa

+ QATAR AIRWAYS BRINGS BACK ERBIL SERVICE TO BOOST REGIONAL CONNECTIVITY

Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of passenger flights to Erbil on 10th May 2026, as part of its broader return to Iraq. The carrier will restart flights to Erbil, Baghdad and Basra in a phased rollout to rebuild the regional network.Freighter operations to Baghdad will resume on 7th May, complementing passenger services and boosting cargo ties. By continuing to restore routes, the airline is expanding its footprint across the Middle East as conditions allow.By mid-June 2026, the airline aims to serve more than 150 destinations, with Erbil travellers able to access the Doha hub via Hamad International Airport and reach six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS NETWORK AMID POST-CRISIS REBOUND, TARGETS OVER 150 DESTINATIONS

Qatar Airways is advancing a targeted restart of its network, resuming routes across the Middle East, Asia, and Europe as it rebuilds after the West Asia crisis. The carrier confirmed the reintroduction of services to Dubai and Sharjah as of 5th May 2026, a step toward restoring regional air traffic.The company will also bring back daily services to Bahrain (BAH) and Kozhikode (CCJ) from 1st May 2026, while a broader rollout to more than 150 destinations is planned to begin on 16th June 2026 and run through 15th September 2026. This staged approach aligns with shifting travel demand.Travelers are advised to check Qatar Airways ' website or app for updates and to ensure their contact details are current, with affected passengers receiving direct notifications about any changes. The resumption signals a broader recovery trend in air travel and a renewed emphasis on connectivity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJP-SPM3-RXSM-W0DC-00000-00INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:37ZLexisNexisCLASS ACTIONLAW & LEGAL ISSUESINVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

NEW YORK, May 12, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Pomerantz LLP is investigating claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation (“Primoris” or the “Company”) (NYSE: PRIM).  Such investors are advised to contact Danielle Peyton at newaction@pomlaw.com  or 646-581-9980, ext. 7980.

The investigation concerns whether Primoris and certain of its officers and/or directors have engaged in securities fraud or other unlawful business practices. 

[Click here for information about joining the class action]

On May 5, 2026, Primoris issued a press release reporting its financial results for the first quarter of 2026.  Primoris reported results below analyst expectations and slashed full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million.  Primoris attributed the reduction to lower renewable energy activity, delayed project starts, and increased costs on renewable energy projects. 

On this news, Primoris’s stock price fell $101.69 per share, or 50.11%, to close at $101.23 per share on May 6, 2026.

Pomerantz LLP, with offices in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, London, Paris, and Tel Aviv, is acknowledged as one of the premier firms in the areas of corporate, securities, and antitrust class litigation. Founded by the late Abraham L. Pomerantz, known as the dean of the class action bar, Pomerantz pioneered the field of securities class actions. Today, more than 85 years later, Pomerantz continues in the tradition he established, fighting for the rights of the victims of securities fraud, breaches of fiduciary duty, and corporate misconduct. The Firm has recovered numerous multimillion-dollar damages awards on behalf of class members. See www.pomlaw.com.

Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:Danielle PeytonPomerantz LLPdpeyton@pomlaw.com 646-581-9980 ext. 7980
